headlines.headline: Mali Defence Minister Sadio Camara killed in coordinated attacks
headlines.summary: Mali's Defence Minister Sadio Camara was killed in a coordinated attack on the garrison town of Kati, amid simultaneous attacks across the West African country, marking a major escalation in regional instability.

headlines.headline: China backs Myanmar's push to ease diplomatic isolation, fight telecoms scams
headlines.summary: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi concluded a five-day Southeast Asia tour by backing Myanmar's efforts to reduce its diplomatic isolation and crack down on telecoms fraud operations.

headlines.headline: Two US citizens among 19 killed in Philippines clashes with communist rebels
headlines.summary: Two American citizens were among 19 suspected New People's Army guerrillas killed during clashes with Philippine military forces on April 19 in Negros Occidental province.

headlines.headline: EU commissioners arrive in Kyiv to secure Chornobyl site
headlines.summary: EU Economy Commissioner Valdis Dombrovskis and Energy Commissioner Dan Jørgensen visited Kyiv on April 26 to discuss making the Chornobyl nuclear site safe again, marking continued European support for Ukraine's nuclear safety.

headlines.headline: Syria holds first public trial of Assad-era officials in Damascus
headlines.summary: Syria opened its first public trial of officials linked to former President Bashar Assad's rule in Damascus on Sunday, marking a significant step in post-civil war accountability efforts.

headlines.headline: Mexico: US agents killed in crash not authorized for local operations
headlines.summary: Two US federal agents, reportedly CIA, died in a car crash after a Mexican-led operation to destroy a drug lab. Mexican authorities say they were not permitted to operate in the country.

headlines.headline: Trump cancels US envoys trip to Pakistan for Iran war talks
headlines.summary: US President cancelled the delegation trip led by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner after Iran said there were no plans for direct meeting. Peace prospects dim further.

headlines.headline: Palestinians hold local elections in West Bank and Gaza
headlines.summary: Local elections conducted in the occupied West Bank and one Gazan city, though Hamas and other groups did not participate.

headlines.headline: Afghanistan urges Afghans stuck in Qatar to return home
headlines.summary: Taliban government calls on Afghans who helped US forces and are now stranded in Qatar to return to Afghanistan.

headlines.headline: Venezuela prisoner release scheme nearing end, rights groups warn
headlines.summary: More than 500 political prisoners remain jailed as amnesty program winds down amid criticism from human rights organizations.

headlines.headline: US-Iran Peace Hopes Fade as Trump Cancels Negotiator Trip
headlines.summary: President Trump canceled a planned trip by envoys Kushner and Witkoff to Pakistan for Iran ceasefire talks, citing inadequate Iranian proposals. Both sides show little willingness to soften terms as the war enters a stalemate phase.

headlines.headline: Germany Suspects Russia Behind Signal Phishing Campaign Targeting Politicians
headlines.summary: The German government believes Russia orchestrated phishing attacks via the Signal messaging app targeting high-profile politicians, diplomats, military officers, and journalists. At least 300 accounts were compromised in the cyber campaign.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian Drone Strikes Apartment Building in Yekaterinburg
headlines.summary: A Ukrainian drone hit a multi-story apartment building in Russia's fourth-largest city of Yekaterinburg, marking the first attack on the city since the conflict began. Regional authorities reported damage to residential infrastructure.

headlines.headline: Colombia Bus Bombing Kills 13 Ahead of Presidential Election
headlines.summary: At least 13 people were killed and 17 wounded in an explosives attack on a bus in western Colombia. Authorities blamed dissidents of the FARC guerrilla group for the violence amid escalating pre-election tensions.

headlines.headline: Finland Joins Nordic Boycott Over Russian Athlete Decision
headlines.summary: Finland announced it will not host international championships while World Aquatics allows Russian and Belarusian athletes to compete, joining Norway and Poland in protest against the governing body's policy.

headlines.headline: Hezbollah accused of sabotaging Israel-Lebanon peace efforts amid ceasefire extension
headlines.summary: Israeli PM Netanyahu said Hezbollah is trying to derail a potential historic peace agreement with Lebanon, after Israeli forces struck Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon in response to ceasefire violations.

headlines.headline: Ukraine unveils new AI-powered strike drones Anubis and Seth-X for deep missions
headlines.summary: Ukrainian firm Airlogix revealed two new drones: Anubis for deep-strike missions with stealth characteristics, and Seth-X designed for swarm attacks, expanding Ukraine's AI strike capabilities in contested environments.

headlines.headline: Chinese analysts study how Iran detected and damaged US F-35 stealth jet
headlines.summary: South China Morning Post reports that Chinese military analysts are mapping out how Iran managed to detect and damage a US F-35 stealth fighter during combat operations, suggesting electro-optical/infrared sensor systems may have been used.

headlines.headline: Dutch intelligence: Russia could fight NATO within a year after Ukraine war ends
headlines.summary: The Dutch MIVD warns Russia could be ready for a regional conflict with NATO within a year after hostilities in Ukraine cease, aiming to create political division in the alliance through limited territorial gains under nuclear threats.

headlines.headline: Thailand and Cambodia sign ceasefire with Trump presiding at ASEAN summit
headlines.summary: The two nations signed an expanded ceasefire deal ending their five-day war, with Trump taking credit for pushing negotiations. Thailand will release prisoners and Cambodia will withdraw heavy artillery as first steps.

headlines.headline: Russian Defense Minister Visits North Korea as Military Ties Deepen
headlines.summary: Russian Defense Minister Andrei Belousov arrived in North Korea for talks with top leadership, the second senior Russian official to visit Pyongyang this week. The visit follows State Duma Chairman Vyacheslav Volodin meeting with Kim Jong Un to commemorate North Korean troop deployment in Ukraine.

headlines.headline: CNN, NBC Report on White House Correspondents Dinner Shooting Incident
headlines.summary: President Trump was evacuated from the White House Correspondents Dinner after shots were fired at the Washington Hilton. A suspect was taken into custody after exchanging gunfire with security. Trump and the First Lady were unharmed in the incident.

headlines.headline: TotalEnergies Warns of Global Energy Shortages as Hormuz Crisis Continues
headlines.summary: The head of TotalEnergies warned of global energy shortages if the Iran war continues for months, as French President Macron reaffirmed efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. LNG markets are bracing for sustained price elevation throughout 2026.

headlines.headline: Egypt Economic Outlook Trimmed as Iran War Drives Up Energy Prices
headlines.summary: Analysts reduced their forecasts for Egyptian economic growth as the Iran war drives up energy prices and puts pressure on inflation, according to a Reuters poll.

headlines.headline: France and Greece Strengthen Defense Alliance Amid NATO Uncertainty
headlines.summary: French President Macron and Greek PM Mitsotakis signed expanded bilateral defense agreements in Athens, affirming that EU defense capabilities complement rather than replace NATO. The Greece-France Alliance 2.0 comes as concerns grow over US commitment to the alliance.

headlines.headline: Trump Rushed Off Stage After Armed Man Opens Fire at White House Correspondents Dinner
headlines.summary: An armed gunman stormed the Washington Hilton lobby as President Trump was beginning his speech at the annual White House Correspondents Association Dinner. Secret Service agents shot the suspect, who was armed with multiple guns and knives. One officer was hit but protected by a bulletproof vest.

headlines.headline: Major Coordinated Attack Hits Multiple Cities Across Mali
headlines.summary: Jihadist group JNIM and separatist Azawad Liberation Front launched simultaneous attacks on Bamako, Kati, Gao, Kidal, and other cities in what analysts call the largest coordinated jihadist attack in Mali in years. Gunmen attacked the airport area and a military helicopter was reportedly shot down near Gao.

headlines.headline: Putin Approval Ratings Drop as Russian Influencers Voice Growing Discontent
headlines.summary: A viral 19-minute video by a Russian blogger with 31 million views has sparked a wave of public criticism over internet restrictions and the strained wartime economy. Putin's approval fell to 65.6% in state polls, the lowest since before the Ukraine war began, amid growing frustration with the conflict's fifth year.

headlines.headline: Seven Killed as Russia Launches Massive Overnight Strike on Ukraine
headlines.summary: Russian missiles and drones killed at least seven people across Ukraine overnight, including five in Dnipro where an apartment building was hit. Ukraine reported repelling most of over 600 drones in one of the largest attacks in days. Romania investigated an object falling on its territory near the Ukrainian border.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu Orders Vigorous Attack on Hezbollah After Ceasefire Extension
headlines.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu directed the military to "vigorously attack" Hezbollah targets in Lebanon, just two days after a ceasefire was extended. At least six people were killed in Israeli strikes on Saturday, with cross-border exchanges straining the truce agreement.

headlines.headline: Colombia Highway Bombing Kills 14 Ahead of Presidential Election
headlines.summary: A bomb attack on the Pan-American Highway in Cauca department killed 14 and injured 38, including five minors. Authorities blamed FARC dissidents led by Ivan Mordisco, the country's most-wanted criminal, as political violence escalates before the May 31 vote.

headlines.headline: Japanese Destroyer Transits Taiwan Strait in First Major Passage Under Takaichi
headlines.summary: The JS Ikazuchi, a Murasame-class destroyer, conducted a deliberate 14-hour transit through the Taiwan Strait, sparking outrage in Beijing. The move signals a significant shift in Japan's regional security stance under Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian Drones Strike Russian Refinery and Chemical Plant in Deep-Raids
headlines.summary: Ukrainian long-range drones hit the Slavneft-YANOS refinery in Yaroslavl and a chemical complex in Volgograd, continuing Kyiv's campaign to degrade Russian industrial infrastructure. Five workers were hospitalized with chemical burns at the Apatit nitrogen complex.

headlines.headline: Macron Warns Europe Must Wake Up as US, Russia, and China Align Against It
headlines.summary: Speaking in Athens, the French president called for European strategic autonomy, saying Washington, Moscow, and Beijing are now categorically opposed to European interests. The remarks come as Germany unveils its first comprehensive defense strategy since WWII.

headlines.headline: Spain Rejects NATO Suspension Threat, Says It Is Reliable Member
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez defended Spain's NATO credentials after reports the Pentagon considered suspending allies refusing to support Iran operations. Italy and France also refused overflight rights for US military aircraft deployed for the Middle East conflict.

headlines.headline: Russian Duma Speaker Visits Pyongyang for North Korea War Memorial Opening
headlines.summary: Vyacheslav Volodin arrived in North Korea to attend the opening of a memorial honoring North Korean soldiers killed fighting for Russia in Ukraine. The visit underscores deepening military ties between Moscow and Pyongyang following North Korea's troop deployment.

headlines.headline: Macron Affirms EU Defense Clause Stronger Than NATO's Article 5
headlines.summary: The French president said during a visit to Greece that the EU's mutual defense clause (Article 42.7) is 'stronger' than NATO's collective defense provision, citing military aid sent to Cyprus after a drone attack. The statement comes amid uncertainty over US commitment to NATO.

headlines.headline: Ukrainians Mark Chernobyl Anniversary Amid War Fears
headlines.summary: Residents of Slavutych gathered for a midnight vigil commemorating the 40th anniversary of the Chernobyl nuclear disaster, defying wartime curfews. The anniversary comes as Ukraine worries Russia's war could trigger another nuclear catastrophe.

headlines.headline: Russian Strikes on Dnipro Kill Five, Wound Dozens
headlines.summary: Russian drone and missile strikes on the Ukrainian city of Dnipro killed at least five people and wounded 46, authorities said. The attacks caused fires across the city and partially destroyed several apartment buildings.

headlines.headline: Gunman opens fire at White House Correspondents Dinner, Trump evacuated safely
headlines.summary: A shooter fired shots at the Washington Hilton during the White House Correspondents Association dinner, forcing President Trump and top officials to be evacuated. One officer was shot but saved by a bulletproof vest; the suspect is in custody.

headlines.headline: US-Iran peace talks collapse as Trump cancels envoy visit to Pakistan
headlines.summary: Diplomatic efforts stalled after Trump called off Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner trip to Islamabad, saying Iran offered a lot but not enough. Both sides show little willingness to soften terms as the war enters its third month.

headlines.headline: Tunisia suspends one of Africas oldest human rights groups in widening crackdown
headlines.summary: Authorities ordered a one-month suspension of the Tunisian League for Human Rights, part of the Nobel Peace Prize-winning National Dialogue Quartet. The move signals deepening authoritarianism in the North African nation.

headlines.headline: Chinas top diplomat meets Myanmar junta leader on regional tour
headlines.summary: Chinas Foreign Minister visited Naypyidaw and met with the military-backed government leader, deepening Beijings regional ties as Myanmars civil war continues. The visit underscores Chinas growing influence in Southeast Asia.

headlines.headline: Macron says EU mutual defense clause is stronger than NATO amid US commitment doubts
headlines.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron declared the EUs Article 42.7 mutual defense clause stronger than NATO, as European leaders debate building independent defense capabilities. The statement reflects growing anxiety about Washingtons reliability.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu Orders IDF Strikes on Hezbollah After Ceasefire Violations
headlines.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu ordered the IDF to "vigorously" attack Hezbollah in southern Lebanon after the group launched projectiles into northern Israel. The ceasefire extension announced by Trump appears increasingly fragile as both sides exchange fire.

headlines.headline: Hungary's Orban Refuses Parliament Seat After Historic Election Defeat
headlines.summary: Viktor Orban, Hungary's dominant political figure for 36 years, said he will not take up his parliament seat following his party's landslide defeat. He plans to lead Fidesz through a "renewal" process, with analysts seeing his loss as evidence that right-wing populism may be peaking.

headlines.headline: Israel Kills Four in Southern Lebanon Despite Ceasefire Extension
headlines.summary: Israeli strikes killed at least four people in southern Lebanon's Nabatieh district, as Israel continues attacks despite a three-week ceasefire extension. Hezbollah lawmakers say the ceasefire is "meaningless" given Israel's ongoing hostilities.

headlines.headline: India Signs $1.2 Billion Deal for Russian Long-Range Air-to-Air Missiles
headlines.summary: India has reportedly signed a deal for 300 Russian R-37M ultra-long-range missiles designed to target support aircraft like airborne early-warning and tanker planes. The move comes as Pakistan and China continue integrating their aerial weapons systems.

headlines.headline: UN Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty Review Opens Amid Global War Tensions
headlines.summary: Signatories to the landmark NPT meet at the UN as arms control agreements between major powers have collapsed and nuclear capabilities expand. The nine nuclear-armed states now possess over 12,000 warheads, with the US and Russia holding 90%.

headlines.headline: US-Iran Ceasefire Talks Collapse as Trump Cancels Envoys Trip to Pakistan
headlines.summary: The latest ceasefire negotiations between Washington and Tehran failed after Iran's foreign minister left Islamabad and Trump told his envoys not to travel. Trump said Iran needs to call if they want to talk, while Iran questioned how it can trust the US after its port blockade.

headlines.headline: Macron Pushes to Reopen Hormuz as TotalEnergies Warns of Global Energy Shortages
headlines.summary: French President Macron reiterated efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz after TotalEnergies CEO warned of global energy shortages if the Iran war continues for months. The waterway remains nearly blocked, disrupting oil and LNG shipments worldwide.

headlines.headline: Trump Cancels Kushner and Witkoff Iran Mission After Tehran Offer Falls Short
headlines.summary: President Trump cancelled his envoys' trip to Pakistan for Iran talks, saying Tehran 'offered a lot but not enough.' Iran had said no direct talks were planned and its foreign minister left Islamabad for Oman before heading to Russia.

headlines.headline: Republicans Recalibrate Midterm Strategy Amid Trump Approval Slump and Iran War
headlines.summary: With gas prices up, Trump's approval ratings down, and the Iran war dragging on, Republicans are retooling their midterm election blueprint to focus on Trump's policies while distancing themselves from the president personally.

headlines.headline: Trump Cancels US Peace Delegation Trip to Pakistan as Iran Talks Falter
headlines.summary: President Trump canceled a planned US delegation trip to Pakistan for peace talks with Iran, citing "infighting" in Tehran and the lack of a clear negotiation plan. The move deals a blow to diplomatic efforts to end the US-Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Trump Fires Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem
headlines.summary: President Trump fired Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem following mounting criticism over her handling of immigration enforcement and disaster response. Senator Markwayne Mullin has been nominated as her replacement.

headlines.headline: World's Largest Condom Maker Karex to Raise Prices Up to 30% Due to Iran War
headlines.summary: Malaysia-based Karex, which produces over 5 billion condoms annually for brands like Durex and Trojan, announced price increases of up to 30% due to Iran war disruptions to oil-derived raw materials including ammonia and silicone lubricants.

headlines.headline: Trump Vows No Hormuz Blockade Lift Until Iran Deal Reached
headlines.summary: President Trump stated the US will not lift its naval blockade of Iranian ports until a deal is reached with Tehran. US Central Command reports 27 vessels have been turned away since the blockade began, while Iran maintains its own blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: Acid Attacks Target Indonesian Activists Amid Growing Climate of Repression
headlines.summary: Environmental and human rights activists in Indonesia face escalating attacks, including acid assaults, under President Prabowo's government which rights groups say is increasingly hostile to dissent.

headlines.headline: EU Seeks Alternative Middle East Energy Routes to Bypass Hormuz
headlines.summary: European Commission President von der Leyen announces plans to work with Gulf countries on new export infrastructure that avoids the Strait of Hormuz, as the bloc's energy bill has skyrocketed by €25 billion due to the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Colombia Highway Bomb Attack Kills 10 Ahead of Presidential Election
headlines.summary: A highway bombing in the Cauca department killed 10 people and injured at least 12, with authorities blaming FARC dissidents. The attack comes amid a wave of violence before next month's presidential election.

headlines.headline: Mali Declares Curfew After Coordinated Terrorist Attacks Hit Bamako
headlines.summary: Authorities imposed a night curfew in the Gao region after coordinated attacks by terrorist groups targeted military sites in Bamako and other areas. The military claims hundreds of attackers were neutralized.

headlines.headline: Former MIT President Warns US Is Losing Innovation Race to China
headlines.summary: L. Rafael Reif, who led MIT from 2012 to 2022, argues in Foreign Affairs that America is ceding scientific leadership to China, pointing to advances in EVs, nuclear energy, and military technology.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Commemorates 40th Anniversary of Chornobyl Disaster Under Shadow of War
headlines.summary: Ukraine marked the 40th anniversary of the Chornobyl nuclear disaster on Sunday amid ongoing fears that Russia's war could trigger another nuclear catastrophe. The Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant remains a concern.

headlines.headline: Al Qaeda-Linked Militants Launch Major Coordinated Attacks Across Mali
headlines.summary: JNIM, an al Qaeda affiliate, and Tuareg rebels claimed responsibility for coordinated attacks across Mali targeting the capital Bamako and military bases. Experts called it one of the boldest operations in years.

headlines.headline: Lebanese Civilians Question Fragile Ceasefire as Violence Simmers in South
headlines.summary: Residents in southern Lebanon express skepticism about the extended ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah, as continuing attacks and tensions threaten to reignite the conflict.

headlines.headline: Germany Readies Naval Units for Possible Hormuz Deployment
headlines.summary: Germany is sending naval units to the Mediterranean in preparation for possible deployment in an international mission to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, which remains blocked by Iran.

headlines.headline: Zelenskyy Signs Military-Industrial Agreement With Azerbaijan as Russian Attacks Kill 10
headlines.summary: Ukraine's president signed a military-industrial cooperation agreement in Baku as Russia continued missile and drone strikes on Ukrainian cities. The death toll from recent attacks rose to at least 10.

headlines.headline: US-Iran Peace Talks Stall as Trump Cancels Envoys Pakistan Trip
headlines.summary: President Trump cancelled a planned trip by envoys Witkoff and Kushner to Pakistan for Iran negotiations, saying Iran improved its offer but not enough. Tehran says it will not be coerced into a deal through pressure or threats.

headlines.headline: Trump Evacuated from White House Correspondents Dinner After Gunshots
headlines.summary: President Trump and First Lady Melania Trump were rushed out by Secret Service after a loud noise and shots fired at the Washington Hilton. A suspect is in custody and Trump later returned safely to the White House.

headlines.headline: Islamic Militants and Separatists Claim Sweeping Attacks Across Mali
headlines.summary: Gunfire was heard near Malis international airport in the capital Bamako early Saturday. Coordinated attacks by gunmen targeted military positions across the West African nation.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian drones strike Russian oil refinery in Yaroslavl, hit multiple regions including occupied Crimea
headlines.summary: A fire erupted at an oil refinery in the Russian city of Yaroslavl as Ukrainian drones attacked multiple Russian regions overnight. Strikes also targeted occupied Crimea, marking an escalation in Ukraine's long-range drone campaign against Russian energy infrastructure.

headlines.headline: South Korea and Japan face renewed nuclear deterrent debate as Iran war reshapes security calculations
headlines.summary: Seven in ten South Koreans now support acquiring nuclear weapons, while Japan's PM refuses to rule out the option. The Iran war has brought the long-simmering debate over nuclear proliferation in US allies to a boil, with IAEA sounding the alarm.

headlines.headline: Germany deploys minesweeper to Mediterranean for possible Strait of Hormuz mission
headlines.summary: Germany's defense minister announced the deployment of a minesweeper to the Mediterranean Sea in preparation for a potential mission to the Strait of Hormuz, as European nations consider naval operations to secure the vital oil transit route.

headlines.headline: Israeli strikes kill six in southern Lebanon despite ceasefire agreement
headlines.summary: Israeli airstrikes killed six people in southern Lebanon, according to the Lebanese health ministry. The strikes come amid accusations of Hezbollah ceasefire violations and rising tensions along the Israel-Lebanon border.

headlines.headline: Trump evacuated from White House Correspondents Dinner after security incident, shooter reported dead
headlines.summary: President Trump and First Lady Melania Trump were evacuated from the White House Correspondents Dinner after shots were fired outside the venue. The Secret Service neutralized the shooter, who was reported dead. Vice President Vance and several Cabinet secretaries were also evacuated.

headlines.headline: Trump Evacuated from White House Correspondents Dinner After Shooting
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump was evacuated by Secret Service after a shooter opened fire at the White House Correspondents Dinner in Washington. The shooter was killed by security forces. No injuries to Trump were reported.

headlines.headline: US-Iran Ceasefire Talks Collapse as Trump Cancels Envoy Trip to Pakistan
headlines.summary: The latest US-Iran ceasefire talks appeared to fail before they began, with Iranian FM Araghchi leaving Pakistan and Trump saying he told envoys Witkoff and Kushner not to travel, citing too much time and work wasted on traveling.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu Orders Vigorous Attacks on Hezbollah After Ceasefire Strains
headlines.summary: Israeli PM Netanyahu ordered the military to vigorously attack Hezbollah targets in Lebanon after at least six people were killed in Israeli strikes. Cross-border exchanges have strained the extended ceasefire agreement.

headlines.headline: Colombia Highway Bombing Kills 14 as President Blames Rebel Dissidents
headlines.summary: A bombing on a Colombian highway killed at least 14 people. President Gustavo Petro blamed the attack on rebels linked to dissident FARC factions. The attacks come one month before the presidential election.

headlines.headline: Al Qaeda affiliate launches coordinated attacks across Mali
headlines.summary: An al Qaeda affiliate and Tuareg rebels claimed coordinated attacks on army bases across Mali in one of the boldest insurgent operations against the military-led government.

headlines.headline: Russian forces pound Ukraine with massive drone and missile barrage
headlines.summary: Russian forces attacked southeastern Ukraine including Dnipro with waves of drones and missiles, killing at least 10 people and injuring dozens in one of the largest attacks of the war.

headlines.headline: Trump cancels envoys trip for Iran talks, says we have all the cards
headlines.summary: President Trump scrapped a planned trip by Kushner and Witkoff to Pakistan for Iran negotiations, saying the talks involved too much travel and expense.

headlines.headline: Macron reaffirms efforts to reopen Strait of Hormuz as TotalEnergies warns of shortages
headlines.summary: The French president said he is focused on reopening the Strait of Hormuz after TotalEnergies CEO warned of global energy shortages if the Iran war continues for months.

headlines.headline: Germany Blames Russia for Global Cyber Campaign Targeting Signal and WhatsApp
headlines.summary: Berlin believes Russia is behind a global cyber operation targeting encrypted messaging apps Signal and WhatsApp, according to Der Spiegel, marking an escalation in Russian cyber warfare tactics.

headlines.headline: Macron: EU Joint Defense Clause Is Stronger Than NATOs Article 5
headlines.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron stated that the EU mutual defense clause (Article 42.7) is steadier than NATOs Article 5, pushing for greater European strategic autonomy amid questions about US reliability.

headlines.headline: Washingtons Resentment Toward Paper Tiger NATO Growing
headlines.summary: The Trump administration has accused NATO allies of failing to show solidarity during the Iran war, with frustration particularly strong toward Spain, France, and the United Kingdom over their perceived lack of support.

headlines.headline: Japans Accelerated Remilitarization Raises Regional Concerns
headlines.summary: Japans quickened remilitarization under the Takaichi administration, including eased restrictions on exporting lethal weapons, is posing grave threats to neighbors and requires international vigilance, analysts warn.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Exports Drone Warfare Expertise to Allies
headlines.summary: Ukraines hard-won drone warfare knowledge is being shared with allies, as three Ukrainian drone operators reveal how the country has become a global leader in unmanned aerial warfare technology.

headlines.headline: Colombia explosives attack kills at least 13
headlines.summary: An explosives attack in western Colombia killed at least 13 people and wounded 17 others, in one of the deadliest incidents amid escalating violence by FARC dissident groups.

headlines.headline: Trump cancels US envoy trip to Pakistan for Iran talks
headlines.summary: President Trump abruptly cancelled plans to send envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Pakistan for ceasefire talks with Iran, saying "too much time" had been "wasted on traveling" and that Iran had "tremendous infighting."

headlines.headline: Macron says EU defence clause stronger than NATO Article 5
headlines.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron stated that the EU mutual assistance clause was "stronger" than NATO Article 5, citing recent military aid to Cyprus as proof of Europe ability to defend itself amid concerns over US reliability.

headlines.headline: Ukraine marks Chornobyl 40th anniversary under shadow of war
headlines.summary: Ukraine commemorated the 40th anniversary of the Chornobyl nuclear disaster amid fears that Russia four-year-old war could trigger another nuclear catastrophe.

headlines.headline: Russia parliament speaker visits Pyongyang for war memorial ceremony
headlines.summary: Russian State Duma Chairman Vyacheslav Volodin arrived in North Korea to attend the opening of a memorial for North Korean troops who died fighting in Ukraine, highlighting deepening military ties between Moscow and Pyongyang.

headlines.headline: China and Mozambique to map critical minerals in conflict zone
headlines.summary: China and Mozambique agreed to survey rare earth elements, graphite, and lithium deposits in insurgency-hit Cabo Delgado province, as Beijing seeks to secure critical mineral supplies for the green energy transition.

headlines.headline: Iran Resumes Commercial Flights From Tehran Airport
headlines.summary: Iran has resumed commercial flights from Tehran Imam Khomeini International Airport for the first time since the US-Israel conflict began two months ago, with flights operating to Istanbul, Muscat, Medina, and domestic destinations including Mashhad.

headlines.headline: Macron warns US not "fully reliable ally," urges European self-reliance
headlines.summary: French President Macron said the US can no longer be seen as a fully reliable ally during a visit to Athens, calling for greater European autonomy in defense. The remarks came after a Pentagon memo revealed US plans to suspend Spain's NATO membership over its refusal to support the Iran war.

headlines.headline: Saudi Arabia looks to China and regional partners as US security guarantee wavers
headlines.summary: The Iran war has exposed vulnerabilities in US security guarantees to Gulf allies, prompting Saudi Arabia to diversify partnerships with China, Pakistan, and Turkey. Riyadh has signed defense deals with Beijing and a mutual defense pact with nuclear-armed Pakistan.

headlines.headline: Trump cancels envoys' Pakistan trip for Iran talks, saying US "holds all the cards"
headlines.summary: President Trump abruptly cancelled Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner's trip to Pakistan for Iran negotiations, telling Fox News "you're not flying 18 hours to sit and talk about nothing." Iranian FM Araghchi left Islamabad without meeting US officials.

headlines.headline: Russia-Ukraine Exchange Deadly Attacks as Zelenskyy Calls for More Talks
headlines.summary: Russian drone and missile strikes killed at least 3 and wounded 21 in Dnipro overnight. Both sides reported casualties in intensified fighting as Zelenskyy pushed for continued diplomatic engagement.

headlines.headline: China Warns US Export Bills Risk Disrupting Global Chip Supply Chains
headlines.summary: China said it is closely monitoring US legislative plans after the House Foreign Affairs Committee advanced export-control bills targeting semiconductors, warning of potential global supply chain disruptions.

headlines.headline: France and Greece Say EU Defense Buildup No Alternative to NATO
headlines.summary: President Macron stated the EU push for defensive capabilities is not meant to create a NATO alternative but to address a long-standing US request for Europe to do more, amid transatlantic tensions over the Iran war.

headlines.headline: Mali Hit by Wave of Coordinated Attacks from Armed Groups
headlines.summary: Gunfire and explosions rocked Mali capital Bamako and other cities in one of the most significant coordinated attacks in years, as jihadist insurgents and separatist rebels exploit worsening insecurity in the Sahel.

headlines.headline: Former NATO Chief Urges Britain and France to Lead New European Defense
headlines.summary: A former NATO secretary general called for Britain and France to lead a new European security arrangement, warning the continent must be prepared to act without US support as transatlantic relations strain over the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Macron Reaffirms Efforts to Reopen Strait of Hormuz as TotalEnergies Warns of Energy Shortages
headlines.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron reiterated his focus on efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a day after TotalEnergies CEO warned of global energy shortages if the Iran war continues for months.

headlines.headline: Trump Cancels Envoys Trip for Iran Talks, Says We Have All the Cards
headlines.summary: President Trump called off the trip of US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Pakistan for Iran negotiations, saying too much time has been wasted on traveling.

headlines.headline: Mexico Says US Agents Killed in Crash Were Not Authorized for Local Operations
headlines.summary: Mexicos government stated that two US federal agents killed in a car crash in the northern region were not authorized to participate in any local operation.

headlines.headline: China Drops Hint Its Next Aircraft Carrier May Be Nuclear-Powered
headlines.summary: A video released for the PLA Navys anniversary uses wordplay suggesting a new nuclear-powered warship is under construction, potentially reshaping the Pacific naval balance.

headlines.headline: Iran Foreign Minister Leaves Islamabad Without Meeting US Envoys
headlines.summary: Irans foreign minister departed Islamabad before US envoys could arrive for talks, highlighting the collapse of Pakistans mediation efforts between the US and Iran.

headlines.headline: Pakistan and Iran Leaders Discuss Regional Peace in Phone Call
headlines.summary: Pakistani PM Shehbaz Sharif spoke with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian about regional stability as diplomatic efforts continue to resolve tensions with the US. The call comes amid uncertainty over whether US envoys will travel to Islamabad for talks.

headlines.headline: Iran FM Travels to Oman After Sharing Diplomatic Framework in Pakistan
headlines.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi arrived in Oman from Islamabad where he presented a framework for ending the US-Iran conflict. Araghchi expressed skepticism about US commitment to diplomacy as Trump cancels envoy visit.

headlines.headline: Coordinated Attacks Hit Multiple Cities Across Mali
headlines.summary: Gunmen launched coordinated attacks against Malian military positions in the capital Bamako, Kati, Sevare, and northern areas in one of the largest assaults on the junta in years. The attacks targeted the international airport and military bases.

headlines.headline: Hungary's Orban to Leave Parliament After Landslide Election Defeat
headlines.summary: Viktor Orban announced he will give up his parliamentary mandate after losing to Peter Magyar in Hungary's election. The long-serving prime minister said he wants to stay on as Fidesz party leader while rebuilding the national side.

headlines.headline: Trump Renews Call to End Filibuster Over SAVE America Act Impasse
headlines.summary: President Trump called for eliminating the filibuster to pass the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility Act, which remains stalled in the Senate. The push highlights ongoing legislative gridlock on election-related legislation.

headlines.headline: Armed groups launch coordinated attacks across Mali
headlines.summary: Explosions and gunfire reported in Bamako, Kati, Gao, Kidal, and Sevare as jihadist group JNIM and separatist Azawad Liberation Front stage simultaneous assaults. The UK Foreign Office has advised against all travel to Mali, and Bamako International Airport has been temporarily closed.

headlines.headline: Macron says EU defense clause is stronger than NATO Article 5
headlines.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron declared during a visit to Greece that the EU mutual defense clause (Article 42.7) is stronger than NATO Article 5 because it leaves no option for non-compliance. The statement comes as Trump questions NATO and US commitment to European defense.

headlines.headline: US eases sanctions to let Venezuela pay Maduro legal fees
headlines.summary: The US Department of Justice modified sanctions to allow the Venezuelan government to cover legal fees for former president Nicolas Maduro, who faces federal drug trafficking charges in New York. Maduro defense lawyers had argued that blocking payment violated his right to counsel.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo brushes off Trump criticism during Africa tour
headlines.summary: Pope Leo XIV said he will continue speaking out against war despite criticism from President Trump, who attacked the pontiff for opposing US Iran policy. The Pope began an 11-day tour of Africa amid escalating Vatican-Washington tensions over the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: US senator urges China participation in nuclear arms control
headlines.summary: Senator Deb Fischer, chair of the Senate Armed Services strategic forces subcommittee, said China must be involved in any nuclear arms control talks. China has repeatedly refused to join US-Russia nuclear agreements, seeking parity before negotiations.

headlines.headline: Trump Cancels US Envoys' Trip to Pakistan for Iran Talks
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump cancelled a planned trip by US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Pakistan for Iran war mediation talks, saying 'If they want to talk, all they have to do is call.' Iran's foreign minister had already left Islamabad, dealing a setback to peace prospects.

headlines.headline: Republicans Retool Midterm Strategy Amid Iran War and Gas Prices
headlines.summary: With US gas prices up and President Trump's approval ratings down as the Iran war drags on, Republicans are recalibrating their blueprint for November's midterm elections, focusing on Trump's policies while distancing from Trump himself.

headlines.headline: Mexico Says US Agents Killed in Crash Weren't Authorized to Operate There
headlines.summary: The Mexican government stated that two US federal agents - widely reported to be CIA officers - killed in a car crash during an anti-narcotics raid were not authorized to operate on Mexican territory. One had entered as a visitor, the other on a diplomatic passport.

headlines.headline: Gaza's Deir el-Balah Holds First Municipal Elections in 20 Years
headlines.summary: Residents of Deir el-Balah in central Gaza cast ballots in the first municipal elections since 2006, with voters expressing joy at participating in democratic processes after two decades. The elections come amid stalled ceasefire negotiations.

headlines.headline: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi Meets Myanmar's Military Leader
headlines.summary: China's top diplomat visited Myanmar's capital Naypyitaw and met with Min Aung Hlaing as part of a regional tour aimed at strengthening Beijing's political, security and strategic ties in Southeast Asia. The visit follows Min Aung Hlaing's swearing in as president on April 10.

headlines.headline: Confusion over Iran talks as Trump cancels envoy trip to Pakistan
headlines.summary: Iran says no direct talks planned with US despite Trump sending envoys Kushner and Witkoff to Pakistan for negotiations. Tehran foreign ministry denies any US discussions even as Araghchi arrives in Islamabad.

headlines.headline: Islamabad bears the cost of Pakistan mediates US-Iran negotiations
headlines.summary: Pakistani capital experiences semi-lockdown conditions as the country attempts to broker peace between Washington and Tehran. Next round of talks remains uncertain as daily life disrupted.

headlines.headline: Eight killed in Russian mass strike on Dnipro and multiple cities
headlines.summary: Russia launched one of its largest aerial attacks on Ukraine, killing at least 8 and injuring 60 across Dnipro and other cities. The assault involved missiles and drones targeting multiple locations overnight.

headlines.headline: China tungsten export restrictions hit global hobby and defense supply chains
headlines.summary: Beijing limits exports of tungsten, a critical material for both hobby manufacturing and defense industries. The restrictions come amid broader trade tensions and supply chain realignments.

headlines.headline: Low Earth orbit control could determine future world order, Chinese analysis warns
headlines.summary: A Chinese strategic analysis highlights Starlink capabilities in conflict zones and warns of a new form of power that could allow or cut connectivity in real time, reshaping geopolitical leverage.

headlines.headline: Finland Joins Nordic Boycott of World Aquatics Over Russia Participation
headlines.summary: Finland announced it will not host international swimming championships while World Aquatics allows Russian and Belarusian athletes to compete, joining Norway and Poland in a growing Nordic protest against the governing body.

headlines.headline: King Charles III to Visit US Amid Iran War Tensions
headlines.summary: King Charles III and Queen Camilla begin a four-day state visit to the US on Monday, seeking to strengthen US-UK ties despite tensions over Britain refusing to support the Iran war. The visit follows Queen Elizabeth IIs 1991 precedent of addressing Congress.

headlines.headline: Iran Rejects Direct Talks with US as Pakistan Mediation Continues
headlines.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi laid out Iran's demands in Islamabad while ruling out face-to-face meetings with US negotiators. The US has dispatched envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Pakistan for indirect peace talks.

headlines.headline: Major Russian Attack Kills Seven in Ukraine as Dnipro Struck by Barrage
headlines.summary: Russia launched over 660 drones and missiles in a massive overnight attack that killed at least seven people and wounded dozens in Dnipro and other Ukrainian regions. It marks one of the largest Russian barrages in recent weeks.

headlines.headline: Four Killed in Israeli Strikes on Southern Lebanon Despite Ceasefire
headlines.summary: Israeli strikes killed four people in southern Lebanon, state media reported, while the Israeli military said Hezbollah had fired rockets at Israel. The attacks come despite an extended ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon.

headlines.headline: Ukraine and Russia Swap 193 Prisoners Each in UAE-Facilitated Exchange
headlines.summary: Russia and Ukraine exchanged 193 prisoners of war each in a swap facilitated by the United States and UAE. The returned prisoners included soldiers, border guards, and police, some with injuries.

headlines.headline: Macron Pushes to Reopen Strait of Hormuz as TotalEnergies Warns of Shortages
headlines.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron reaffirmed efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz amid the Iran conflict. TotalEnergies CEO warned of global energy shortages if the war continues for months.

headlines.headline: Germany attributes global Signal and WhatsApp cyber campaign to Russia
headlines.summary: Berlin believes the coordinated attack on encrypted messaging platforms presumably originated in Russia, according to Der Spiegel, highlighting ongoing hybrid warfare concerns.

headlines.headline: Zelensky signs cooperation deals with Azerbaijan during first wartime visit
headlines.summary: President Zelensky met with Azerbaijani President Aliyev in Baku, marking his first wartime visit to Azerbaijan and signing bilateral cooperation agreements.

headlines.headline: HH-60W Jolly Green II helicopters to take on doomsday evacuation role in Washington
headlines.summary: The US Air Force will modify its new combat rescue helicopters for VIP airlift and continuity of government operations, replacing aging UH-1N Twin Hueys.

headlines.headline: Navy rushing Hellfire missiles to carrier strike groups for counter-drone ops
headlines.summary: The US Navy is pushing more hard-kill counter-drone capabilities to its fleet as it confronts growing threats from Iranian one-way attack drones in the Middle East.

headlines.headline: Swedish intelligence warns Russian Orthodox church is espionage platform
headlines.summary: A Russian Orthodox church near Vasteras airport with an unusually tall spire and elusive priest is suspected of being used by Moscow for espionage and hybrid activities threatening Sweden's security.

headlines.headline: US Justice Department Drops Federal Reserve Investigation
headlines.summary: The DOJ has ended its criminal investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell, clearing the path for Kevin Warsh's confirmation. A judge earlier quashed subpoenas citing "essentially zero evidence" of criminal conduct.

headlines.headline: UK Reaffirms Falklands Sovereignty After US Review Report
headlines.summary: Downing Street insisted the Falkland Islands belong to the UK after reports the Pentagon was considering options to punish NATO allies. An internal email suggested reviewing the US position on Britain's claim to the territory.

headlines.headline: Seven killed in major Russian attack on Ukraine as jets scrambled in Romania
headlines.summary: Russia launched its largest attack in days with over 600 drones and missiles, killing at least seven people including five in Dnipro where an apartment building was hit. British Typhoons were scrambled from Romania as Russian drones approached NATO airspace.

headlines.headline: Coordinated jihadist attacks hit multiple cities across Mali
headlines.summary: Explosions and gunfire reported in Bamako, Gao, Kidal and Sevare in what analysts describe as the largest coordinated jihadist attack on Mali in years. Airport closed as military battles JNIM and Tuareg rebels.

headlines.headline: Bolsonaro son launches presidential bid to free father from prison
headlines.summary: Flavio Bolsonaro announces candidacy for Brazilian president with explicit goal of freeing his father Jair Bolsonaro, who is currently imprisoned. The move sets up a high-stakes election battle against Lula.

headlines.headline: NATO pushes back at reported US threat to suspend Spain over Iran war support
headlines.summary: An internal Pentagon email reportedly outlined options to punish allies perceived as lacking support for the Iran war, including suspending Spain from NATO. European allies strongly rejected the notion.

headlines.headline: US sanctions China-based oil refinery in sweeping crackdown on Iranian oil trade
headlines.summary: Treasury targets China teapot refinery and 40 shipping entities for purchasing Iranian oil, expanding enforcement against Tehrans shadow trade network amid the ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: Iran Executes Alleged Israeli Agent Over Sabotage During Protests
headlines.summary: Iran executed a man accused of acting as an Israeli agent and carrying out sabotage operations during nationwide protests. The execution highlights ongoing tensions between Iran and Israel.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu Declared Cancer-Free After Hiding Diagnosis During Iran War
headlines.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu was declared cancer-free after concealing his diagnosis during the Iran conflict. The revelation raises questions about transparency during wartime.

headlines.headline: Islamabad Residents Bear Cost of Pakistan US-Iran Mediation
headlines.summary: As Pakistan hosts US-Iran talks, local residents face disruptions from a semi-lockdown in the capital. The mediation effort comes with significant costs to daily life and commerce in Islamabad.

headlines.headline: US Air Force Aims to Double F-15EX Eagle Fleet to 267 Aircraft
headlines.summary: The US Air Force plans to boost F-15EX Eagle II inventory to 267 aircraft, doubling the original program size. The move reflects growing demand for advanced fighter capabilities amid global tensions.

headlines.headline: UK Greens Leader Slams Starmer Over 'Weaponizing' Antisemitism
headlines.summary: UK Green Party co-leader Carla Denyer criticized Prime Minister Keir Starmer for what she called 'weaponizing' antisemitism after a parliamentary rebuke. The exchange highlights tensions over Middle East policy in British politics.

headlines.headline: US Envoys Head to Pakistan for Iran Talks as Diplomatic Push Intensifies
headlines.summary: Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will travel to Islamabad on Saturday for negotiations with Iranian officials, as both sides seek to de-escalate the ongoing conflict. Iran's foreign minister Abbas Araghchi is also in Pakistan for the discussions.

headlines.headline: Europe Prepares for Prolonged Ukraine War With No Clear End Strategy
headlines.summary: With US attention focused on Iran, neither Russia nor Ukraine has a path to victory or negotiation. European leaders are bracing for a protracted conflict as American diplomatic resources shift elsewhere.

headlines.headline: US Sanctions Chinese Refinery for Buying Iranian Oil, Funding Military
headlines.summary: The Treasury Department targeted Hengli refinery for generating hundreds of millions in revenue for Iran's military operations through illicit oil sales, stepping up enforcement of sanctions.

headlines.headline: Palestinians vote in local elections across West Bank and Gaza
headlines.summary: Local elections held in the occupied West Bank and one Gazan city, though Hamas and other groups are not participating in what marks a rare electoral moment.

headlines.headline: Europe pushes back at reported US threat to suspend Spain from NATO
headlines.summary: NATO officials say there is no provision to expel members after an internal Pentagon email reportedly outlined options to punish allies over perceived lack of support for the Iran war.

headlines.headline: Everest flood warning system abandoned, thousands at risk
headlines.summary: A multi-million dollar flood warning system at Imja glacial lake near Everest has not been maintained since 2016, leaving local communities vulnerable to outburst floods.

headlines.headline: Chinese EV giant BYD says it can thrive without US market
headlines.summary: Chinas largest electric vehicle manufacturer positions itself to benefit from the global shift away from fossil fuels as fuel prices rise and geopolitical tensions shape trade.

headlines.headline: Pirates hijack oil tanker off Somalia coast
headlines.summary: Piracy in the Indian Ocean has made a comeback in recent years, with a vessel seized in a region once notorious for maritime attacks.

headlines.headline: Iran Rules Out Direct US Talks as Envoys Head to Pakistan
headlines.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi arrived in Islamabad but Iranian officials say there will be no direct negotiations with Washington, despite US envoys Witkoff and Kushner also traveling to Pakistan for potential indirect talks.

headlines.headline: Germany Deploying Warships to Mediterranean for Possible Hormuz Mission
headlines.summary: German navy vessels are being sent to the Mediterranean as Berlin considers a potential role in protecting shipping through the Strait of Hormuz amid Iran attacks on commercial vessels.

headlines.headline: Three US Aircraft Carriers Now Operating in Middle East
headlines.summary: For the first time since 2003, three US carrier strike groups are operating in Central Command's area of responsibility as the US military buildup around Iran continues.

headlines.headline: Ukraine to Field 25,000 Ground Robots for Frontline Logistics
headlines.summary: Ukraines military plans to deploy 25,000 unmanned ground vehicles to replace soldiers in dangerous logistics missions. Ground robots ran more than 9,000 frontline missions in March, triple Novembers count.

headlines.headline: Zelensky Signs Cooperation Deals During First Wartime Visit to Azerbaijan
headlines.summary: President Volodymyr Zelensky arrived in Azerbaijan for talks with Azerbaijani President Ilham Aliyev, signing cooperation agreements in his first wartime visit to the Caucasus nation.

headlines.headline: Seven Dead as Russian Strike Hits Residential Building in Dnipro
headlines.summary: A major Russian attack on Ukraine killed at least seven people, with the city of Dnipro hardest hit after a strike on a residential building. Four deaths were reported from the Dnipro attack alone.

headlines.headline: Mali Army Reports Coordinated Armed Group Attacks Across Country
headlines.summary: Mali's military says armed groups have launched coordinated attacks across the country, with witnesses reporting explosions and sustained gunfire near a military base outside the capital, Bamako.

headlines.headline: NATO Chief Says No Provision to Expel Members After US Threat Report
headlines.summary: NATO's secretary general responded to reports that the US could seek to suspend Spain over perceived lack of support for the Iran war, stating there is "no provision" in the alliance's treaty to expel members.

headlines.headline: UK Affirms Falklands Sovereignty After Reported US Review
headlines.summary: British Prime Minister's office stated that Falklands sovereignty rests with the UK following reports of a Pentagon document raising the prospect of a change in US position in retaliation for Britain not joining the Iran war.

headlines.headline: US Sanctions China-Based Oil Refinery and 40 Shippers Over Iranian Oil
headlines.summary: The US Treasury Department imposed sanctions on a China-based oil refinery and 40 shipping companies for handling Iranian oil, escalating economic pressure on Tehran amid the ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu Treated for Early-Stage Prostate Cancer
headlines.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu underwent surgery to remove a malignant tumor and says he is in "excellent physical condition." The 76-year-old leader continues to face domestic political pressures while managing Israels role in the regional conflict.

headlines.headline: Key Tadamon Massacre Suspect Arrested in Syria
headlines.summary: Amjad Youssef, wanted for the mass killing of blindfolded civilians during the Syrian civil war in April 2013, has been arrested. The notorious Tadamon massacre video showed victims being executed in the Damascus district.

headlines.headline: Thousands of Seafarers Stranded by Hormuz Blockade
headlines.summary: As the U.S. blockade on Iranian ports continues, thousands of seafarers remain stranded on ships unable to dock. The standoff is causing economic shockwaves globally, disrupting maritime trade routes.

headlines.headline: Mandelson Under EU Anti-Fraud Investigation
headlines.summary: The EUs anti-fraud office has opened a formal investigation into UK ambassador to the U.S. Peter Mandelson. The office confirmed it has "sufficient information" to proceed but did not specify if it involves fraud.

headlines.headline: European Airlines Slash Flights as Iran War Cuts Jet Fuel
headlines.summary: Soaring jet fuel costs due to the Iran conflict are forcing European airlines to cancel tens of thousands of flights. Energy authorities warn of possible fuel shortages if supplies are not replenished soon.

headlines.headline: Germany Unveils First-Ever Military Strategy, Names Russia Top Threat
headlines.summary: The Bundeswehr strategy marks Germany's first comprehensive military doctrine since WWII, explicitly identifying Russia as the primary security threat amid shifting European defense posture.

headlines.headline: Iran War: No Direct Talks Expected in Islamabad
headlines.summary: US negotiators arrived in Islamabad but Iran says no direct talks are planned, as diplomatic channels remain constrained amid the ongoing ceasefire.

headlines.headline: How Iran Is Wielding the Strait of Hormuz as Leverage
headlines.summary: Analysis of Iran's strategic use of the critical waterway for oil shipments as negotiating leverage in the ongoing conflict with the US and its allies.

headlines.headline: Israel Waiting for Green Light to Renew War Against Iran
headlines.summary: Defense Minister Katz says Israel is prepared to resume operations against Iran if the fragile ceasefire collapses, pending US approval.

headlines.headline: Battle-Tested Ukrainians Begin Training German Troops for Future War
headlines.summary: Ukrainian soldiers with frontline experience are now training German Bundeswehr troops, sharing combat lessons learned from the Russia-Ukraine war.

headlines.headline: Duterte to Face Trial for Crimes Against Humanity
headlines.summary: Philippines former President Rodrigo Duterte will stand trial at the International Criminal Court for crimes against humanity related to his deadly war on drugs, marking a major accountability moment for the controversial leader.

headlines.headline: EU Approves €106 Billion Ukraine Loan After Hungary Lifts Veto
headlines.summary: The European Union approved a massive loan package to help Ukraine after Hungary dropped its opposition, signaling continued Western financial support amid ongoing conflict with Russia.

headlines.headline: Turkey Bans Social Media for Children Under 15
headlines.summary: Turkey passed legislation prohibiting children under 15 from using social media platforms, one of the strictest digital regulations globally amid growing concerns about online safety.

headlines.headline: Trump Officials Consider Sending Afghan Allies to Congo
headlines.summary: The Trump administration is considering relocating approximately 1,100 Afghans who aided US forces to the Republic of Congo, raising concerns about the treatment of US allies after withdrawal.

headlines.headline: Pirates Hijack Oil Tanker Off Somalia Coast
headlines.summary: Pirates hijacked an oil tanker off the coast of Somalia, raising concerns about maritime security in the region and potential disruptions to shipping routes.

headlines.headline: US envoys to visit Islamabad for Iran talks as Tehran rules out direct negotiations
headlines.summary: Trump sends Witkoff and Kushner to Pakistan for indirect Iran negotiations. Tehran rejects direct talks but signals openness to a proposal addressing US demands, as markets rally on peace prospects.

headlines.headline: US State Department warns global allies about DeepSeek AI theft allegations
headlines.summary: Washington issues worldwide alert to allies about alleged AI technology theft by Chinese firms including DeepSeek, escalating tech tensions between Washington and Beijing.

headlines.headline: Britain affirms Falklands sovereignty after reported US review of status
headlines.summary: UK government states Falkland Islands sovereignty rests with Britain following reports Washington was reviewing the territory status amid shifting geopolitical priorities.

headlines.headline: NATO says no mechanism to expel members amid US-Spain tensions
headlines.summary: Alliance clarifies it has no provision for expelling members after reports the US could seek to suspend Spain over defense spending and trade disputes.

headlines.headline: US Space Force awards $3.2 billion Golden Dome missile defense contracts
headlines.summary: Twelve firms selected for major missile defense program as US accelerates space-based defense capabilities amid growing strategic competition.

headlines.headline: Germany's Merz rules out immediate EU membership for Ukraine, proposes interim Council participation
headlines.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said there is little appetite for fast-tracking Kyiv to full EU membership, but suggested allowing Ukraine to attend European Council sessions without voting rights as an interim step.

headlines.headline: US and EU sign critical minerals cooperation pact to reduce China dependence
headlines.summary: The United States and European Union signed a memorandum of understanding on April 24 to strengthen cooperation on critical minerals, part of a broader effort to secure and diversify supply chains for semiconductors, EVs, and advanced weapons.

headlines.headline: US sanctions China-based oil refinery and 40 shippers over Iran oil trade
headlines.summary: The US imposed sanctions on Hengli Petrochemical's Dalian facility, one of China's largest independent refineries with 400,000 barrels per day capacity, along with 40 shipping entities for processing and transporting Iranian crude.

headlines.headline: Trump Justice Department adds firing squads for federal executions
headlines.summary: The US Department of Justice added electrocution, gas chamber, and firing squads as execution methods, after President Trump called for expanding the use of the death penalty.

headlines.headline: US drops criminal probe of Fed Chair Powell, clearing path for Warsh nomination
headlines.summary: Federal prosecutors produced "essentially zero evidence" to suspect Powell of a crime, ending an investigation that had clouded the Federal Reserve leadership as Trump's nominee David Warsh awaits confirmation.

headlines.headline: European airlines slash thousands of flights as Iran war cuts jet fuel supplies
headlines.summary: Soaring jet fuel costs from the Iran conflict are forcing European airlines to cancel tens of thousands of flights, with energy authorities warning of possible shortages if supplies are not replenished soon.

headlines.headline: Pirates hijack oil tanker off the coast of Somalia
headlines.summary: Pirates have hijacked an oil tanker off Somalia, marking a resurgence of piracy in the Indian Ocean after years of decline.

headlines.headline: Cuban ambassador says US to blame for island crisis amid confrontation fears
headlines.summary: As the US threatens military action, Cuba ambassador blames Washington for the country crisis and says prepared for confrontation if diplomacy collapses.

headlines.headline: Mandelson under formal investigation by EU anti-fraud office
headlines.summary: The EU anti-fraud office OLAF confirms it has sufficient information to launch an investigation into Peter Mandelson, the UK ambassador to the US.

headlines.headline: US to allow firing squads, gas, and electrocution for federal executions
headlines.summary: The Department of Justice memo says the move will strengthen the death penalty and deter crime, expanding execution methods available for federal cases.

headlines.headline: US envoys Witkoff and Kushner head to Pakistan for indirect Iran talks
headlines.summary: Trump dispatched his special envoy and son-in-law to Islamabad to meet with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi through Pakistani intermediaries, as Tehran rules out direct negotiations. The diplomatic push comes with three US aircraft carriers now positioned in the region.

headlines.headline: NATO says no provision to expel members after US reportedly considered suspending Spain
headlines.summary: A NATO official stated the alliance treaty has no mechanism for suspension or expulsion following a Reuters report about an internal Pentagon email suggesting retaliating against allies for insufficient support in the Iran war. Spain refused use of its air bases for attacks on Iran.

headlines.headline: Russia Launches Major Missile and Drone Attack on Multiple Ukrainian Cities, Injuring 14
headlines.summary: Russia struck multiple Ukrainian cities overnight with missiles and drones, injuring at least 14 people. The large-scale attack continues the pattern of systematic strikes on Ukrainian civilian infrastructure.

headlines.headline: NATO Official: Trump Cannot Expel Members Over Iran War Stance
headlines.summary: A NATO official confirmed that the US cannot unilaterally expel alliance members for refusing to back the US-Israeli war against Iran. The statement comes amid Pentagon discussions about punishing non-cooperative NATO allies.

headlines.headline: Poland's Tusk Questions US Loyalty to NATO Alliance
headlines.summary: Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk has publicly questioned whether the United States remains committed to NATO, reflecting growing European anxiety about US reliability as a security partner.

headlines.headline: Ukraine's An-28 Turboprop Now Launches Interceptor Drones to Hunt Russian UAVs
headlines.summary: Ukraine has upgraded its An-28 Cash counter-drone platform to launch interceptor drones alongside its Minigun armament, marking a significant evolution in Ukrainian air defense capabilities.

headlines.headline: Italy Scraps  Billion Defense Deficit Spending Amid Economic Concerns
headlines.summary: Italy has abandoned plans for 14 billion euros in deficit spending on defense, citing competing priorities including energy costs. The decision raises questions about European defense spending commitments.

headlines.headline: US Sanctions Major Chinese Refinery and 40 Shippers Over Iranian Oil
headlines.summary: The Trump administration imposed sanctions on a major China-based oil refinery (Hengli Petrochemical) and roughly 40 shipping companies involved in transporting Iranian oil. The move implements secondary sanctions threats and comes weeks before Trump meets Xi Jinping in China.

headlines.headline: Colombia's Petro Becomes First Leader to Visit Venezuela Post-Maduro Abduction
headlines.summary: Colombian President Gustavo Petro met interim Venezuelan President Delcy Rodriguez in Caracas, becoming the first foreign leader to visit since the US military abducted Nicolas Maduro in January. Border security and US-Colombia tensions dominated discussions.

headlines.headline: Kosovo Court Jails Three Serbs for Life Over 2023 Banjska Attack
headlines.summary: A court in Pristina sentenced two ethnic Serbs to life in prison and a third to 30 years on terrorism charges for the 2023 armed attack in Banjska that killed one police officer and three gunmen. The attack was the worst since Kosovo declared independence in 2008.

headlines.headline: UN Warns Israeli Attacks on Lebanon May Violate International Law
headlines.summary: A UN human rights report found that Israeli strikes on residential buildings in Lebanon and Hezbollah rocket fire into Israel may constitute serious violations of international humanitarian law. Over 2,400 people have been killed in Lebanon since the escalation began.

headlines.headline: Trump Admin Moves Again to End Legal Status of CBP One Migrants
headlines.summary: The Trump administration announced plans to again terminate the temporary legal status of hundreds of thousands who entered the US via the CBP One app, despite a previous court ruling blocking the move. About 900,000 people were granted humanitarian parole under the Biden-era program.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu treated for prostate cancer, Israeli PM reveals
headlines.summary: Benjamin Netanyahu announced he was successfully treated for prostate cancer, revealing a previously undisclosed diagnosis. The news comes amid ongoing tensions with Iran and domestic political challenges.

headlines.headline: US Justice Department drops criminal probe into Federal Reserve Chair Powell
headlines.summary: The Justice Department has dropped its criminal investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell over renovation costs. The move likely clears the way for Kevin Warsh to potentially succeed Powell.

headlines.headline: Suspected leader of notorious Tadamon massacre arrested in Syria
headlines.summary: Amjad Youssef, wanted over the mass killing of blindfolded civilians in April 2013 during the Syrian civil war, has been arrested. The Tadamon massacre was one of the conflict most notorious atrocities.

headlines.headline: EU leaders activate mutual defence pact amid Trump NATO threats
headlines.summary: European leaders are looking to a little-known mutual assistance clause in EU treaties as Donald Trump threatens NATO allies. The move reflects growing concerns about US commitment to European security.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian soldiers emaciated from lack of food and water on frontlines
headlines.summary: Ukrainian troops are suffering from severe malnutrition as supply lines falter. Reports describe soldiers left in critical condition from inadequate food and water supplies at the front.

headlines.headline: Ukraine to field 25,000 ground robots for frontline logistics
headlines.summary: Ukraine plans to deploy 25,000 unmanned ground vehicles to replace soldiers in dangerous logistics missions. Ground robots ran over 9,000 frontline missions in March alone, triple November count.

headlines.headline: Three US carriers operate in Middle East for first time since 2003
headlines.summary: Three US aircraft carriers are now operating in Central Command area of responsibility simultaneously, reflecting the massive naval buildup amid the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: G7 expresses concern over Russia and China nuclear build-up
headlines.summary: G7 nations voice alarm at expanding nuclear arsenals of Russia and China ahead of month-long NPT review conference. China is rapidly expanding its warhead stockpile while Russia modernizes delivery systems.

headlines.headline: NATO eyes Swedish-Canadian jet for AWACS role, shifting from Boeing
headlines.summary: NATO is considering Saab GlobalEye aircraft for airborne early warning missions, moving away from Boeing platforms. Political momentum has been building for the Swedish-Canadian solution.

headlines.headline: US military pursues fleet of missile-killing laser drones
headlines.summary: The US military is reviving directed energy weapons programs for airborne platforms to counter missile threats. The initiative aims to protect American airspace from evolving aerial threats.

headlines.headline: UK affirms Falklands sovereignty after reported US review
headlines.summary: Downing Street says Falklands sovereignty rests with the UK after an internal Pentagon document reportedly raised the prospect of a change in position in retaliation for Britain not joining the Iran war.

headlines.headline: EU opens anti-fraud investigation into UK ambassador Mandelson
headlines.summary: The European Anti-Fraud Office confirms it has sufficient information to launch an investigation into Peter Mandelson, the UK ambassador to the US, though it did not specify if fraud is involved.

headlines.headline: P&G warns of $1 billion profit hit from Iran war oil prices
headlines.summary: Procter & Gamble warns higher oil prices driven by the Iran conflict will cost the company approximately $1 billion in reduced profit for fiscal 2027.

headlines.headline: Germany halves 2026 growth forecast as Iran war raises inflation
headlines.summary: The German government has cut its 2026 growth forecast in half and raised inflation expectations, citing economic disruption from the Iran conflict and elevated energy costs.

headlines.headline: UK warns of rising state-backed cyberattacks as AI accelerates threats
headlines.summary: Britain must prepare for increased state-sponsored cyberattacks, with security officials warning that AI is speeding up the evolution of cyber threats.

headlines.headline: NATO says no provision to expel members after report US could seek to suspend Spain
headlines.summary: An internal Pentagon email reportedly outlined options to punish allies over perceived lack of support for the Iran war, raising concerns about NATO alliance stability.

headlines.headline: Trump envoys Witkoff and Kushner to fly to Pakistan for Iran talks
headlines.summary: US special envoys will travel to Pakistan for diplomatic discussions as Iran says its Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is already in Pakistan but no US-Iran meeting is planned.

headlines.headline: US appeals court rejects Trump ban on asylum seekers
headlines.summary: Federal judges ruled Trumps order for swift removal at the border cast aside federal laws affording the right to seek asylum, teeing up a likely Supreme Court appeal.

headlines.headline: US to allow firing squads, gas, and electrocution for federal executions
headlines.summary: The Department of Justice issued a memo expanding execution methods to strengthen the death penalty and deter crime, a significant shift in federal execution policy.

headlines.headline: Justice Department drops criminal probe of Fed Chair Powell
headlines.summary: President Trump had accused Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell of improper cost overruns in renovating the Fed building. The probe clearance likely opens the way for a potential replacement.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu diagnosed with prostate cancer, kept diagnosis hidden during Iran war
headlines.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu was diagnosed with prostate cancer but did not inform the Israeli public during the ongoing Iran conflict. His office stated he is now cancer-free after treatment.

headlines.headline: US sanctions China-based Hengli refinery and 40 shippers over Iran oil
headlines.summary: The US imposed sanctions on Hengli Petrochemical facility in Dalian, one of China largest independent refineries processing 400,000 barrels per day, along with 40 shipping entities for trading Iranian oil.

headlines.headline: China stages navy drill with Liaoning carrier as US-Philippines begin Balikatan 2026
headlines.summary: China conducted naval exercises involving 14 large vessels including the Liaoning aircraft carrier, coinciding with the launch of US-Philippines Balikatan 2026 military exercises in the region.

headlines.headline: Turkey Fidan: Israel has become direct threat to global security
headlines.summary: Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan declared that Israel poses a direct threat to global security during a press conference, escalating diplomatic tensions between Turkey and Israel.

headlines.headline: Russia suspected of using Orthodox church near Swedish airport for espionage
headlines.summary: Swedish intelligence services suspect Moscow is using a Russian Orthodox church near Vasteras airport as a platform for espionage and hybrid activities threatening Swedens national security.

headlines.headline: Key suspect in notorious Tadamon massacre arrested in Syria
headlines.summary: Amjad Youssef, wanted for the mass killing of blindfolded civilians in April 2013 in Damascus, has been arrested. The Tadamon massacre was one of the most notorious atrocities of the Syrian civil war.

headlines.headline: UN warns Israeli attacks on Lebanon may violate international law
headlines.summary: A UN report says both Israeli attacks on Lebanon and Hezbollah rocket attacks on Israel may have breached international humanitarian law during the recent conflict.

headlines.headline: Pirates hijack oil tanker off the coast of Somalia
headlines.summary: Piracy has made a comeback in the Indian Ocean with a new hijacking incident, reviving concerns about maritime security in the region.

headlines.headline: US appeals court rejects Trump ban on asylum seekers
headlines.summary: Federal judges ruled that Trump executive order for swift removal at the border violated federal laws affording the right to seek asylum, teeing up a likely Supreme Court appeal.

headlines.headline: King Charles faces high stakes in state visit with Trump
headlines.summary: The upcoming state visit to the US looks fraught with difficulty but could be key to rescuing the special relationship, with the Falklands sovereignty question adding complexity.

headlines.headline: Kosovo court sentences three Serb separatists for 2023 monastery attack
headlines.summary: Two men received life sentences and another a 30-year term for the Banjska monastery siege that killed one police officer and three gunmen.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu treated for early-stage prostate cancer
headlines.summary: Israel's prime minister says he is in excellent physical condition after having a malignant tumor removed, raising questions about leadership stability during wartime.

headlines.headline: EU approves 106 billion euro loan package for Ukraine after Hungary lifts veto
headlines.summary: The European Union approved the massive loan package to help Ukraine meet its economic and military needs for two years, ending months of political deadlock.

headlines.headline: Trump administration vows crackdown on Chinese firms exploiting US AI models
headlines.summary: The administration is targeting foreign tech companies exploitation of US artificial intelligence models, singling out China as the gap narrows in the AI race.

headlines.headline: Colombian president visits Venezuela in first trip since Maduro abduction
headlines.summary: Border security is expected to loom large as Colombia leader Gustavo Petro meets interim Venezuelan President Delcy Rodriguez.

headlines.headline: NATO official: Trump cannot expel members over Iran stance
headlines.summary: A NATO official confirmed that the alliance founding treaty has no provision for suspending or expelling members, responding to reports the Pentagon considered punishing Spain for refusing to support US-Iran operations. Spain rejected using its bases for Iran strikes and opposed the 5% defense spending proposal.

headlines.headline: Ukraine completes 73rd prisoner swap, brings home 193 POWs
headlines.summary: Ukraine and Russia exchanged 193 prisoners each in the continuation of the Easter exchange. Released soldiers include those held in Chechnya, wounded personnel, and some facing fabricated criminal charges. Over 7,000 Ukrainians have been returned since the war began.

headlines.headline: Ukraine to field 25,000 ground robots for frontline logistics
