headlines.summary: Ukraines military plans to deploy 25,000 ground robots to replace soldiers for frontline logistics operations. Ground robots ran more than 9,000 missions in March, triple Novembers count, and captured their first Russian position last summer.

headlines.headline: Three US carriers operate in Middle East for first time since 2003
headlines.summary: Three US aircraft carriers are operating in Central Commands area of responsibility simultaneously for the first time since 2003, reflecting the massive naval presence deployed for the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Pentagon chief says US Iran blockade going global
headlines.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced the US blockade on Iran is expanding globally, with the military redirecting 34 vessels as part of the pressure campaign.

headlines.headline: US deploys three aircraft carriers to Middle East
headlines.summary: For the first time since 2003, the US has three aircraft carriers in the Middle East as tensions with Iran remain high amid a fragile ceasefire.

headlines.headline: Iran to make offer satisfying US demands, says Trump
headlines.summary: Iran is preparing to make an offer aimed at satisfying US demands, according to Trump, as diplomatic efforts continue through Pakistan.

headlines.headline: US Space Force awards $3.2B in missile defense contracts
headlines.summary: The US Space Force has tapped 12 firms for the Golden Dome missile defense program, a major new defense initiative.

headlines.headline: Trump envoys head to Pakistan for Iran ceasefire talks
headlines.summary: US special envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner are traveling to Pakistan as Iran signals willingness to make an offer satisfying US demands. Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi is already in Islamabad but no direct meeting with US delegation is confirmed.

headlines.headline: US adds firing squads, electrocution to federal execution methods
headlines.summary: The Trump administration is expanding federal execution methods to include firing squads, electrocution, and gassing as part of a broader push to prioritize the death penalty.

headlines.headline: Israel kills at least 12 Palestinians in Gaza amid ceasefire
headlines.summary: Hamas says the Israeli escalation represents the failure of the international community to uphold the Gaza truce, as ceasefire violations continue.

headlines.headline: Google to invest up to $40 billion in AI rival Anthropic
headlines.summary: Google is planning a major investment in Anthropic as competition heats up in the AI arms race between tech giants.

headlines.headline: NATO says no provision to expel members after US report on suspending Spain
headlines.summary: NATO stated its founding treaty has no mechanism for suspending or expelling members after a leaked Pentagon email suggested the US could seek to suspend Spain over its lack of support for the Iran war. Spain has refused to allow US bases on its territory for attacks on Iran.

headlines.headline: UK reaffirms Falklands sovereignty after US Pentagon memo raises review
headlines.summary: Downing Street stated Falklands sovereignty 'rests with the UK' after an internal Pentagon email reportedly suggested the US could reassess support for Britain's claim to the islands in retaliation for the UK not supporting the Iran war.

headlines.headline: US Justice Department drops criminal probe into Federal Reserve Chair Powell
headlines.summary: The Department of Justice has dropped its criminal investigation into Fed Chairman Jerome Powell, clearing the path for confirmation of his successor Kevin Warsh. Trump had accused Powell of improper cost overruns.

headlines.headline: EU anti-fraud office opens formal investigation into Mandelson
headlines.summary: The European Anti-Fraud Office (Olaf) has launched a formal investigation into former UK ambassador to the US Peter Mandelson over allegations of misconduct during his time as EU trade commissioner, including exchanges with Jeffrey Epstein.

headlines.headline: UN report: Israeli attacks on Lebanon may violate international humanitarian law
headlines.summary: A UN human rights report found Israeli strikes on residential buildings in Lebanon may amount to serious violations of international law, killing over 2,400 people since March. Hezbollah rocket fire into Israel also likely violated IHL, the report said.

headlines.headline: Trump administration to prioritize death penalty, expand use of firing squads
headlines.summary: The Justice Department announced plans to expand federal death penalty implementation, including firing squads and lethal gas. The policy reverses Biden's moratorium and calls for expanding federal death row capacity.

headlines.headline: Key suspect in notorious Tadamon massacre arrested in Syria
headlines.summary: Amjad Youssef, wanted for the 2013 mass killing of civilians in Damascus, has been arrested after a security operation. Video evidence showed Syrian soldiers leading bound, blindfolded victims to a pit before shooting them.

headlines.headline: Pirates hijack oil tanker off Somalia with 17 crew amid Hormuz disruption
headlines.summary: Pirates seized the oil tanker Honour 25 with 17 crew near the Somali coast. The hijacking highlights renewed piracy concerns as the Iran conflict disrupts Gulf shipping routes. The vessel was carrying 18,500 barrels of oil.

headlines.headline: Trump orders US military to shoot Iranian boats laying mines in Hormuz Strait
headlines.summary: President Trump has ordered the US Navy to shoot and kill any Iranian boats deploying mines in the Strait of Hormuz, escalating the standoff as Iran continues to disrupt traffic through the critical waterway. The order came after Iran laid mines for a second time this week.

headlines.headline: Pakistan blames US blockade, not Iranian divisions, for stalled ceasefire talks
headlines.summary: Pakistani officials say the US naval blockade of Iranian ports is preventing Tehran from attending talks in Islamabad, contradicting Trump who cited Irans "fractured" government. The diplomatic stalemate continues as both sides set preconditions for negotiations.

headlines.headline: Trump administration pitches US companies for Gulf reconstruction contracts
headlines.summary: The US is lobbying Gulf states to use American firms to rebuild infrastructure damaged by Iranian strikes, pressing Kuwait, Bahrain and the UAE. An Arab official called the push "tone-deaf" given the ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: Indias Reliance Industries profits hit by Iran war supply shock
headlines.summary: Billionaire Mukesh Ambanis conglomerate reported a year-on-year profit decline for the final quarter, as supply disruptions from the US-Iran war impacted oil and gas operations at Indias largest company.

headlines.headline: US lawmakers introduce bill to counter Russias religious repression in Ukraine
headlines.summary: A bipartisan group in Congress introduced the Countering Russias War on Faith Act, requiring State and Defense departments to report on Moscows persecution of religious groups in Ukraine and authorizing sanctions against those responsible.

headlines.headline: US Soldier Charged for Using Classified Intel to Bet on Maduro Raid
headlines.summary: A US soldier was charged with using classified intelligence to place a $400,000 Polymarket bet on the Venezuela raid, raising serious security concerns about insider trading on military operations.

headlines.headline: Russian Oil Exports Slump as Ukraine Hammers Ports and Refineries
headlines.summary: Russian oil exports are projected to fall to their lowest levels since 2023 as Ukrainian drone strikes continue targeting ports and refineries, despite a US sanctions waiver.

headlines.headline: EU Leaders Eye Mutual Defense Pact Amid Trump NATO Uncertainty
headlines.summary: European Union nations are examining a little-known mutual assistance obligation as an alternative to NATO, as Trump continues to criticize the alliance and European defense commitments.

headlines.headline: Kosovo Court Jails Three Serb Separatists Over 2023 Banjska Attack
headlines.summary: A Kosovo court sentenced three Serb separatists for the 2023 Banjska attack that left one police officer and three gunmen dead, a key verdict in the volatile Kosovo-Serbia conflict.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian Soldiers Left Emaciated on Frontline From Lack of Food and Water
headlines.summary: Ukrainian soldiers on the frontline are suffering from severe malnutrition due to critical supply shortages, highlighting the dire logistical challenges facing Kyiv's forces.

headlines.headline: NATO says no mechanism to expel members after US reportedly considers suspending Spain
headlines.summary: An internal Pentagon email reportedly outlines options to punish allies over perceived lack of support for the Iran war. NATO clarified there is no provision for expelling members from the alliance.

headlines.headline: UK reaffirms Falklands sovereignty after report of US policy review
headlines.summary: Downing Street stated Falklands sovereignty rests with the UK after an internal Pentagon document reportedly raised the prospect of a position change in retaliation for Britain not joining the Iran war.

headlines.headline: US Justice Department drops criminal probe of Fed Chair Jerome Powell
headlines.summary: The DOJ ended its investigation into the Federal Reserve chairman after President Trump accused Powell of improper cost overruns in renovating the Fed building. The move clears the path for Powell successor Kevin Warsh confirmation.

headlines.headline: Mandelson under formal investigation by EU anti-fraud office
headlines.summary: The European Anti-Fraud Office confirmed it has sufficient information to launch an investigation into the UK trade minister, though it has not specified whether the probe involves fraud allegations.

headlines.headline: Trump to send Witkoff and Kushner to Pakistan for Iran talks
headlines.summary: US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will fly to Pakistan for discussions on Iran, while Irans foreign minister is in Islamabad. Iranian state media reports direct US talks are not on the agenda.

headlines.headline: Zelensky makes second Saudi Arabia visit in less than a month for security-energy talks
headlines.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky is advancing agreements with Saudi Arabia in security, energy, and infrastructure during his second visit to the kingdom in under a month.

headlines.headline: Ukraine brings home 193 POWs from Russian captivity
headlines.summary: The prisoners returned include individuals against whom Russian authorities had opened criminal cases, as well as wounded soldiers. Russia also received 193 POWs in exchange.

headlines.headline: Trump cannot expel NATO members over Iran stance, alliance official says
headlines.summary: A NATO official clarified that the US president lacks authority to unilaterally suspend alliance members, amid reports that the Pentagon considered punishing Spain and others for not backing the Iran war.

headlines.headline: Iran war shows strategic value of AI for Chinese military, PLA Daily says
headlines.summary: Chinese state media highlighted the Iran conflict as demonstrating artificial intelligence's growing importance in modern warfare, emphasizing lessons for the People's Liberation Army.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu diagnosed with prostate cancer without informing Israeli public
headlines.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu was diagnosed with prostate cancer but did not disclose the information to the public, Haaretz reports.

headlines.headline: US Justice Department drops probe into Federal Reserve Chair Powell
headlines.summary: The DOJ dropped its investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell after Trump accused him of cost overruns in renovating the Fed building. The move clears the path for confirmation of his successor Kevin Warsh.

headlines.headline: Pirates hijack oil tanker off coast of Somalia
headlines.summary: A commercial oil tanker has been hijacked by pirates off the Somali coast, marking a resurgence of piracy in the Indian Ocean. The incident comes amid heightened global shipping concerns due to the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: 18 wolves found dead from suspected poisoning in Italian national park
headlines.summary: Italian authorities are investigating the deaths of 18 wolves in a national park, with poisoning suspected. Ten carcasses were found last week and another eight in recent days.

headlines.headline: Three US aircraft carriers operate in Middle East for first time since 2003
headlines.summary: The US military has three aircraft carrier strike groups in the Middle East simultaneously for the first time since 2003, amid fragile truce negotiations with Iran. The USS George H.W. Bush joins existing carriers as tensions remain high.

headlines.headline: Gaza to hold first local election in two decades
headlines.summary: Residents of Deir al-Balah are set to vote in municipal elections, the first in Gaza in 20 years. Hamas is not participating in the elections scheduled for this weekend.

headlines.headline: Pirates hijack oil tanker off Somalia coast as piracy returns to Indian Ocean
headlines.summary: An oil tanker was hijacked off the coast of Somalia, marking a resurgence of piracy in a region once notorious for attacks. Piracy had declined after international naval patrols but has made a comeback in recent years.

headlines.headline: Three Kosovo Serbs sentenced to life imprisonment for 2023 monastery siege
headlines.summary: Kosovo court sentenced two men to life imprisonment and another to 30 years for the deadly gun battle and monastery siege in 2023, one of the worst incidents since Kosovo declared independence.

headlines.headline: Taiwan president accuses China of forcing cancellation of Eswatini diplomatic trip
headlines.summary: Taiwan President accused Beijing of pressuring Eswatini to cancel a planned diplomatic visit, highlighting continued Chinese efforts to isolate Taiwan internationally.

headlines.headline: Philippines ex-president Rodrigo Duterte to face trial for crimes against humanity
headlines.summary: Former Philippine President Duterte will face trial for crimes against humanity related to his deadly war on drugs campaign, which killed thousands during his tenure.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu Diagnosed With Prostate Cancer, Kept Public in Dark
headlines.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu was diagnosed with prostate cancer without informing the Israeli public, Haaretz reports. The diagnosis comes as Israel continues to navigate the Iran war and hostage negotiations.

headlines.headline: Israeli Child Dies of Wounds From Iranian Cluster Missile Attack
headlines.summary: An 11-year-old Israeli girl has died from wounds sustained in an Iranian cluster missile strike last month, highlighting the ongoing civilian toll of the Iran-Israel conflict.

headlines.headline: Navy Rushing Hellfire Missiles to Carrier Strike Groups for Drone Defense
headlines.summary: The U.S. Navy is accelerating deployment of Hellfire missiles to carrier strike groups as counter-drone capabilities amid growing threats from one-way attack drones in the Middle East theater.

headlines.headline: Trump Orders Kill Order on Iranian Small Boats, Tripling Demining Efforts
headlines.summary: President Trump has issued a kill order for Iranian small boats and announced plans to triple demining operations in the Strait of Hormuz, though the status of current demining activities remains unclear.

headlines.headline: Mandelson Under Investigation by EUs Anti-Fraud Office
headlines.summary: The EUs anti-fraud office OLAF has launched a formal investigation into UK ambassador to the US Peter Mandelson.

headlines.headline: US Justice Department Drops Probe into Fed Chair Powell
headlines.summary: The DOJ dropped its investigation into Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell after Trump accused him of cost overruns in Fed building renovations.

headlines.headline: Irans Foreign Minister to Visit Pakistan as US Talks Remain Stalled
headlines.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi will travel to Pakistan, Oman and Russia to explore whether peace talks with the US can be reopened.

headlines.headline: EU Leaders Look to Mutual Assistance Pact Amid NATO Jitters
headlines.summary: European Union nations are examining a little-known mutual defense obligation as questions arise about US commitment to NATO under Trump.

headlines.headline: Kushner and Witkoff Said to Be Headed to Pakistan for Iran Negotiations
headlines.summary: President Trump is sending Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff to Pakistan for potential peace talks with Iran, with VP Vance on standby.

headlines.headline: NATO Has No Provision to Expel Members After US Threatens Spain
headlines.summary: NATO stated there is no provision in its treaty to expel members after a leaked Pentagon email suggested the US was considering suspending Spain from the alliance over lack of support for the Iran war.

headlines.headline: UK Rejects Falklands Review Report After Leaked Pentagon Memo
headlines.summary: Downing Street stated that Falklands sovereignty rests with the UK after a reported Pentagon document raised the prospect of changing the UKs position in retaliation for not joining the Iran war.

headlines.headline: US Soldier Charged for Betting $400K on Maduro Raid Using Classified Intel
headlines.summary: A US special forces soldier involved in the operation that captured Venezuelas Nicolas Maduro has been charged with insider trading after allegedly using classified information to bet on the outcome before it was public.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu Treated for Early-Stage Prostate Cancer
headlines.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu revealed he underwent radiation therapy for early-stage prostate cancer after a malignant tumor was detected during follow-up care.

headlines.headline: Third US aircraft carrier arrives in Middle East as Iran war continues
headlines.summary: The USS George H.W. Bush has joined two other carriers in the region as President Trump demands progress in peace negotiations with Tehran.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu reveals he underwent prostate cancer treatment during Iran war
headlines.summary: The Israeli prime minister said he kept the diagnosis secret due to the ongoing conflict with Iran. He is now in excellent physical condition after radiation therapy.

headlines.headline: Syrian authorities arrest main suspect in notorious 2013 Tadamon massacre
headlines.summary: Ex-intelligence officer Amjad Youssef was arrested for the mass killing of blindfolded civilians during the Syrian civil war. He was identified in a leaked video.

headlines.headline: Bulgaria elects pro-Russia president, EU partners watch nervously
headlines.summary: Rumen Radev won elections this week with a record of pro-Russia statements. Now Bulgarians and EU partners wait to see which way he will turn.

headlines.headline: Three US aircraft carriers operate in Middle East for first time since 2003
headlines.summary: The USS Carl Vinson, USS Truman, and a third carrier are now in CENTCOM area, marking the largest US naval presence in the region since the Iraq War.

headlines.headline: ArianeGroup studying ballistic missile production in Germany
headlines.summary: European defense contractor explores deep-strike missile capabilities in Germany as European nations seek conventional ballistic missiles amid expanding conflicts.

headlines.headline: NATO eyes Swedish-Canadian GlobalEye jet for AWACS role
headlines.summary: NATO considers Saab GlobalEye as replacement for aging Boeing E-3 Sentry fleet, signaling shift away from US manufacturer for key surveillance capability.

headlines.headline: Russia cuts key interest rate to 14.5% amid wartime inflation easing
headlines.summary: Russian Central Bank slashes rate for eighth consecutive time from record highs as wartime spending pressures begin to ease.

headlines.headline: Australia awards counter-drone contracts with lasers and interceptors
headlines.summary: Australian Army pushes forward counter-UAS capabilities under Land 156 program, integrating laser-based and interceptor solutions.

headlines.headline: India Denounces Trump for Sharing "Hellhole" Remark
headlines.summary: India summoned the US diplomatic envoy after President Trump shared radio host Michael Savage's comments calling India a 'hellhole'. India called the remarks 'uninformed' and summoned the US charge d'affaires.

headlines.headline: Gaza Set for First Local Elections in Two Decades
headlines.summary: Gaza is preparing to hold its first local elections in nearly 20 years. Hamas is not participating in the municipal elections in Deir al-Balah scheduled for this weekend.

headlines.headline: Global Hunger Report Warns of Rising Malnutrition
headlines.summary: A new global hunger report warns that conflict remains the leading driver of hunger, affecting nearly 150 million people facing acute food shortages worldwide.

headlines.headline: 18 Wolves Found Poisoned in Italian National Park
headlines.summary: Italian authorities are investigating after the carcasses of 18 wolves were found poisoned in a national park. Ten were found last week and eight more in recent days.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian Soldiers Left Emaciated on Frontline Due to Supply Shortages
headlines.summary: Soldiers on the front lines are suffering from severe lack of food and water as supply chains struggle amid ongoing Russian attacks.

headlines.headline: EU Leaders Push Mutual Defense Pact Amid NATO Crisis
headlines.summary: European Union members are planning how to come to each others aid in the event of an attack as NATO faces its worst crisis in history due to Trump criticism.

headlines.headline: Trump Says He Will Probably Put Big Tariff on UK Over Digital Services Tax
headlines.summary: President Trump accused Britain of trying to make an easy buck from American tech firms and threatened tariffs just weeks after a UK-US trade deal was announced.

headlines.headline: China Stages Navy Drill as US and Philippines Begin Balikatan Exercises
headlines.summary: The Liaoning aircraft carrier appears among 14 large naval vessels in maneuvers coinciding with the start of US-Philippines Balikatan 2026 military exercises.

headlines.headline: Polands Tusk Questions US Loyalty to NATO Alliance
headlines.summary: Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk told the Financial Times that Europe must question whether the United States remains as loyal to NATO as treaty obligations require.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu Diagnosed With Prostate Cancer
headlines.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has been diagnosed with prostate cancer but did not inform the Israeli public, Haaretz reports.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian Neptune Missiles Destroy Russian Drone Factory in Rostov
headlines.summary: Ukrainian forces attacked the factory involved in the full cycle of drone production including design, manufacturing and testing, the General Staff confirmed.

headlines.headline: US to Delay Weapons Deliveries to Europe Due to Iran War
headlines.summary: Some European counterparts have been told that select US weapons deliveries may be delayed as the Iran war continues to draw on weapons stocks, sources say.

headlines.headline: Japan Builds Up Its Southern Shield as Faith in US Security Cover Falters
headlines.summary: Japan is pushing the legal limits of its defense capabilities as it faces what officials call its most severe and complex security environment since 1945, fortifying islands in the East China Sea amid growing concerns about US reliability.

headlines.headline: UK Convicts Pro-Palestine Activists in Landmark Free Speech Case
headlines.summary: A UK court has convicted two pro-Palestine protest organizers, Ben Jamal and Chris Nineham, in a ruling that rights groups warn will spawn a broader chilling effect on civil liberties.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu Treated for Early-Stage Prostate Cancer
headlines.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu says he is in excellent physical condition after having a malignant tumor removed, with his office indicating he will continue working during recovery.

headlines.headline: Russia Kills Two in Odesa Drone Strike, Hits Foreign Merchant Ship
headlines.summary: Russian drones killed an elderly married couple and struck a foreign merchant vessel in Odesa overnight, continuing attacks on Ukrainian port infrastructure amid the ongoing war.

headlines.headline: China DeepSeek Unveils New AI Model Challenging US Rivals
headlines.summary: Chinese startup DeepSeek has released its latest AI model, DeepSeek-V4-Pro, which the company claims beats all rival open models in mathematics and coding benchmarks, marking another advance in the AI competition between Beijing and Washington.

headlines.headline: Trump Orders Shoot-to-Kill for Iranian Boats Blocking Hormuz
headlines.summary: President Trump has authorized the US military to fire on Iranian small boats obstructing the Strait of Hormuz, escalating tensions in the critical shipping chokepoint. The order comes as Iran continues its blockade operations amid the ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: Iran's Exiled Crown Prince Reza Pahlavi Attacked in Berlin
headlines.summary: Iran's exiled crown prince Reza Pahlavi was splattered with red liquid during an appearance in Berlin, in what appears to be a protest action. The incident highlights tensions within the Iranian diaspora amid the ongoing Iran-US conflict.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu Treated for Early-Stage Prostate Cancer
headlines.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced he has been treated for early-stage prostate cancer, with a malignant tumor successfully removed. Netanyahu stated he is in excellent physical condition following the procedure.

headlines.headline: NATO Rejects US Moves to Suspend Spain Over Iran War Stance
headlines.summary: NATO officials stated there is 'no provision' to expel members after reports emerged of a Pentagon email outlining options to punish allies for perceived lack of support during the Iran war. The revelation has sparked concern about US pressure on alliance partners.

headlines.headline: UK Reaffirms Falklands Sovereignty Amid US Review Concerns
headlines.summary: The UK government has stated that Falkland Islands sovereignty rests with the UK following reports that a Pentagon document suggested reviewing US positions in retaliation for Britain not joining the Iran war effort.

headlines.headline: Ukraine urges EU to shut down Russia crypto sanctions evasion in 21st package
headlines.summary: Kyiv is pushing Brussels to target cryptocurrency channels Russia uses to evade sanctions as part of the upcoming 21st EU sanctions package. Ukraine's top sanctions official said the measures must "finally shut down channels for using cryptocurrencies to circumvent restrictions."

headlines.headline: DeepSeek releases V4 AI model in fresh challenge to US competitors
headlines.summary: Chinese AI startup DeepSeek unveiled its long-delayed V4 model, intensifying competition among Chinese AI companies and posing a renewed challenge to U.S. players already feeling threatened by Chinese foundation models. Huawei says its Ascend chips "fully support" the model.

headlines.headline: Israel and Lebanon extend ceasefire three weeks after White House talks
headlines.summary: Israel and Lebanon agreed to extend their ceasefire by three weeks following talks at the White House. Trump said the U.S. will work with Lebanon to protect it from Hezbollah. Hezbollah was not present at the talks and says it retains "the right to resist."

headlines.headline: NATO Has No Provision to Expel Members, Spokesperson Says After Spain Suspension Report
headlines.summary: NATO responded to reports of a Pentagon email proposing to suspend Spain over lack of support for the Iran war, clarifying the alliance has no mechanism to expel members. The incident highlights growing tensions between the US and European allies.

headlines.headline: Kenyan President Sparks Diplomatic Outcry After Mocking Nigerians English
headlines.summary: President William Ruto claimed Kenyans speak the best English in the world while mocking Nigerians pronunciation, drawing criticism from Nigerians and diplomatic observers across Africa.

headlines.headline: White House Memo Alleges Systematic AI Model Theft by Chinese Firms
headlines.summary: A memo from Michael Kratsios claims Chinese companies are distilling US AI models through systematic theft, escalating tensions over technology and intellectual property between Washington and Beijing.

headlines.headline: Philippines ex-president Duterte to stand trial for crimes against humanity
headlines.summary: The International Criminal Court has confirmed crimes against humanity charges against former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte over the extrajudicial killing of thousands during his "war on drugs." He will face trial at The Hague.

headlines.headline: Europe debates mutual defense options outside NATO as US commitment wavers
headlines.summary: European Union nations are examining their little-known mutual defense obligation as confidence in US security guarantees erodes. Experts caution it is no replacement for NATO but reflects growing European anxiety about American reliability.

headlines.headline: India voices anger after Trump shares comments calling it a hellhole
headlines.summary: India has expressed outrage after US President Donald Trump shared social media posts calling India a "hellhole." The comments have sparked diplomatic tensions between the two nations amid ongoing geopolitical realignments.

headlines.headline: Ukraine First Country to Scale Remotely Operated Interceptor Drones
headlines.summary: Ukraine has become the first nation to scale the use of remotely operated interceptor drones capable of long-distance strikes, Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov announced, marking a significant advancement in drone warfare technology.

headlines.headline: China Places EU Defense and Aerospace Firms on Export Control List
headlines.summary: Beijing barred seven EU defense and aerospace entities from receiving dual-use items originating in China, escalating trade tensions amid the ongoing Iran conflict and Western sanctions regime.

headlines.headline: DeepSeek Unveils New AI Model as Huawei Pledges Full Chip Support
headlines.summary: Chinese AI company DeepSeek released its cost-efficient V4 model that competes with top closed-source models from OpenAI and Google, while Huawei vowed full support with new chips, intensifying the US-China AI race.

headlines.headline: Iran War Has Significantly Depleted US Ammunition Stockpiles
headlines.summary: The ongoing conflict with Iran has significantly depleted US ammunition stockpiles, raising concerns about American military readiness and the sustainability of prolonged engagements in the Middle East.

headlines.headline: Thailand to Put 44 Opposition Figures on Trial Over Royal Insult Law Reform Bid
headlines.summary: The Supreme Court ordered trial for current and former lawmakers from the People's Party and dissolved Move Forward party who attempted to amend the lese-majeste law, facing potential lifetime bans from office.

headlines.headline: US Says Iran Can Play at World Cup but Bars Those With IRGC Ties
headlines.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio confirmed Iranian players can participate in the 2026 World Cup hosted in the US, but individuals with connections to the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps will be denied entry.

headlines.headline: Iran Rejects Trump Leadership Rift Claims, Presents Unified Front
headlines.summary: Iranian officials including President Pezeshkian and Foreign Minister Araghchi dismissed Trump assertions of internal divisions, emphasizing iron unity and complete obedience to Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei.

headlines.headline: EU Unblocks €90 Billion Ukraine Loan, Approves New Russia Sanctions
headlines.summary: The European Union gave final approval to a massive €90 billion loan package for Ukraine and a 20th round of sanctions on Russia after Hungary and Slovakia dropped objections following Ukraine restoration of Russian oil flows.

headlines.headline: Philippines Ex-President Duterte to Stand Trial at ICC for Crimes Against Humanity
headlines.summary: The International Criminal Court has confirmed that former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte will stand trial for extrajudicial killings during his war on drugs, which killed thousands.

headlines.headline: Meta Cuts 8,000 Jobs, 10 Percent of Workforce, While Microsoft Offers Buyouts
headlines.summary: Meta announced its largest layoff since 2023, cutting one in ten employees after spending billions on AI development, while Microsoft offers voluntary buyouts to 8,750 workers.

headlines.headline: Pentagon floats suspending Spain from NATO over Iran rift
headlines.summary: An internal Pentagon email outlines options to punish NATO allies that failed to support US operations in the Iran war, including potentially suspending Spain from the alliance and reviewing US position on Britain's Falkland Islands claim.

headlines.headline: Iran air defenses activated over Tehran amid suspected hostile activity
headlines.summary: Iranian state media reported air defense systems were activated in parts of Tehran following reports of suspected hostile aerial activity, the first such incident since the Iran-US ceasefire began.

headlines.headline: Trump rules out using nuclear weapons against Iran
headlines.summary: President Trump stated he would not use nuclear weapons against Iran, saying 'Why would I use a nuclear weapon when we've decimated them without it?' after previous threats about destroying Iranian civilization.

headlines.headline: US Navy reviews Ford-class carrier costs amid budget pressures
headlines.summary: The Navy is analyzing costs and design of future Ford-class aircraft carriers USS William J. Clinton and USS George W. Bush to ensure they 'make sense' amid scrutiny of the $13 billion per ship program.

headlines.headline: China's DeepSeek releases new AI model with 'drastically reduced' costs
headlines.summary: DeepSeek released a new AI model capable of processing extra-long texts at significantly lower costs, intensifying the US-China AI competition following last year's shockwaves from its reasoning model.

headlines.headline: Trump Says King Charles Visit Could Help Repair US-UK Relations
headlines.summary: President Trump told BBC that King Charles upcoming visit to the US could help mend strained relations between the two countries.

headlines.headline: India Elections Test Hindu Right in Bengali Heartland
headlines.summary: PM Modis BJP has been making gains in West Bengal, but critics decry an audit that removed nine million voters, many of them Muslim.

headlines.headline: Iran's Exiled Crown Prince Reza Pahlavi Splattered With Red Liquid in Berlin
headlines.summary: Iran's exiled Crown Prince Reza Pahlavi was splattered with red liquid after a news briefing in Berlin where he criticized the US-Iran ceasefire. The attack occurred as he left Germany's federal news conference building.

headlines.headline: Trump Says Israel and Lebanon Agree to Extend Ceasefire by 3 Weeks
headlines.summary: President Trump announced Israel and Lebanon agreed to extend the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire by three weeks after talks at the White House. The meeting between Israeli and Lebanese ambassadors was the second in a week, representing the first direct diplomatic talks in decades.

headlines.headline: Millions of Americans May Now Qualify for Canadian Citizenship Under New Law
headlines.summary: A recent change to Canada's citizenship requirements has led to a surge in applications from Americans. The new law, which took effect December 15, allows those with Canadian parents or grandparents to claim citizenship more easily.

headlines.headline: Russian drone strike kills married couple, injures 15 in Odesa residential buildings
headlines.summary: A Russian overnight drone attack on multiple residential buildings in Odesa killed two civilians and wounded 15 others, the latest in a series of strikes on southern Ukrainian cities.

headlines.headline: Seven torture chambers holding Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant staff uncovered
headlines.summary: Ukrainian human rights organization Truth Hounds has identified at least seven torture chambers used to detain staff of the occupied Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant, revealing systematic abuse of Ukrainian personnel.

headlines.headline: US offers $4 million bounty for Chinese money launderer linked to crypto scams
headlines.summary: The US State Department announced a $4 million reward for information leading to the arrest of Daren Li, a Chinese national who fled after admitting to laundering funds from cryptocurrency scams targeting victims across Southeast Asia.

headlines.headline: Navy rushing Hellfire missiles to carrier groups for anti-drone defense
headlines.summary: The US Navy is accelerating deployment of Hellfire missiles to carrier strike groups as the threat of one-way attack drones in the Middle East intensifies following Iranian attacks on shipping.

headlines.headline: Iran war created biggest ever energy security threat, IEA warns
headlines.summary: The International Energy Agency declared the US-Iran conflict has created the single largest threat to global energy security in history, with Strait of Hormuz disruptions sending shockwaves through oil markets worldwide.

headlines.headline: Trump orders US military to "shoot and kill" Iranian boats laying mines in Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: President Trump has ordered the Navy to destroy small Iranian boats deploying mines in the strategic waterway, tripling minesweeping operations as tensions escalate despite an ongoing ceasefire.

headlines.headline: Ukraine emerges as international arms exporter, selling drone defense tech to Middle East
headlines.summary: Ukraine has developed defense technology to counter Iranian drones similar to those Russia uses, and is now exporting these systems to countries facing similar threats.

headlines.headline: Meta Cuts 8,000 Jobs as Microsoft Offers Buyouts Amid AI Spending Surge
headlines.summary: Major tech layoffs continue as Meta announces 10% workforce reduction and Microsoft offers voluntary retirement to 7% of workers, both citing AI investments.

headlines.headline: More Than 500 Killed in Tanzania Election Violence Inquiry Finds
headlines.summary: Opposition parties accused security forces, but the official report does not attribute responsibility for the deaths during election unrest.

headlines.headline: Trump ally to lead bipartisan delegation to China ahead of May summit
headlines.summary: Republican senator will head to China on May 1 with stops in Shanghai and Beijing as the Trump administration increases pressure on Beijing amid the Iran crisis.

headlines.headline: Finland says China-Russia ties mean EU trade deal is off the table
headlines.summary: Finnish foreign minister says Helsinkis tough stance means a de facto veto on any EU-China trade agreement due to Beijings continued support for Moscow.

headlines.headline: 12 Russian FSB officers killed in drone strike on command post in occupied Donetsk
headlines.summary: Ukrainian forces used FP-2 drones with 60-100kg payloads to strike an FSB command post, killing 12 officers in a significant intelligence blow to Russian security services.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian unmanned vessel launches interceptor to knock out Shahed drone
headlines.summary: Ukraine has deployed a powerful new defense against Russian Shahed drones - an unmanned naval vessel that can launch interceptors, potentially blunting Russias ability to force concessions.

headlines.headline: EU Approves $106 Billion Loan for Ukraine Military Support
headlines.summary: The European Union approved a massive loan package heavily weighted toward military spending, signaling expectations of a prolonged conflict. Unlike previous aid packages, this one is designed to put Ukraine on solid footing for a long fight against Russia.

headlines.headline: Iran Under Collective Military Leadership After Khamenei Death
headlines.summary: Following the killing of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran has shifted to a collective leadership structure with the Revolutionary Guards wielding significantly more power. The new arrangement includes prominent generals and Mojtaba Khamenei as supreme leader.

headlines.headline: BBC Analysis: Iranian Forces Staged Video of Ship Seizures
headlines.summary: Video analysis indicates that parts of a propaganda video showing masked Iranian forces seizing ships appear to have been filmed hours after the vessels were reportedly captured. The revelation raises questions about Iran's information operations during the conflict.

headlines.headline: Pope urges US and Iran to return to peace talks, condemns capital punishment
headlines.summary: Pope Leo XIV called for de-escalation and a return to diplomatic negotiations between the US and Iran during his Africa tour, criticizing capital punishment while addressing the ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: Trump reclassifies medical marijuana as less-dangerous drug in historic shift
headlines.summary: President Trump signed an order reclassifying state-licensed medical marijuana as a less-dangerous drug, marking a historic shift in US drug policy after decades of strict federal classification.

headlines.headline: Meta slashes 8,000 jobs as AI spending soars
headlines.summary: Meta announced its largest layoff since 2023, cutting 10% of its workforce as the company ramps up AI infrastructure investments, joining other tech giants in cost-cutting measures.

headlines.headline: More than 500 killed in Tanzania election violence, inquiry finds
headlines.summary: An official inquiry found over 500 people were killed during Tanzania elections, with opposition parties accusing security forces, though the report does not specify who was responsible for the deaths.

headlines.headline: White House memo claims mass AI theft by Chinese firms
headlines.summary: A White House memo from Michael Kratsios alleges that firms, mainly in China, are wrongfully distilling US AI models, raising tensions over intellectual property and technology competition.

headlines.headline: UK Police Arrest Two More Over Alleged Iran-Backed Arson Plot Targeting Jewish Site
headlines.summary: Police did not name a specific location but said the intended target was connected to the Jewish community. The arrests are part of a counter-terrorism investigation.

headlines.headline: Israeli Police Detain Professor Over Kippa With Israeli and Palestinian Flags, Cut Off Palestine Flag
headlines.summary: Alex Sinclair says two officers detained him at a cafe, accusing him of incitement. When he refused to leave without his kippa, an officer cut out the Palestinian flag.

headlines.headline: Trump Administration Probes NYC Education Department Over Pro-Palestinian Group
headlines.summary: The probe escalates federal pressure on local institutions over activities related to Israel-Gaza protests and speech.

headlines.headline: Venice Biennale Jury Excludes Russia and Israel From Artist Awards as EU Threatens Funding Cut
headlines.summary: The decision marks a cultural front opening in geopolitical tensions, with European Union officials questioning continued funding.

headlines.headline: The Quad Is on the Brink of Extinction
headlines.summary: The US-India-Japan-Australia security partnership faces existential questions as member states recalibrate their China strategies.

headlines.headline: Trump Orders Military to Shoot Iranian Boats Blocking Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: President Trump has authorized US forces to fire on Iranian small boats obstructing the strategic waterway, escalating tensions as the naval blockade continues.

headlines.headline: Israel and Lebanon Agree to Extend Ceasefire by Three Weeks
headlines.summary: Trump announced the extension after ambassadors from both countries met in Washington. The fragile truce was due to expire Sunday.

headlines.headline: US Boards Ship Carrying Iranian Oil as Blockade Intercepts 33 Vessels
headlines.summary: US Central Command reports intercepting 33 vessels since imposing the blockade on ships traveling to and from Iran.

headlines.headline: Navy Secretary John Phelan Leaves Post Effective Immediately
headlines.summary: The Pentagon announced Phelan's departure amid tensions over US shipbuilding, the latest in a series of top defense leadership exits.

headlines.headline: US Soldier Charged After Betting $400,000 on Maduro's Removal Using Classified Info
headlines.summary: Gannon Ken Van Dyke allegedly made trades on Polymarket based on classified information about the Venezuela operation.

headlines.headline: Prince Harry visits Kyiv in show of support for Ukraine
headlines.summary: The Duke of Sussex attended a security conference in the Ukrainian capital, demonstrating continued Western support as global attention remains focused on the Middle East conflict.

headlines.headline: US boards Iranian oil tanker in Indian Ocean as blockade expands
headlines.summary: US forces intercepted the M/T Majestic X carrying Iranian oil, marking an escalation of the naval blockade. Trump also ordered the Navy to "shoot and kill" any boats laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: Philippines ex-president Duterte to face ICC trial for crimes against humanity
headlines.summary: International Criminal Court judges ruled there are substantial grounds to believe Duterte guided the anti-drugs crackdown that killed thousands, clearing the way for a landmark trial.

headlines.headline: Taiwan president blames China for cancellation of Africa trip
headlines.summary: President Lai Ching-te postponed his visit to Eswatini after China pressured African countries to deny overflight permissions, in the latest sign of escalating cross-strait tensions.

headlines.headline: Trump officials consider sending 1,100 Afghan allies to Congo
headlines.summary: Afghans who aided US forces during the 20-year war may be resettled in Congo instead of the United States, drawing criticism from veterans and advocacy groups.

headlines.headline: EU Approves €106 Billion Ukraine Loan After Hungary Lifts Veto
headlines.summary: The European Union formally approved a massive loan package to help Ukraine meet its economic and military needs for the next two years, overcoming Hungary's previous objections.

headlines.headline: Iran's Exiled Crown Prince Targeted with Red Substance in Berlin
headlines.summary: Reza Pahlavi was splashed with red substance while leaving a press conference where he stated diplomacy with Iran 'has been given enough chance', amid ongoing Iran-US tensions.

headlines.headline: Trump's Navy Secretary Out After Reported Pentagon Clashes
headlines.summary: John Phelan's abrupt departure comes as the US Navy enforces a high-stakes blockade of Iran, following the firing of a top US Army general.

headlines.headline: Deaths of CIA Agents in Mexico Spark Sovereignty Row
headlines.summary: President Sheinbaum spars with Washington over the role of Americans in a Chihuahua anti-drug operation, raising diplomatic tensions between the two countries.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Unmanned Vessel Launches Interceptor Against Shahed Drone
headlines.summary: Ukraine unveiled a powerful new defense against Shahed drones that could blunt Russia's ability to force concessions in future negotiations—the first such capability demonstrated.

headlines.headline: Warner Bros shareholders approve $111 billion Paramount takeover
headlines.summary: Shareholders approved one of the largest media mergers in history as Warner Bros accepts Paramount's $111 billion offer. The deal comes as Trump attended a dinner with Paramount's billionaire backers, the Ellisons.

headlines.headline: Canada rejects US trade demands as tensions escalate over tariffs
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Carney stated that the US "does not get to dictate terms" for any trade agreement, responding to US officials who threatened action over Canada's boycott of American alcohol products.

headlines.headline: Italy rejects replacing Iran at World Cup after Trump official suggestion
headlines.summary: Italian officials slammed as "shameful" the idea that their national team should replace Iran at the 2026 World Cup, following suggestions from Trump administration figures.

headlines.headline: US Justice Department watchdog to investigate delayed Epstein files release
headlines.summary: The DOJ inspector general will review the withholding and overdue release of secret files related to Jeffrey Epstein, as lawmakers accuse the Trump administration of using heavy redactions to protect powerful figures.

headlines.headline: Trump says King Charles visit could repair US-UK relations
headlines.summary: President Trump told the BBC that next week's visit by King Charles III could help repair strained relations between the US and UK, following tensions over the Iran war and trade disputes.

headlines.headline: More than 500 killed in Tanzania election violence
headlines.summary: An official inquiry found over 500 people were killed during Tanzania's contested elections. Opposition parties accused security forces, but the report did not specify who was responsible.

headlines.headline: Third US aircraft carrier arrives near Iran as tensions persist
headlines.summary: The USS George H.W. Bush has arrived in Middle Eastern waters, joining two other carriers as the US maintains naval pressure on Iran amid fragile ceasefire negotiations.

headlines.headline: Trump plans to invite Putin to G-20 summit in Miami
headlines.summary: President Trump intends to invite Russian President Vladimir Putin to the G-20 summit scheduled for Miami, officials say, signaling potential diplomatic engagement despite ongoing Ukraine tensions.

headlines.headline: Condom prices could surge 30% due to Iran war disruption
headlines.summary: The world's largest condom producer warns that shortages of raw materials and chemicals caused by the Iran conflict could force price increases of up to 30 percent globally.

headlines.headline: Ukraine expands ePoints system rewarding battlefield kills with weapons credits
headlines.summary: Ukraine is developing the ePoints system as a foundation for troop motivation and the mathematics of war, Defense Minister Fedorov said. The system rewards soldiers with credits for battlefield achievements.

headlines.headline: Russia recruited Ukrainian teenagers to carry out school attacks, SBU says
headlines.summary: Russian operatives recruited Ukrainian teenagers and gave them step-by-step instructions to prepare attacks on schools and shoot classmates, according to Ukraines Security Service.

headlines.headline: Sweden warns of fuel rationing as Hormuz standoff disrupts global energy flows
headlines.summary: Rising oil and gas prices linked to the Strait of Hormuz standoff are pushing Sweden to prepare for tougher economic measures as inflation risks grow. The government is warning citizens that fuel rationing may be necessary.

headlines.headline: Turkey bans social media for under-15s in major digital safety overhaul
headlines.summary: Turkeys new rules prohibit minors under 15 from registering on social media platforms and mandate effective age verification systems. The move is part of a major digital safety initiative.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo XIV Africa Tour Sparks Debate Over Political Commentary
headlines.summary: During his Africa trip, Pope Leo XIV made unusually forthright comments but grew uncomfortable with how his criticism was interpreted, highlighting tensions between religious leadership and geopolitics.

headlines.headline: South African Police Chief Suspended Over $20M Health Contract Scandal
headlines.summary: President Ramaphosa suspended police chief Fannie Masemola over a controversial health contract, citing the critical role he plays in fighting crime.

headlines.headline: US-Kuwaiti Journalist Acquitted in Kuwait Over Iran War Social Media Posts
headlines.summary: Ahmed Shihab-Eldin faced charges including spreading false information after reposting images related to the Iran war. Lawyers say he has been acquitted.

headlines.headline: Everest Route Blocked by Massive Glacier Chunk During Peak Season
headlines.summary: Sherpas cannot prepare the route to the world highest peak because a huge block of ice is blocking the way during the critical climbing season.

headlines.headline: European Airlines Cancel Tens of Thousands of Flights as Jet Fuel Costs Soar
headlines.summary: The Iran war's impact on oil supplies has driven jet fuel prices to record highs, forcing European carriers to slash routes and raise fares.

headlines.headline: UK PM Warns Growing Foreign-Backed Proxy Attacks on British Soil
headlines.summary: The prime minister expressed increasing concern that multiple countries are orchestrating attacks within the UK through proxy actors.

headlines.headline: Somalia Faces Famine as 6.5 Million Face Acute Hunger
headlines.summary: Drought, failed rains, and ongoing conflict have pushed over 6 million Somalis into food insecurity, with children facing acute malnutrition risks.

headlines.headline: EU Sanctions Hermitage Museum Director Over Crimea Excavations
headlines.summary: The EU sanctioned Mikhail Piotrovsky, director of the Hermitage Museum, for the museum's role in illegal archaeological excavations and destruction of Ukrainian heritage sites in occupied Crimea.

headlines.headline: Trump Orders Navy to Shoot and Kill Boats Laying Mines in Hormuz
headlines.summary: President Trump announced the US will "shoot and kill" any vessels laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz, including small boats. The Pentagon warned that clearing advanced mines could take months, raising energy concerns.

headlines.headline: Israel Signs M Aerial Munitions Deal Amid Regional Tensions
headlines.summary: Israel's Defense Ministry signed a  million deal with Elbit for aerial munitions, citing preparations for an "intense security decade" as the country faces ongoing multi-front tensions despite ceasefires.

headlines.headline: Trump Orders Navy to Shoot Iranian Boats Laying Mines in Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: President Trump authorized the US Navy to attack any Iranian boats found laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz, stating forces can "shoot and kill" such vessels. The order comes amid continued tensions despite a ceasefire extension.

headlines.headline: ICC Confirms Crimes Against Humanity Trial for Ex-Philippine Leader Duterte
headlines.summary: ICC judges ruled there are substantial grounds to believe former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte guided an anti-drugs crackdown that killed thousands, clearing the way for a crimes against humanity trial.

headlines.headline: US Senate Passes  Billion Funding Plan for ICE and Border Patrol
headlines.summary: Republicans passed the first step of a major immigration enforcement funding package after a marathon vote-a-rama, but face a long road to break the impasse over funding Trump's border policies.

headlines.headline: US Navy Secretary John Phelan leaving post immediately amid shipbuilding tensions
headlines.summary: Pentagon says Navy Secretary Phelan is departing "effective immediately," the latest in a series of top defense leadership exits. Reports cite tensions over US shipbuilding and naval readiness.

headlines.headline: Trump reclassifies medical marijuana as less-dangerous drug in historic shift
headlines.summary: The Trump administration has moved state-licensed medical marijuana into a lower drug category, a historic policy shift aimed at increasing research and access.

headlines.headline: Two trains collide head-on in Denmark, five critically injured
headlines.summary: A head-on train collision north of Copenhagen has left five people critically injured. The accident occurred at a level crossing during early Thursday hours.

headlines.headline: Condom prices could rise 30% due to Iran war, says world top producer
headlines.summary: Karex, the world largest condom producer, warns prices could increase 30% due to supply chain disruptions from the Iran conflict, affecting global healthcare supplies.

headlines.headline: Israeli soldiers testify to systematic looting in Lebanon with commanders knowledge
headlines.summary: Haaretz reports Israeli soldiers have testified to widespread looting of homes in southern Lebanon, with claims that commanders are aware but taking no action. The report adds to growing scrutiny of Israeli military conduct during the conflict.

headlines.headline: Germany unveils strategy to become Europes strongest military by 2039
headlines.summary: Defense News reports Germany has unveiled an ambitious plan to build Europes most powerful military within 15 years, citing the Russian threat and the need for European self-reliance amid US focus on Iran.

headlines.headline: Ukraine signs drone defense deal with Saudi Arabia Qatar and UAE
headlines.summary: President Zelensky announced agreements with Gulf states to supply interceptor drones capable of destroying Iranian Shahed drones at a fraction of their cost. The deal strengthens Ukraines air defense capabilities.

headlines.headline: UK health data of 500000 people listed for sale in China
headlines.summary: The Straits Times reports UK health records of half a million people have been discovered for sale in China, prompting outrage and concerns about data security in an already strained bilateral relationship.

headlines.headline: Russia and North Korea open new road bridge linking the two countries
headlines.summary: A ground-breaking ceremony was held for a new road bridge spanning the Tumen River that will directly connect North Korea to Russia, deepening the strategic partnership between Moscow and Pyongyang.

headlines.headline: Israel kills Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil, drawing international condemnation
headlines.summary: Lebanese PM called the attack a "war crime" after journalist Amal Khalil was killed in what officials described as a "double-tap" strike. Her death has prompted fresh claims about Israel targeting media workers.

headlines.headline: EU formally approves €90 billion Ukraine loan and 20th Russia sanctions package
headlines.summary: The European Union gave final approval to a massive €90 billion loan package for Ukraine, heavily weighted toward military spending, reflecting the view that peace in Ukraine remains distant.

headlines.headline: Millions in India stripped of vote before critical West Bengal state election
headlines.summary: Experts say Muslims and other minorities have been disproportionately deleted from electoral rolls ahead of the West Bengal elections, as the government seeks to "purify" voter lists.

headlines.headline: About 250 Rohingya refugees missing after boat capsizes in Andaman Sea
headlines.summary: A boat carrying Rohingya refugees capsized in the Andaman Sea, leaving approximately 250 people missing in one of the deadliest maritime incidents involving the persecuted minority in recent months.

headlines.headline: EU Formally Approves 90 Billion Euro Ukraine Loan, 20th Russia Sanctions Package
headlines.summary: The EU gave final approval to a massive 90 billion euro loan for Ukraine alongside its 20th sanctions package against Russia. Zelensky said the funds will strengthen Ukraine's army and enable social obligations.

headlines.headline: Russia Uses Subsidized Mortgages to Drive Population Replacement in Occupied Mariupol
headlines.summary: Russian authorities are drawing new Russian buyers to occupied Mariupol with subsidized mortgages while thousands of Ukrainians whose homes were destroyed or seized remain without housing.

headlines.headline: Iran Fires on 3 Ships in Strait of Hormuz After Trump Extends Ceasefire
headlines.summary: Iranian forces fired on three vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. Iranian media reported the MSC Francesca and Epaminondas were being escorted to Iran, raising concerns about continued tensions.

headlines.headline: Iran's Exiled Crown Prince Splashed with Red Substance in Berlin
headlines.summary: Reza Pahlavi was targeted while leaving a press conference where he said diplomacy with Iran "has been given enough chance." The incident highlights tensions among Iranian opposition figures.

headlines.headline: US Seizes Second Iranian Oil Tanker as Strait of Hormuz Tensions Escalate
headlines.summary: The US Navy has seized a second tanker carrying Iranian oil, with destroyers now shadowing additional vessels that departed Iran before the US-imposed blockade began. President Trump has ordered the Navy to shoot any boats placing mines in the strategic waterway.

headlines.headline: Germany Breaks With Israel Over Iran War Conduct
headlines.summary: German officials have issued critical comments about Israel in recent weeks, signaling Berlins growing frustration with its longtime ally amid the ongoing Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Bulgarias Pro-Russia Leader Faces West-East Choice After Election Win
headlines.summary: Rumen Radev, known for pro-Russia statements, handily won Bulgarias presidential election. Now both Bulgarians and EU partners wait to see whether he will align with Moscow or Brussels.

headlines.headline: Putins Approval Ratings Drop as Internet Restrictions Frustrate Russians
headlines.summary: Expanding state restrictions on the Internet are making daily life difficult for millions of Russian citizens, sending President Putins approval ratings on a rare downward trajectory.

headlines.headline: China Rejects US Criticism Over Taiwan Flight Pressure
headlines.summary: Beijing dismissed Washington's criticism after the US accused China of using intimidation to block Taiwan President Lai Ching-te's flight permits to Africa. The diplomatic spat highlights escalating tensions over Taiwan's international space.

headlines.headline: EU Approves €106 Billion Loan for Ukraine, Signaling Long War Ahead
headlines.summary: The European Union approved a massive €106 billion loan package for Ukraine, reflecting Brussels' assessment that peace with Russia remains distant. The loan is backed by profits from frozen Russian assets.

headlines.headline: Pew Poll Shows Americans Losing Confidence in Trump's Ukraine Handling
headlines.summary: A new Pew Research Center survey reveals declining American confidence in President Trump's approach to the Russia-Ukraine war, with growing skepticism about US strategy as the conflict enters its third year.

headlines.headline: Russian Battlefield Gains Halt, Worst Progress in Two Years
headlines.summary: Moscow's forces have made minimal territorial gains in recent weeks, with analysts describing it as their worst progress in two years. Ukrainian defensive positions have stabilized despite ongoing Russian assaults.

headlines.headline: EU Announces 20th Sanctions Package Targeting Russian Energy, Military
headlines.summary: The European Union adopted its 20th round of sanctions against Russia, targeting energy revenues, the military-industrial complex, trade, and financial services including cryptocurrency operations.

headlines.headline: Israel Air Force Technicians Charged with Spying for Iran
headlines.summary: Two Israeli Air Force technicians have been charged with espionage for Iran, marking a significant internal security breach as the Iran-Israel conflict continues. The suspects allegedly passed sensitive military information to Iranian intelligence.

headlines.headline: Trump Class Battleship Program Revealed: $17 Billion Per Vessel
headlines.summary: New details emerge about the US Navy's Trump Class battleship program, with an estimated cost of $17 billion per vessel. The program represents a major shift in naval strategy amid tensions with Iran.

headlines.headline: Patriot PAC-3 Missiles to Arm Navy Destroyers in Major Integration
headlines.summary: The US Navy has awarded a contract to integrate Patriot PAC-3 MSE missiles with Aegis destroyers, planning to purchase 405 missiles in FY2027. This significantly enhances naval air defense capabilities.

headlines.headline: Russia Recruited Ukrainian Teenagers for School Attacks, SBU Says
headlines.summary: Russian operatives gave Ukrainian teenagers step-by-step instructions to prepare attacks on schools and shoot classmates, according to Ukraine's Security Service. The recruitment targeted minors as part of destabilization efforts.

headlines.headline: Two trains collide head-on in Denmark, five critically injured
headlines.summary: A head-on collision between two trains at a level crossing north of Copenhagen has left at least five people critically injured. Danish authorities are investigating the cause of the accident which occurred early Thursday morning.

headlines.headline: South African court orders Zambia to return former president Lungu's body
headlines.summary: A South African court has ruled that Zambia must return the remains of former president Edgar Lungu, who died in exile. Zambian authorities and Lungu's family have been in a dispute over where the former leader should be buried.

headlines.headline: Nigeria's suspected coup plotters deny treason charges
headlines.summary: Suspected coup plotters in Nigeria have pleaded not guilty to treason charges. Allegations of a coup plot first surfaced last year when an Independence Day parade was cancelled.

headlines.headline: US intercepts three Iranian oil tankers in Asian waters
headlines.summary: The United States has intercepted three Iranian oil tankers in Asian waters as tensions escalate over Iran's seizure of commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, marking a significant escalation in the maritime conflict.

headlines.headline: Europe has 'maybe 6 weeks of jet fuel left,' energy agency warns
headlines.summary: The head of Europe's energy agency warns the continent faces a critical jet fuel shortage within six weeks due to supply disruptions from the Middle East conflict, threatening aviation across the continent.

headlines.headline: US announces ceasefire extension with Iran
headlines.summary: The United States announces an extension of its ceasefire with Iran as diplomatic efforts continue, though tensions remain high over ship seizures in the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: Lufthansa cuts 20,000 flights as Iran war causes jet fuel shortage
headlines.summary: German airline Lufthansa announces massive flight reductions citing surging fuel prices and supply disruptions caused by the US-Iran conflict, signaling broader economic impacts on global aviation.

headlines.headline: Beijing turns to cautious diplomacy in Iran's war with Israel
headlines.summary: China, a longtime friend of Tehran, is carefully navigating its diplomatic role as the US-Iran conflict threatens regional stability and global oil supplies.

headlines.headline: Russian Oil Pumping Station Hit by Ukrainian Drones SBU Source Says
headlines.summary: Ukrainian drones struck the Gorky oil pumping station in Nizhny Novgorod Oblast overnight, causing serious disruption according to an SBU source.

headlines.headline: Prince Harry Arrives in Kyiv to Remind World What Ukraine Faces
headlines.summary: Prince Harry visited Kyiv stating it is good to be back in Ukraine, in a show of support for the country amid ongoing war.

headlines.headline: Navy Secretary John Phelan Removed by Pentagon
headlines.summary: The Pentagon removed Navy Secretary John Phelan with no reason provided for the move.

headlines.headline: Lebanon to Confront Israel Over Home Demolitions at Washington Talks
headlines.summary: Lebanese officials will raise the issue of widespread Israeli home demolitions in a new round of talks in Washington on Thursday.

headlines.headline: Japan Lifts Post-WWII Ban on Lethal Weapons Exports
headlines.summary: Japan has ended its decades-long prohibition on exporting lethal weapons, allowing sales to 17 countries amid heightened regional security concerns. The move represents a major shift from Japan's pacifist constitutional stance.

headlines.headline: EU Approves €90 Billion Loan for Ukraine, Reopens Druzhba Pipeline
headlines.summary: The European Union approved a massive €90 billion loan package for Ukraine, heavily weighted toward military spending, after Hungary and Slovakia dropped opposition following the reopening of the Druzhba oil pipeline.

headlines.headline: Lebanon Accuses Israel of Killing Journalist in Air Strike
headlines.summary: Lebanon's prime minister accused Israel of war crimes after an air strike killed a journalist, with attacks on Red Cross vehicles also preventing rescuers from reaching the site.

headlines.headline: China Tightens Grip on AI Firms Shedding Chinese Ties
headlines.summary: A Chinese government probe of Manus AI, a company acquired by Meta, reveals Beijing's new red line for tech companies attempting to decouple from Chinese ownership amid US-China technology competition.

headlines.headline: EU Approves €90 Billion Loan for Ukraine, Unblocking Critical Pipeline
headlines.summary: European Union approved a massive loan package for Ukraine after Hungary dropped its opposition following Viktor Orban election defeat. The deal also restarts the Druzhba oil pipeline, ending months of stalemate over stalled supplies to Hungary.

headlines.headline: Japan Lifts Post-WWII Ban on Lethal Weapons Exports
headlines.summary: Japan announced a historic shift from its pacifist stance, allowing Japanese companies to sell lethal arms to 17 countries amid heightened regional security concerns and the ongoing Iran conflict affecting global stability.

headlines.headline: Prince Harry Visits Kyiv in Surprise Trip to Support Ukraine
headlines.summary: The Duke of Sussex arrived in Kyiv to remind people what Ukraine is up against, marking a significant diplomatic gesture as European leaders gather in Cyprus to discuss a 90 billion euro loan package for Ukraine.

headlines.headline: Iran Fires on Three More Ships in Strait of Hormuz After Ceasefire Extension
headlines.summary: Iranian forces opened fire on three vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz after Trump extended the ceasefire deadline. The MSC Francesca and Epaminondas were reportedly being escorted to Iran, further disrupting critical energy shipping routes.

headlines.headline: Navy Secretary John Phelan Out Immediately After Pentagon Clashes
headlines.summary: The Pentagon announced Navy Secretary John Phelan is out immediately, providing no reason for the sudden departure. The move comes amid reports of internal clashes over naval operations in the Middle East.

headlines.headline: UK and France Lead 30-Nation Military Push to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: The United Kingdom and France are spearheading a 30-nation coalition military effort to secure and reopen the Strait of Hormuz for commercial shipping, as Iran continues to disrupt the critical energy transit corridor.

headlines.headline: US Treasury considers currency swap lines for Gulf and Asian allies amid Iran war fallout
headlines.summary: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told senators that allies in the Gulf and Asia have requested dollar swap lines to stabilize markets amid economic uncertainty from the US-Iran conflict. Democrats questioned whether Trump family financial ties to UAE influenced the decision.

headlines.headline: Prediction market Kalshi penalizes three US candidates for betting on their own races
headlines.summary: The platform fined and suspended three unnamed political candidates for "insider trading" by wagering on their own election outcomes. The enforcement action comes amid growing calls for regulation of prediction markets.

headlines.headline: Iran Fires on Three Ships in Strait of Hormuz as Diplomacy Stalls
headlines.summary: Iran attacked three vessels in the strategic waterway, seizing two of them. The escalation comes as US blockade continues and diplomatic efforts to end the conflict face mounting challenges.

headlines.headline: Rep. David Scott, Georgia Democrat, Dies at Age 80
headlines.summary: The 13-term congressman from Georgia died while seeking re-election. Scott served on the House Financial Services Committee and was known for bipartisan efforts.

headlines.headline: Slovakia Resumes Russian Oil Flow Through Ukraine Pipeline
headlines.summary: Russian oil deliveries to Slovakia have resumed through the Druzhba pipeline after Ukraine unblocked the route, resolving a months-long standoff over transit fees.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo Criticises Equatorial Guinea Prisons at End of Africa Tour
headlines.summary: During his visit, Pope Leo delivered a frank critique of prison conditions in Equatorial Guinea, calling for reforms as he concluded his Africa tour.

headlines.headline: India and South Korea Agree to Double Trade to  Billion by 2030
headlines.summary: The two nations signed agreements to expand cooperation in shipbuilding, defense, and AI technology, strengthening economic ties amid regional tensions.

headlines.headline: Tencent releases first flagship AI model led by former OpenAI researcher
headlines.summary: Chinese tech giant Tencent unveiled its HY3 AI model, described as its most powerful yet, developed under former OpenAI researcher Yao Shunyu. The model is open-source with 295 billion parameters.

headlines.headline: G7 drops climate change from Paris agenda to avoid US confrontation
headlines.summary: France confirmed climate change has been removed from the G7 environment ministers meeting agenda in Paris to prevent conflict with the United States, focusing instead on less contentious issues.

headlines.headline: EU unblocks €90bn Ukraine loan after Hungary pipeline repaired
headlines.summary: Ukraines Zelensky joins EU leaders in Cyprus as the bloc approves a massive loan package, ending a deadlock after Hungary lifted its veto following Kyivs repair of a Russia-damaged pipeline.

headlines.headline: Peru ministers resign after president postpones F-16 fighter jet deal
headlines.summary: Top Peruvian cabinet members resigned following President Boluarte decision to postpone a US-backed F-16 fighter jet purchase. The Trump administration warned the move reflects bad faith negotiations.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Minister Visits Disputed South China Sea Island as Tensions Rise
headlines.summary: Taiwan Ocean Affairs Minister Kuan Bi-ling made a rare visit to Itu Aba (Taiping Island) to observe coast guard drills, as US-Philippines military exercises draw Chinese anger. China dispatched a warship and sailed an aircraft carrier through the Taiwan Strait.

headlines.headline: China Builds New Military Island in Disputed South China Sea Waters
headlines.summary: Satellite imagery shows China rapidly constructing a crescent-shaped island on Antelope Reef in the Paracels. The base, nearly twice the size of New York's Central Park, will likely host airstrips, radar, and missile facilities.

headlines.headline: Warner Bros Discovery and Paramount Merger Vote Looms Amid Regulatory Scrutiny
headlines.summary: Shareholders are set to vote on a merger combining Warner Bros Discovery with Paramount Skydance, placing CNN and CBS News under one roof. The deal faces antitrust investigations and concerns over foreign investments from Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and UAE.

headlines.headline: Iran Attacks Ships in Strait of Hormuz as US Reinforces Middle East
headlines.summary: Iran has fired on commercial vessels in the strategic Strait of Hormuz after Trump extended a ceasefire, prompting the US to send thousands more troops to the region. The escalation threatens one of the worlds most critical oil shipping lanes.

headlines.headline: US Intercepts Three Iranian Oil Tankers in Asian Waters
headlines.summary: American forces have seized three Iranian oil tankers in the Indian Ocean, expanding the US-Iran conflict into the Indo-Pacific region. The interceptions target Irans covert oil exports to China.

headlines.headline: Israeli Strike Kills Journalist in Lebanon Amid War Crimes Accusations
headlines.summary: An Israeli airstrike killed a Lebanese journalist after ongoing attacks blocked rescuers from reaching the area. Lebanons prime minister accused Israel of war crimes over the targeted killing.

headlines.headline: Navy Secretary John Phelan Fired During Iran Crisis
headlines.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has fired Navy Secretary John Phelan amid ongoing naval operations against Iran. The dismissal comes as the US maintains a naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: Chemical Leak at West Virginia Plant Kills Two, Dozens Hospitalized
headlines.summary: A chemical release at a West Virginia industrial plant killed two workers and sent at least 30 people to hospitals. Emergency crews are investigating the cause of the leak.

headlines.headline: Germany unveils first post-WWII military strategy to become Europe's strongest army by 2039
headlines.summary: Defense Minister Boris Pistorius presented a new national defense strategy marking Germany's first such plan since World War II, as Russia's growing threat drives European rearmament. The Bundeswehr will move from rigid hardware quotas to a flexible, effects-based planning model.

headlines.headline: Spanish Defense Minister announces 100 armored vehicles for Ukraine during Kyiv visit
headlines.summary: Spain will send ammunition and 100 armored vehicles to Ukraine, Defense Minister Margarita Robles announced during a visit to Kyiv, calling it a testament to Spain's commitment to democratic values and a just peace.

headlines.headline: Russia could be ready for NATO conflict within a year after Ukraine, Dutch intelligence warns
headlines.summary: Moscow's goal would not be to defeat the alliance militarily but to divide it through limited territorial gains, according to a new Dutch intelligence report as European nations accelerate defense preparations.

headlines.headline: CIA agents deaths in Mexico spark sovereignty row between Mexico and White House
headlines.summary: The deaths of CIA agents in Mexico have triggered a diplomatic dispute with the White House, raising questions about US intelligence operations on Mexican soil.

headlines.headline: US blocks Iraq dollar shipments to pressure Tehran
headlines.summary: The United States has halted dollar shipments to Iraq, forcing Baghdad to distance itself from Iran. The money comes from Iraq own oil revenues but the US controls its delivery.

headlines.headline: Condom prices could surge 30% due to Iran war
headlines.summary: The world largest condom producer Karex warns prices may rise sharply as rubber supplies are disrupted by conflict in the Middle East. The Malaysia-based company supplies global markets.

headlines.headline: Federal judge strikes down Trump clean energy slowdown
headlines.summary: A federal judge has blocked Trump administration actions that slowed clean energy projects, ruling the moves exceeded executive authority. The decision affects wind and solar developments.

headlines.headline: Anti-Trump sentiment unites European politics
headlines.summary: Trump brand has sunk so low that conservatives are joining leftist politicians in opposing him, creating unexpected political unity across Europe. The dynamic is reshaping electoral coalitions.

headlines.headline: Lufthansa cuts 20,000 summer flights as Iran war doubles jet fuel prices
