headlines.summary: German airline Lufthansa will cancel 20,000 European short-haul flights this summer, citing soaring fuel costs from the Strait of Hormuz closure. The carrier will stop flying to 10 cities and retire 27 aircraft as jet fuel prices have doubled since the US-Iran conflict began.

headlines.headline: US restricts intelligence sharing with South Korea after minister reveals North Korean nuclear site
headlines.summary: Washington has partially restricted satellite intelligence sharing with Seoul after South Korea's unification minister publicly identified a suspected North Korean uranium enrichment facility in Kusong. The move highlights growing tensions in the US-South Korea alliance under President Lee Jae Myung's conciliatory approach to Pyongyang.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo condemns prison conditions in Equatorial Guinea, calls for freedom
headlines.summary: During his Africa tour finale, Pope Leo criticized Equatorial Guinea's prison conditions and urged the oil-rich nation to use its resources for all citizens, not just the elite. He addressed 100,000 people including President Obiang, the world's longest-serving leader, ahead of a visit to an infamous prison.

headlines.headline: El Salvador begins mass trial of 486 alleged MS-13 gang members
headlines.summary: A Salvadoran court started one of the biggest mass trials under President Bukele's emergency powers, prosecuting 486 alleged MS-13 members for over 47,000 crimes. Human rights groups warn the collective prosecution violates due process and blocks access to legal counsel.

headlines.headline: Argentina arrests suspect in assassination of Colombian presidential hopeful Miguel Uribe
headlines.summary: Argentine authorities arrested a Colombian man suspected of logistical involvement in the assassination of Senator Miguel Uribe, who was shot during a campaign event in June 2025 and died in August. Prosecutors believe the attack was orchestrated by a FARC dissident group called Second Marquetalia.

headlines.headline: Second French peacekeeper dies after Lebanon ambush blamed on Hezbollah
headlines.summary: Anicet Girardin became the third French soldier to die since the start of Middle East fighting, following an ambush that highlights the dangers facing UN peacekeeping forces in southern Lebanon.

headlines.headline: US condemns China after African states block Taiwan president eSwatini flight
headlines.summary: A State Department official called the incident an abuse of the international civil aviation system, after African countries prevented Taiwan leader from transiting to eSwatini.

headlines.headline: Trump envoy pushes to replace Iran with Italy in World Cup
headlines.summary: The plan is an attempt to mend ties with Italian PM Meloni after she and Trump fell out over his attacks against Pope Leo, according to a report.

headlines.headline: Iran ramps up ship attacks in Strait of Hormuz after ceasefire extension
headlines.summary: With Iran refusing to commit to peace talks, the future of the ceasefire remains in doubt as Iranian forces continue targeting vessels in the strategic waterway.

headlines.headline: US Navy destroyer fires deck gun at ship for first time since 1988
headlines.summary: USS Spruance engaged another vessel with its deck gun in a first for the Navy in nearly four decades, reflecting the escalation in maritime conflict in the region.

headlines.headline: Iran says reopening Strait of Hormuz 'impossible' amid US blockade
headlines.summary: Iran's chief negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf declared it 'not possible' to reopen the strategic waterway while the US maintains its naval blockade of Iranian ports, calling the blockade a 'blatant violation' of the ceasefire. President Pezeshkian said the blockade and threats are obstacles to genuine negotiations.

headlines.headline: US Navy Secretary John Phelan leaves Trump administration immediately
headlines.summary: Pentagon announced Navy Secretary John Phelan's departure 'effective immediately' with Undersecretary Hung Cao named acting secretary. Phelan is the latest high-ranking military leader to exit as the US enforces a naval blockade against Iran.

headlines.headline: Israeli strike kills journalist in Lebanon after blocking rescue efforts
headlines.summary: Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil was killed in an Israeli strike in southern Lebanon after rescuers were blocked from accessing the site by further Israeli fire. Lebanon's prime minister called the targeting of journalists and obstruction of relief 'war crimes'.

headlines.headline: High-stakes West Bengal election begins amid voter roll controversy
headlines.summary: Voters in India's West Bengal state head to the polls in a critical contest where about 9 million voters — 12% of the electorate — were removed from electoral rolls. PM Modi's BJP is mounting an aggressive push to unseat Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee's Trinamool Congress.

headlines.headline: Taiwan President Cancels Africa Trip After China Pressures Countries to Revoke Flight Permits
headlines.summary: Seychelles, Mauritius and Madagascar revoked flight permits for President Lai Ching-tes aircraft after intense Chinese pressure. This marks the first time a Taiwanese leader has had to cancel a foreign trip due to revoked overflight rights.

headlines.headline: Iran Sees Trump Ceasefire Extension as Sign US Blinked First in Standoff
headlines.summary: Iranian leaders believe Trumps indefinite ceasefire extension validates their strategy of outlasting American patience. Analysts say Tehran calculates it can endure the costs of war longer than Washington can tolerate the Strait of Hormuz closure.

headlines.headline: Peru's Top Ministers Resign Over Stalled US Fighter Jet Deal
headlines.summary: Multiple Peruvian cabinet ministers have resigned in protest after interim president Balcazar postponed the purchase of F-16 fighter jets, saying the decision should be left to the country's next leader.

headlines.headline: Moldova Sentences Former Powerful Oligarch Plahotniuc to 19 Years
headlines.summary: Vladimir Plahotniuc, Moldova's wealthiest businessman who de facto controlled the country's government in the 2010s in what critics called a "captured state," was sentenced to 19 years in prison as part of Moldova's alignment with European democratic values.

headlines.headline: EU Unblocks €90 Billion Loan for Ukraine After Hungary Lifts Veto
headlines.summary: The European Union approved a massive €90 billion loan package for Ukraine after Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban dropped his opposition following his election defeat. The loan comes as Ukraine reopens the Druzhba pipeline to Hungary, ending months of stalemate.

headlines.headline: Two CIA Officers Killed in Mexico Counter-Narcotics Operation
headlines.summary: Two Americans killed in a vehicle crash in Chihuahua, Mexico were CIA officers participating in a counter-cartel operation led by Mexican armed forces. The CIA has significantly expanded its international antidrug work under the Trump administration.

headlines.headline: Condom Prices Could Rise 30% Due to Iran War
headlines.summary: Karex, the world's largest condom producer, warns prices may jump 30% as the US-Iran conflict disrupts global supply chains. The Malaysian company produces over 5 billion condoms annually and supplies major brands including Durex and Trojan.

headlines.headline: Iranians cross into Turkey just to access the internet as Iran cuts global connectivity
headlines.summary: Iran has cut off access to the global internet, forcing some citizens to travel across the border into Turkey to make video calls and access outside communications.

headlines.headline: Ships attacked in Strait of Hormuz as US-Iran ceasefire holds but tensions persist
headlines.summary: Three vessels came under fire in the strategic waterway hours after Trump extended the ceasefire with Iran. Peace talks in Pakistan failed to materialize.

headlines.headline: Taiwan president cancels Africa trip after China pressures countries to revoke overflight rights
headlines.summary: Lai Ching-te abandoned his visit to Eswatini after Seychelles, Mauritius and Madagascar revoked overflight permission due to intense Chinese pressure.

headlines.headline: Climate change poses rising threat to democracy with extreme weather disrupting elections
headlines.summary: At least 94 elections across 52 countries have been disrupted by climate-related impacts over the past two decades, researchers found.

headlines.headline: Trump officials consider sending Afghans who aided US forces to Democratic Republic of Congo
headlines.summary: Up to 1,100 Afghans who helped US forces during the war could be sent to DRC after Trump stopped the resettlement program that allowed them to apply for US residency.

headlines.headline: Trump fires Navy Secretary John Phelan amid ongoing Iran war
headlines.summary: Pentagon announced Navy Secretary John Phelan will leave office immediately, making him the latest in a string of senior military officials dismissed during the US-Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Iran fires on three ships in Strait of Hormuz as ceasefire holds
headlines.summary: Iran state TV reported the Revolutionary Guard attacked two ships which are now in Iranian custody, extending the blockade of the vital waterway.

headlines.headline: UK PM Starmer refuses to resign amid growing Mandelson scandal
headlines.summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer faced fresh calls to step down over his appointment of Peter Mandelson, defending his decision in Parliament.

headlines.headline: War in Iran has depleted half of US Patriot and THAAD missile stockpiles
headlines.summary: The prolonged conflict with Iran has consumed significant portions of American air defense reserves, raising concerns about global military readiness.

headlines.headline: Migrant caravan leaves southern Mexico, many no longer US-bound
headlines.summary: A migrant caravan departed from a southern Mexican city, but participants say many are no longer aiming for the US border, reflecting shifting migration patterns amid heightened enforcement.

headlines.headline: El Salvador holds mass trial for 486 alleged MS-13 members
headlines.summary: El Salvador conducted a mass trial for 486 alleged members of the notorious MS-13 gang, with human rights groups warning that collective prosecutions violate due process and block defendants from accessing legal counsel.

headlines.headline: Iran Seizes Two Cargo Ships in Strait of Hormuz as Blockade Standoff Continues
headlines.summary: Iranian forces attacked and seized two vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, with one cargo ship reported as "heavily damaged." The escalation comes as Iran says reopening the strait is "impossible" if the US blockade continues, keeping the Gulf shipping chokepoint on edge.

headlines.headline: Moldova's former powerful oligarch Plahotniuc sentenced to 19 years in prison
headlines.summary: Vladimir Plahotniuc, Moldova's wealthiest businessman who de facto controlled the country's government in the 2010s, was sentenced in absentia. His fall is seen as part of Moldova's alignment with European democratic values.

headlines.headline: Germany unveils strategy to become Europe's strongest military by 2039
headlines.summary: The Bundeswehr will move away from rigid hardware quotas toward a flexible, effects-based planning model. The plan comes amid concerns about Russian military buildup.

headlines.headline: Canada PM hits back after Trump team demands 'entry fee' before trade talks
headlines.summary: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney rejected US demands for concessions before trade negotiations could begin, pushing back against the Trump administration's approach.

headlines.headline: Baltic nations brace for Iran war impact on US weapons shipments
headlines.summary: Delays in ammunition deliveries could reopen a thorny question for Baltic HIMARS users: Would the US permit non-Lockheed rockets to be loaded?

headlines.headline: US Justice Department Withdraws Subpoenas in John Brennan Investigation
headlines.summary: The DOJ reversed course on subpoenas in the investigation of former CIA Director John Brennan, now requesting voluntary interviews. A Trump loyalist, Joe diGenova, was installed to lead the inquiry.

headlines.headline: NATO Launches First Response to Suspected Deep-Sea Cable Sabotage
headlines.summary: A suspected sabotage attack on a deep-sea communications cable has triggered NATO's first coordinated response under its new critical infrastructure protection framework, marking a significant escalation in hybrid warfare tactics.

headlines.headline: Rwanda-Backed Rebels Seize Congo's Humanitarian Hub City
headlines.summary: Rwanda-backed M23 rebels have entered a key safe-haven city in eastern DR Congo that serves as a critical aid hub, raising fears for hundreds of thousands of displaced civilians and threatening regional stability.

headlines.headline: Italy Backs Saudi Arabia Joining UK-Japan Fighter Jet Program
headlines.summary: Italian PM Meloni announced support for Saudi Arabia's participation in the Global Combat Air Programme (GCAP), signaling deepening defense ties between Gulf states and Western allies amid shifting regional dynamics.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo Skips Major African Catholic Nations on Continental Tour
headlines.summary: Pope Leo's Africa tour is drawing attention for skipping several of the continent's largest Catholic populations, raising questions about the Vatican's strategic priorities in the region.

headlines.headline: USAF Doubles F-15EX Eagle II Order as Fleet Modernization Accelerates
headlines.summary: The US Air Force has doubled its procurement order for the F-15EX Eagle II fighter jet, providing a ready successor to aging F-15E Strike Eagles. The decision reflects growing defense needs amid global tensions.

headlines.headline: Iranian Mines in Hormuz Could Take Six Months to Clear
headlines.summary: Iranian naval mines deployed in the Strait of Hormuz could require up to six months of clearing operations, according to reports, prolonging the disruption to global oil shipments even if hostilities cease.

headlines.headline: Israel's Dependence on Chinese Drones Exposes IDF Vulnerabilities
headlines.summary: Israel's military reliance on Chinese-made drones has created strategic vulnerabilities, as the IDF's supply chain depends on a country with close ties to Iran. The dependence raises concerns about technology transfers and operational security.

headlines.headline: Russia forces Ukrainians in occupied territories to accept Russian property titles or face eviction
headlines.summary: A new Russian law requires Ukrainians in captured regions to obtain Russian title deeds or risk losing their homes, darkening Moscow's portrayal of normalcy in occupied territories.

headlines.headline: Millions of Indians stripped of voting rights before critical West Bengal election
headlines.summary: Muslims and other minorities have been disproportionately deleted from electoral rolls ahead of West Bengal state elections, in what experts describe as a government effort to "purify" voter lists.

headlines.headline: Zelensky Pushes to Restart Peace Talks, Open to Any Venue Except Russia, Belarus
headlines.summary: President Zelensky said Ukraine is ready to meet with Russian representatives in any country except Russia or Belarus, calling for resumption of U.S.-mediated talks. The appeal comes as peace negotiations have remained frozen for nearly two months, with Washington shifting focus to the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: EU Approves 20th Russia Sanctions Package After Hungary, Slovakia Drop Veto
headlines.summary: EU ambassadors agreed on the 20th package of sanctions against Moscow after Hungary and Slovakia lifted their veto. The move follows Orban election defeat and paves way for a 90-billion-euro loan for Kyiv. Ukraine also resumed oil flows through the Druzhba pipeline.

headlines.headline: China Praises African Nations for Blocking Taiwan President Flight
headlines.summary: China expressed "high appreciation" for Seychelles, Mauritius and Madagascar after they denied overflight rights to Taiwanese President Lai Ching-te plane, forcing cancellation of his trip to Eswatini. Beijing denied applying economic pressure while praising the countries for supporting the one-China principle.

headlines.headline: US Seizes Iranian-Linked Ship in Indian Ocean, Extends Ceasefire Deadline
headlines.summary: U.S. forces boarded the sanctioned oil tanker M/T Tifani in the Indian Ocean, marking the first interdiction outside the Middle East since Operation Epic Fury began. Trump announced an extended ceasefire with Iran at Pakistan request while maintaining the blockade, as Iranian officials rejected the terms.

headlines.headline: Russia to Halt Oil Flow to Germany via Druzhba Pipeline from May 1
headlines.summary: Moscow confirmed it will suspend shipments of Kazakh oil to Germany through the pipeline, adding another layer of energy disruption to European markets already strained by the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: India Eyes Mediator Role in US-Iran Conflict as Singh Signals Diplomatic Opening
headlines.summary: Defense Minister Rajnath Singh suggested India may take on a larger peacemaker role in the US-Iran conflict during a visit to Berlin, highlighting Modis balanced diplomatic posture.

headlines.headline: Singapore Faces Threefold Governance Test From Iran War Fallout
headlines.summary: The Straits Times analysis examines how Singapore must manage immediate disruptions while navigating ongoing geopolitical turmoil and uncertainty from the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: China-Japan Flight Rift Deepens as 2,700 Flights Cancelled in March
headlines.summary: Nearly half of planned flights between China and Japan were cancelled last month as Beijing and Tokyo remain locked in tensions, signaling deteriorating bilateral relations.

headlines.headline: Asia Braces for Food Shortages as El Nino and Iran War Effects Collide
headlines.summary: A precarious planting season looms as suppressed rainfall and soaring fertilizer costs threaten harvests across the region, with Iran war disruptions compounding El Nino impacts.

headlines.headline: Lebanon Charts Own Path in Israel Talks Separating From Iran
headlines.summary: Lebanon is seeking to distance itself from Iran in potential negotiations with Israel, with officials saying We cannot let Iran be our spokesperson in regional diplomacy.

headlines.headline: IDF Kills Two Hezbollah Operatives Who Crossed Ceasefire Line in Lebanon
headlines.summary: The Israeli Air Force struck and killed two Hezbollah operatives who crossed the ceasefire line in southern Lebanon, amid ongoing tensions on the northern border.

headlines.headline: Woman and Child Killed in Russian City After Ukrainian Drone Strike
headlines.summary: A Ukrainian drone attack caused an apartment building to partially collapse in the Russian city of Syzran, killing a woman and child and injuring 12 others.

headlines.headline: EU Drafts Entry Ban on Former Russian Combatants Targeting War Veterans
headlines.summary: EU High Representative Kaja Kallas proposed restricting former Russian combatants entry to the bloc by Junes European Council, with Ukraines foreign minister endorsing the plan.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Asks Turkey to Host Zelenskyy-Putin Peace Talks
headlines.summary: Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha said Ukraine had approached Turkey and other capitals about hosting potential peace talks between Zelenskyy and Putin.

headlines.headline: Russia Launches Massive Drone and Missile Attack on Ukraine, Killing 16
headlines.summary: Russian forces targeted civilian areas across Ukraine with nearly 700 drones and dozens of ballistic and cruise missiles in the worst aerial attack in weeks. Ukrainian officials acknowledged interceptor stockpiles are running critically low.

headlines.headline: Key Takeaways After Iran-US-Israel Ceasefire Reached
headlines.summary: A tentative two-week ceasefire has paused hostilities between Iran, Israel, and the United States, but significant challenges remain for a permanent deal. Major differences persist between Trump and Iran's surviving leaders following Khamenei's death.

headlines.headline: Trump Global Tariffs Face Fresh Legal Challenge in Trade Court
headlines.summary: Trump's temporary global import tariffs are under renewed legal assault in the U.S. Court of International Trade after the Supreme Court struck down his broader IEEPA-based tariffs in February.

headlines.headline: Europe Accelerates NATO Fallback Plan in Case of US Withdrawal
headlines.summary: European officials are advancing contingency plans dubbed "European NATO" to preserve defense capabilities if the U.S. pulls out. Germany, previously opposed to go-it-alone approaches, has now signed on.

headlines.headline: Zelenskyy Seeks More Arms Deals With European Allies
headlines.summary: Ukraine's president is visiting three European capitals in 48 hours to secure military and financial support. Germany agreed to a 4 billion euro defense package while Norway pledged 9 billion euros in assistance.

headlines.headline: Italy Summons Russian Ambassador Over TV Host Insults to PM Meloni
headlines.summary: Italy summoned the Russian ambassador to protest insults directed at Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni by a pro-Kremlin TV host, escalating diplomatic tensions between Rome and Moscow.

headlines.headline: China Leverages Energy Aid Against Philippines During US Military Drills
headlines.summary: China implied energy assistance for the Philippines could be tied to Manila holding military drills with the US and allies, signaling Beijings willingness to weaponize energy reserves in diplomatic disputes.

headlines.headline: Iran War May Crush Oil Demand Now But Spike It Long-Term
headlines.summary: While the Strait of Hormuz closure is destroying oil demand daily, the longer-term impact of the Iran war may paradoxically boost oil consumption through energy security concerns and a less efficient global energy system.

headlines.headline: South Korea and Vietnam Leaders Meet as Hanoi Seeks High-Tech Investment
headlines.summary: Vietnams leader hosts South Korean President Lee Jae Myung for their second meeting in under a year, as Hanoi seeks greater support for its high-tech ambitions from its top foreign investor.

headlines.headline: Cyprus President Calls for EU Playbook on Member Defense
headlines.summary: Cyprus President Nikos Christodoulides urged EU leaders to prepare a clear playbook for helping member states under attack, highlighting the blocs need for stronger collective defense mechanisms amid growing global threats.

headlines.headline: Iran sustains covert oil exports through Hormuz despite US blockade
headlines.summary: Iran continues crude exports using dark tanker operations, signal spoofing and ship-to-ship transfers to evade the US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: IRGC says at peak readiness to respond to any renewed aggression
headlines.summary: Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps vows to deliver crushing blows to remaining enemy assets in the region as ceasefire talks continue.

headlines.headline: Italy summons Russian ambassador after TV host insults Meloni
headlines.summary: Rome protests after a Russian state TV presenter launched a stream of insults at Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni on air, escalating diplomatic tensions.

headlines.headline: Iran accuses Pakistan of double game in US talks mediation
headlines.summary: Iranian state media targets Pakistan Army Chief Asim Munir, alleging Islamabad is playing both sides in the US-Iran ceasefire negotiations.

headlines.headline: UK inflation jumps to 3.3% as Iran war drives fuel price spike
headlines.summary: British inflation rose sharply as the Iran war and Hormuz closure cause significant increases in fuel prices and cost of living pressures.

headlines.headline: Chinese Navy Passes by Japanese Islands After Pacific Training
headlines.summary: A Chinese naval group including a destroyer passed through a waterway between Okinawa islands as it returned home after far-seas training, following a Japanese transit through the Taiwan Strait.

headlines.headline: Pope Visits Notorious Prison in Equatorial Guinea
headlines.summary: Pope Leo XIV visited one of Equatorial Guinea's notorious prisons, drawing attention to human rights issues following recent US migrant deportations to the country.

headlines.headline: Kevin Warsh Makes Case for Smaller Fed Balance Sheet
headlines.summary: Federal Reserve chair nominee Kevin Warsh told a Senate panel he would work to reduce the Fed's bond holdings, a long-term project that would reshape the central bank's approach to monetary policy.

headlines.headline: Russia Has Taken 1,700 Square Kilometers of Ukraine This Year, Top General Says
headlines.summary: Russian forces have captured 1,700 sq km of Ukrainian territory in 2026 and are advancing on the Donbas fortress belt. The gains come as Ukraine struggles to secure EU loan funding amid the Druzhba pipeline dispute.

headlines.headline: NATO Criticises Russian and Chinese Nuclear Policies, Urges US Cooperation
headlines.summary: NATO criticized Russian and Chinese nuclear arms policies and called on both countries to work with the United States to establish greater stability and transparency at an upcoming international conference.

headlines.headline: US Indo-Pacific Commander Warns Taiwan on Defense Budget Delay
headlines.summary: The head of US Indo-Pacific Command stressed Taiwan must pass its stalled defense budget, saying the US "cant want Taiwans defense more than they want it themselves" amid rising Chinese military activity.

headlines.headline: Dutch Intelligence: Europe Must Focus on Own Security as Global Threats Mount
headlines.summary: The Dutch MIVD warned Europe must take greater responsibility for its own security, citing pressure on Western alliances and Chinas support for Russias war in Ukraine. The call comes amid US-Iran tensions straining transatlantic ties.

headlines.headline: China may expand role in Saudi ties after Iran war concludes
headlines.summary: SCMP analysis examines how Riyadh may shift toward self-reliance and diversifying partnerships as the Iran conflict reshapes regional dynamics.

headlines.headline: Pakistan blames US blockade, not Iranian divisions, for stalled peace talks
headlines.summary: Nikkei Asia reports Pakistani officials attribute the deadlock in ceasefire negotiations to the US naval blockade of Iranian ports in the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian drones strike Samara Oblast in overnight attacks
headlines.summary: Reports and social media footage show damage to residential buildings in Russia's Samara region as Ukrainian drones continue targeting Russian territory.

headlines.headline: IEA chief warns Iran war creating worst energy crisis in history
headlines.summary: International Energy Agency head says combined disruptions from Strait of Hormuz closure and Ukraine war fallout are squeezing global oil and gas supplies.

headlines.headline: US halts dollar shipments to Iraq to pressure Iran-backed militias
headlines.summary: Trump administration freezes US dollar deliveries to Iraq and suspends security cooperation programs in effort to curb Iranian influence, according to Wall Street Journal.

headlines.headline: Europe Seeks Role in Iran War but Remains on Sidelines
headlines.summary: A British-French plan to secure the Strait of Hormuz would give Europe a role in the conflict, but Tehran and Washington continue to call the shots, leaving European powers struggling for influence.

headlines.headline: Mideast Crises Divide Europe Over Fuel Costs and Israel Policy
headlines.summary: European diplomats meeting in Luxembourg are grappling with rising fuel costs from the Iran conflict and policy divisions over Israel, as the war in Ukraine and Middle East tensions strain EU unity.

headlines.headline: Trump Cuba Threats Revive Exile Hopes and Fears Over Property Claims
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump's threat of military intervention in Cuba is raising hopes among Cuban Americans for regime change, while reigniting complex debates over property claims seized after the Castro revolution.

headlines.headline: Uncertainty Hangs Over US-Iran Talks as Ceasefire Deadline Looms
headlines.summary: President Trump says he does not want to extend the ceasefire with Iran as the clock ticks down on the deadline, with diplomatic efforts to end the seven-week war facing significant obstacles.

headlines.headline: World's largest condom maker Karex to raise prices up to 30% due to Iran war
headlines.summary: Malaysia-based Karex, which produces over 5 billion condoms annually for brands like Durex and Trojan, announced price hikes of up to 30% as the Iran conflict disrupts supplies of oil-derived materials including ammonia and silicone lubricants.

headlines.headline: Voting rights groups sue Trump administration over state voter roll demands
headlines.summary: Voting rights activists filed a lawsuit accusing the Trump administration of laying groundwork for voter purges that could illegally remove eligible voters ahead of contested US midterm elections in November.

headlines.headline: Florida launches criminal probe into OpenAI over deadly university shooting
headlines.summary: Florida's attorney general announced a criminal investigation into OpenAI and ChatGPT regarding a deadly shooting at Florida State University that killed two people last year.

headlines.headline: Virginia voters approve Democratic-backed congressional map
headlines.summary: Virginia voters approved a new congressional map that could help Democrats flip up to four Republican seats in the US House, further dimming Trump's party chances of retaining their narrow majority in November.

headlines.headline: New testimony raises pressure on UK PM Starmer over US envoy appointment
headlines.summary: A former top foreign ministry official said he faced "constant pressure" from Prime Minister Keir Starmer's office to speed up the appointment of his pick as ambassador to the US, deepening a controversy threatening the British leader.

headlines.headline: Denmark Chooses European SAMP/T Over US Patriot for Air Defense
headlines.summary: Denmark selected the Franco-Italian SAMP/T NG air defense system over the American Patriot, marking the first major European defection from US defense systems amid Transatlantic tensions.

headlines.headline: Trump Says Iran Keeping Hormuz Closed to Save Face
headlines.summary: President Trump accused Iran of keeping the Strait of Hormuz closed to save face, hours after announcing an indefinite ceasefire extension while maintaining the naval blockade.

headlines.headline: Israel Jails Soldiers for Vandalizing Jesus Statue in Lebanon
headlines.summary: Two Israeli soldiers received military prison sentences for vandalizing a Jesus statue in Lebanon, highlighting ongoing discipline issues amid the broader regional conflict.

headlines.headline: Germany Faces EU Resistance Over AI Deregulation Push
headlines.summary: Berlin is encountering opposition from a group of 10 EU member states in its push to weaken AI regulations, complicating Europes tech competitiveness debate.

headlines.headline: EU Launches Emergency Energy Plan to Counter Iran Crisis
headlines.summary: The European Union moved to unlock emergency funding for member states grappling with energy disruptions from the Iran war and Hormuz closure.

headlines.headline: Taiwan President Cancels Africa Trip After China Pressures Overflight Denials
headlines.summary: Taiwan President Lai Ching-te cancelled his visit to Eswatini after China pressured three African countries to revoke overflight permissions for his aircraft, in what Taipei called a clear act of diplomatic coercion.

headlines.headline: Global Stocks Hit Record Highs Despite Ongoing Iran Conflict
headlines.summary: Global stocks have recouped all losses from the Iran war and hit fresh record highs, with the MSCI World Index now trading 2% above pre-war levels as investors refocus on the AI boom and unwind geopolitical risk hedges.

headlines.headline: China Intensifies Targeted Pressure on Japan Amid Taiwan Strait Tensions
headlines.summary: China is escalating pressure on Japan beyond diplomatic disputes, with military sources citing real-time "targeting within strike range" against Japanese vessels after a Japanese destroyer transited the Taiwan Strait.

headlines.headline: US Trade Rep Tells Mexican Companies Tariffs Will Stay
headlines.summary: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer has told Mexican auto and steel industries not to expect tariff removals in the USMCA renegotiation, signaling Trump administration tariffs are here to stay for key sectors.

headlines.headline: With Tariffs Stalled, Trump China Policy Drifts
headlines.summary: As Trumps tariff campaign against China has lost momentum, his broader China policy appears adrift, with no clear strategy for addressing the strategic competition beyond trade measures.

headlines.headline: Trump Says He Does Not Want to Extend Iran Ceasefire
headlines.summary: President Trump told CNBC he did not want to extend the ceasefire with Iran, stating the US was in a strong negotiating position and would achieve a "great deal." The comments come as a two-week ceasefire was set to expire.

headlines.headline: US to Indefinitely Extend Iran Ceasefire, Unclear If Iran Agrees
headlines.summary: Trump announced an indefinite ceasefire extension while continuing the US Navy blockade of Iranian ports—a move Iran considers an act of war. It remains unclear whether Iran or Israel have agreed to the extension.

headlines.headline: Canada Pushes Military Independence in Arctic Amid US Tensions
headlines.summary: Canada's military is working to demonstrate it can defend its Arctic territory alone, as Trump's threats to make Canada the 51st state push Ottawa to rethink its relationship with its southern neighbor.

headlines.headline: Middle East Scrambles for Alternative Oil Export Routes as Hormuz Remains Blocked
headlines.summary: The Iran conflict has disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, exposing the Middle East's limited alternatives for exporting hydrocarbons. Gulf states are racing to find alternative pipeline routes.

headlines.headline: Japan ends decades of arms export restrictions in major defense policy shift
headlines.summary: Japan unveiled its biggest overhaul of defense export rules in decades, scrapping restrictions on overseas arms sales and opening the way for exports of warships, missiles and other weapons as it faces rising threats from China.

headlines.headline: Saudi Arabia has strong financial buffers to confront Iran war impact, IMF says
headlines.summary: IMF Middle East director Jihad Azour described the situation as a "multidimensional shock" but noted Saudi Arabia maintains robust fiscal reserves to weather the economic fallout from regional conflict.

headlines.headline: Iran economy shrinking 6.1% with $270 billion in war losses, IMF projects
headlines.summary: Iran's economy is contracting sharply under the weight of the ongoing conflict, with the IMF projecting a 6.1% contraction this year. Total war damage is estimated at $270 billion in direct and indirect losses since February 28.

headlines.headline: Zelenskyy says Iran war strengthens Russia but Moscow faces $100 billion deficit
headlines.summary: Ukrainian President noted that Russia is struggling with a massive budget deficit that cannot be resolved by short-term geopolitical gains from higher oil prices, questioning where China will stand in the evolving crisis.

headlines.headline: Pakistan PM Thanks Trump for Extending Iran Ceasefire
headlines.summary: Pakistan Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif expressed gratitude to President Trump for extending the US-Iran ceasefire at Pakistan's request, allowing diplomatic efforts to continue as the original deadline approached.

headlines.headline: US Unilaterally Extends Ceasefire With Iran
headlines.summary: President Trump announced an extension of the ceasefire with Iran, though it appeared unilateral and remained unclear whether Iran or Israel would accept the extension as diplomatic efforts continued.

headlines.headline: NATO Intercepts Russian Bombers Over Baltic Sea
headlines.summary: NATO fighter jets scrambled to intercept Russian strategic bombers and fighter jets flying over the Baltic Sea, a show of air power on the alliance's eastern flank amid ongoing tensions with Moscow.

headlines.headline: UK Cyber Chief Warns Russia, Iran and China Behind Most Serious Attacks
headlines.summary: The head of the UK's National Cyber Security Centre warned that hostile nations including Russia, Iran and China are responsible for the most serious cyberattacks against Britain.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Mass Shooting Reignites Gun Law Debate
headlines.summary: A deadly shooting in Kyiv has fueled calls to loosen restrictions on handgun ownership for self-defense in Ukraine, a country already scarred by years of war against Russia.

headlines.headline: Japan overhauls defense export rules, opening door to global arms market
headlines.summary: Japan announced its biggest overhaul of defense export rules in decades, scrapping restrictions on overseas arms sales and opening the way for exports of warships, missiles and other weapons to allies.

headlines.headline: Trump administration sues Southern Poverty Law Center on fraud charges
headlines.summary: The US Justice Department filed fraud charges against the Southern Poverty Law Center, a major civil rights watchdog, marking an escalation in the administration's tensions with advocacy organizations.

headlines.headline: Russia warns of threats to its citizens in Moldova's Transdniestria region
headlines.summary: Moscow said Russians in Moldova's breakaway Transdniestria region are under threat and vowed to take all steps to protect them, raising concerns about potential escalation in the region.

headlines.headline: Bank of England to hold interest rates through 2026 despite inflation threat
headlines.summary: The Bank of England will keep rates steady next week and likely through the rest of the year, according to a Reuters poll, as policymakers balance inflation risks from the Iran conflict against economic weakness.

headlines.headline: Iran Threatens Immediate Strikes if US Attacks as Ceasefire Extended
headlines.summary: Iran's military stated its forces are 'ready and on the trigger' to strike pre-designated targets if the US launches any new attacks, as Trump extended the ceasefire deadline to allow time for Iran to submit a proposal.

headlines.headline: Lufthansa Cuts 20,000 Summer Flights to Reduce Fuel Costs Amid Iran Conflict
headlines.summary: Germany's Lufthansa Group announced it will trim its summer schedule by 20,000 flights to cut fuel costs, citing the ongoing Iran conflict's impact on aviation operations.

headlines.headline: Pentagon Seeks Funds for Golden Dome, Drones and AI in Record $1.5T Budget
headlines.summary: The Pentagon unveiled its largest-ever budget request, prioritizing the Golden Dome missile defense system, drones, and AI capabilities as key defense investments.

headlines.headline: Trump Extends Iran Ceasefire as Pakistan Talks Remain Uncertain
headlines.summary: Trump announced a ceasefire extension with Iran despite reports that dozens of vessels have evaded the US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. The fate of Vance's Pakistan negotiations remains unclear.

headlines.headline: US Offers Afghans in Qatar Choice Between Taliban-Ruled Afghanistan or DR Congo
headlines.summary: The US is pressuring former Afghan allies stuck in Qatar to choose between returning to Taliban-controlled Afghanistan or resettling in the war-torn Democratic Republic of Congo.

headlines.headline: IEA Calls Iran War Biggest Energy Crisis in History
headlines.summary: The International Energy Agency warns that the ongoing conflict has created the largest energy disruption ever recorded, with significant implications for global supply chains.

headlines.headline: Biofuels Make Comeback as Iran War Drives Oil Prices Higher
headlines.summary: Rising fossil fuel prices from the Middle East conflict are making biofuels economically viable again, spurring renewed investment in alternative energy sources.

headlines.headline: Russia to Halt Kazakhstan Oil Exports to Germany Via Druzhba Pipeline
headlines.summary: Russia is set to stop oil exports from Kazakhstan to Germany via the Druzhba pipeline starting May 1, adding another layer to Europe energy supply concerns.

headlines.headline: Prolonged Energy Crisis Could Strain India Trade and Fiscal Accounts
headlines.summary: Moody warns that extended energy supply disruptions could widen Indias trade deficit and strain the fiscal position of the worlds fastest-growing major economy.

headlines.headline: Trump Extends Iran Ceasefire at Pakistan Request
headlines.summary: President Trump announced an extension of the US-Iran ceasefire following a request from Pakistan, which is mediating peace talks. The US naval blockade of Iranian ports continues while negotiations proceed.

headlines.headline: Two CIA Officers Killed in Mexico Counter-Narcotics Operation
headlines.summary: Two Americans killed in a car crash after a Mexican-led drug lab operation were CIA officers, raising questions about US intelligence involvement in Mexico. Mexican President Sheinbaum questioned why US officials were operating in the country.

headlines.headline: Japan Lifts Post-WWII Ban on Lethal Weapons Exports
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi reversed decades of pacifist policy, allowing Japanese companies to sell weapons to 17 countries amid rising regional threats from China and uncertainty over US commitments.

headlines.headline: Bulgaria Election: Pro-Russian Candidate Heads to Victory
headlines.summary: Rumen Radev, a Kremlin-friendly former president, is headed to victory in Bulgarias election, giving Moscow a new foothold inside the EU after Viktor Orbans defeat in Hungary.

headlines.headline: EU Court Rules Hungary LGBTQ Law Violates Human Rights
headlines.summary: The European Court of Justice ruled that Viktor Orbans government LGBTQ restrictions breach EU law on multiple levels, marking a significant legal setback for Hungary.

headlines.headline: Italy Summons Russian Ambassador After State TV Host Insults PM Meloni
headlines.summary: Italy formally protested after Russian propagandist Vladimir Solovyov used vulgar insults against Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni on state TV, calling her a "fascist" and "certified idiot." Foreign Minister Tajani summoned Ambassador Paramonov to register official protest.

headlines.headline: Japan Lifts Ban on Lethal Weapons Exports in Historic Defense Policy Shift
headlines.summary: Japan revised its Three Principles on Transfer of Defense Equipment, allowing exports of warships, missiles, and air defense systems to 17 partner nations. PAC-3 MSE interceptors for Patriot systems and Type 11/03 air defense systems now available for export amid global shortages.

headlines.headline: Peter Magyar to Become Hungary PM on May 9 After Landslide Victory
headlines.summary: Tisza Party leader Peter Magyar will be elected prime minister on May 9 after winning 141 of 199 parliamentary seats. Viktor Orban acknowledged defeat following record 79.56% voter turnout in Hungary's April 12 elections.

headlines.headline: Central and Eastern Europe Faces Energy Shock Stress Test From Hormuz Crisis
headlines.summary: The Iran war-driven oil shock hits CEE3 and Turkey at a fragile moment with thin fiscal space and newly restored disinflation. Analysis shows Czech Republic best positioned to weather shock; Turkey most vulnerable due to energy sensitivity and limited external backstops.

headlines.headline: Russian Interior Minister Arrives in North Korea for Talks
headlines.summary: Russian Interior Minister Vladimir Kolokoltsev arrived in North Korea for talks on cooperation between the two allies, deepening ties between Moscow and Pyongyang amid the Ukraine war.

headlines.headline: Trump Approval on Economy Falls in AP-NORC Poll
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump's approval rating on the economy has slumped over the past month as the Iran war and cost of living concerns weigh on public sentiment.

headlines.headline: World Bank Climate Financing at Risk as Trump Seeks to Kill Plans
headlines.summary: The Trump administration is moving to end the World Bank's climate financing initiatives while world leaders fight to preserve them, highlighting a growing transatlantic divide.

headlines.headline: Trump Extends Iran Ceasefire as Peace Talks Remain Uncertain
headlines.summary: President Trump said he would extend the ceasefire with Iran at Pakistan's request, but warned the US military remains ready if talks fail to materialize.

headlines.headline: US-Iran Peace Talks Uncertain as Ceasefire Deadline Looms
headlines.summary: The United States and Iran have signaled they will hold new ceasefire talks in Islamabad, but uncertainty is growing about whether negotiations will proceed as the two-week ceasefire deadline approaches.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo Warns Humanity Future Compromised by Wars
headlines.summary: In a forceful speech during his Africa tour, Pope Leo warned that humanitys future is at risk due to ongoing wars and a breakdown of international law.

headlines.headline: China Convenes Emergency Energy Security Meeting as Hormuz Disruption Threatens Oil Supply
headlines.summary: Premier Li Qiang led a State Council session on energy security as China confronts the impact of the Strait of Hormuz crisis. Xi Jinping called for the strait to remain open in his first public statement on the blocked waterway.

headlines.headline: Russia-North Korea Open First Land Bridge Between Countries
headlines.summary: Russia and North Korea held a ceremony for a new 5-kilometer bridge linking the countries by land for the first time. The bridge for cars and trucks marks a deepening of ties between Moscow and Pyongyang.

headlines.headline: Estonia Rejects Ukraine Warnings of Russian Threat to Baltics
headlines.summary: Estonian parliament Foreign Affairs Committee chairman Marko Mihkelson pushed back against Ukrainian warnings that the Baltics could be Russias next target, saying such narratives do not help Ukraine.

headlines.headline: Hezbollah Fires Rockets at Israeli Troops in Southern Lebanon
headlines.summary: The Israeli military said Hezbollah fired rockets toward its troops in a blatant violation of the ceasefire agreement, as the US-Iran truce deadline nears. Trump accused Iran of violating the ceasefire multiple times.

headlines.headline: Russia Destroys Mass Graves Near Mariupol, Satellite Images Suggest
headlines.summary: Satellite imagery indicates Russia has destroyed mass graves near Mariupol used to bury residents who died during the 2022 siege. Ukraines Center for the Study of Occupation called it evidence of attempted cover-up.

headlines.headline: US and Allies Launch Major Combat Drills in Philippines as Japan Joins for First Time
headlines.summary: Over 17,000 US and Filipino troops began the annual Balikatan exercises, with Japan's Self-Defense Forces participating fully for the first time. The drills include live-fire exercises near the South China Sea and Taiwan Strait, signaling strengthened deterrence against Chinese aggression despite US focus on the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Japan Ends Decades-Long Ban on Lethal Weapons Exports in Major Policy Shift
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's Cabinet approved scrapping Japan's postwar ban on lethal weapons exports, allowing sales of warships and missiles to 17 partner countries. The historic shift aims to bolster Japan's defense industry amid regional security concerns, drawing criticism from China.

headlines.headline: Trump's Fed Chair Nominee Kevin Warsh Faces Senate Grilling on Independence and Wealth
headlines.summary: Kevin Warsh testified before the Senate Banking Committee, pledging to run an independent Federal Reserve while facing intense Democratic scrutiny over his $131-209 million in assets and ties to hedge fund billionaire Stanley Druckenmiller. Elizabeth Warren called him 'uniquely ill-suited' for the role.

headlines.headline: US Army Tests New Arctic Vehicles and Drones in Alaska to Counter Russia and China
headlines.summary: CNN gained exclusive access to the 11th Airborne 'Arctic Angels' in northern Alaska as they test next-generation cold-weather vehicles and combat drones. The preparations reflect growing US concern over Russian and Chinese activity in the increasingly strategic Arctic region.

headlines.headline: NATO Fighter Jets Intercept Russian Bombers Over Baltic Sea
headlines.summary: French Rafale fighters deployed from Lithuania intercepted Russian bombers and fighter jets flying over the Baltic Sea, marking another escalation in NATO-Russia tensions.

headlines.headline: Marine Fuel Industry Scrambles for Oil Amid Iran War Disruptions
headlines.summary: Marine fuel providers face tightening supply of heavy sweet crude as the Iran war disrupts Middle East imports, with refiners competing for the same limited supplies.

headlines.headline: EU expands Iran sanctions to include Hormuz blockade officials
headlines.summary: EU countries agreed to widen sanctions against Iran to target those responsible for blocking the Strait of Hormuz, according to EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas. The move escalates European pressure on Tehran amid the ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: US confirms no missile defense system moved from South Korea amid Iran war
headlines.summary: A US general denied reports that a key missile defense system was relocated from South Korea to the Middle East for the Iran conflict, amid regional concerns about security gaps in the Indo-Pacific.

headlines.headline: Russia intensifies coastal mining in Crimea to prevent Ukrainian landings
headlines.summary: Russian forces are mining beaches in occupied Crimea to prevent Ukrainian amphibious operations, according to Ukraine Southern Defense Forces spokesperson. "The beaches will now become minefields," said Vladyslav Voloshyn.

headlines.headline: Hezbollah violates ceasefire with rocket attacks on Israeli troops in Lebanon
headlines.summary: The IDF accused Hezbollah of breaching the ceasefire by launching rockets at Israeli troops in southern Lebanon and sending a drone toward Israel, raising concerns about escalation despite the truce.

headlines.headline: Maersk warns ships to avoid Strait of Hormuz despite ceasefire
headlines.summary: Danish shipping giant Maersk advised vessels to continue avoiding the Strait of Hormuz despite a temporary ceasefire between the US and Iran, citing ongoing volatility and lack of clarity on safe passage.

headlines.headline: Trump Claims New Iran Deal Will Be Better Than 2015 Nuclear Agreement
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump said he believes the nuclear deal currently being negotiated with Iran will be better than the 2015 international agreement that curbed Tehran's nuclear program.

headlines.headline: Pentagon Seeks Billions for Drones and Air Defenses in 2027 Budget After Iran War
headlines.summary: U.S. military officials are calling for tens of billions of dollars in the next budget year for drones and air defense systems used extensively during the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Chinese Student Charged With Illegally Photographing U.S. Military Aircraft
headlines.summary: A college student from China has been charged with illegally taking photos of U.S. military planes in Nebraska during a multistate road trip, raising espionage concerns.

headlines.headline: Nigeria charges six with treason over alleged coup plot to overthrow Tinubu
headlines.summary: Nigeria filed 13 criminal charges against six former security officials accused of plotting the violent overthrow of President Bola Tinubu, marking a serious political crisis in Africa's most populous nation.

headlines.headline: RTX raises profit forecast on strong missile demand amid global tensions
headlines.summary: Defense contractor RTX lifted its 2026 profit and revenue forecasts, citing sustained demand for missile systems and weapons as geopolitical tensions drive defense spending worldwide.

headlines.headline: US Senators Call to Halt Lebanon Army Aid Over Failed Hezbollah Disarmament
headlines.summary: The chairmen of two powerful Senate committees are calling for Congress to halt US aid to the Lebanese army over its failure to disarm Hezbollah, as the Iran conflict continues to reshape regional politics.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Firm Speeds Up Drone Development Amid China Pressure
headlines.summary: A Taiwanese defense company is accelerating drone production as Taipei bolsters air defense systems and places increased emphasis on unmanned aerial vehicles to counter Chinese military threats.

headlines.headline: Zelensky Warns Sanctions Relief Fueling Russia War Ambitions
headlines.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky warned that sanctions relief is fueling Russian war ambitions as Ukraine steps up oil strikes against Russian infrastructure.

headlines.headline: Bulgaria Election: What Radevs Landslide Means for Ukraine Support
headlines.summary: The landslide victory of ex-President Rumen Radevs party in Bulgarias April 19 parliamentary election signals a cooling of Ukraine support rather than a full reversal, analysts say.

headlines.headline: Trump Invokes Defense Production Act for Oil, Coal and Energy Infrastructure
headlines.summary: President Trump invoked wartime authority under the Defense Production Act to push for more oil, gas and coal production as energy markets remain volatile amid the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Trump Warns "Lots of Bombs" If Iran Ceasefire Expires
headlines.summary: President Trump told PBS News that if the Iran ceasefire expires, "lots of bombs start going off." The warning comes as a US delegation prepares for talks in Islamabad, with the ceasefire set to end April 22.

headlines.headline: North Korea Fires Multiple Ballistic Missiles Into Sea
headlines.summary: North Korea launched multiple short-range ballistic missiles from Sinpo, flying about 140km toward eastern waters. The launches came days after the UN nuclear watchdog warned of "very serious" advances in Pyongyang's nuclear weapons program.

headlines.headline: No Agreement After 21 Hours of US-Iran Talks, Vance Says
headlines.summary: US Vice President JD Vance said there was no agreement after 21 hours of talks with Iran, with significant differences remaining on key topics. Iran's foreign ministry said there was no expectation of a deal in just one meeting.

headlines.headline: Chinese Humanoid Robot Beats Human Half-Marathon World Record
headlines.summary: A humanoid robot from Chinese smartphone maker Honor completed a half-marathon in 50 minutes 26 seconds, beating the human world record of 57 minutes. The race was held alongside a human half-marathon in Beijing.

headlines.headline: Four Contenders Vie to Lead Embattled UN as Global Crises Mount
headlines.summary: Chile's Michelle Bachelet, Argentina's Rafael Grossi, Costa Rica's Rebeca Grynspan, and Senegal's Macky Sall face hearings by 193 member states to succeed Antonio Guterres as UN Secretary-General. The organization faces unprecedented global instability, wars, and a budget crisis.

headlines.headline: US Labor Secretary Resigns After Abuse of Power Allegations
headlines.summary: Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer has left Trump's Cabinet following multiple allegations of abusing her position, marking another high-profile departure from the administration.

headlines.headline: Niger and Mali Accuse Neighbors of Sponsoring Terrorism at Senegal Forum
headlines.summary: At a security forum in Senegal, representatives from Niger and Mali alleged that neighboring countries are sponsoring terrorism, highlighting ongoing regional tensions after their break with ECOWAS.

headlines.headline: Italy's Meloni to Amend Controversial Migrant Repatriation Bonus
headlines.summary: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni announced her government is prepared to make changes to a disputed migrant repatriation bonus scheme that has drawn criticism.

headlines.headline: US Forces Board Sanctioned Oil Tanker in Indian Ocean
headlines.summary: The Pentagon confirmed US forces boarded an oil tanker previously sanctioned for smuggling Iranian crude oil in the Indian Ocean, as tensions continue with Tehran ahead of the ceasefire deadline.

headlines.headline: US seizes Iranian container ship Touska in international waters
headlines.summary: US forces boarded and seized the Iranian-flagged container ship Touska, which was reportedly carrying dual-use equipment. Iran condemned the seizure as a ceasefire violation as the truce deadline nears.

headlines.headline: Hungary opposition leader pledges to arrest Netanyahu if elected PM
headlines.summary: Peter Magyar, leader of Hungary opposition, says he will respect ICC obligations by detaining the Israeli leader and reverse Orbans decision to quit the ICC.

headlines.headline: Worlds largest condom maker to raise prices 30% due to Iran war shipping disruption
headlines.summary: Malaysian condom manufacturer Karex announced price hikes of up to 30% as freight costs surge and shipping delays hit supplies due to the Middle East conflict.

headlines.headline: Xi Jinping offers to work with Africa on Middle East conflict spillover
headlines.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping said China is willing to cooperate with African nations to address the economic and humanitarian impacts of the Middle East conflict.

headlines.headline: UK announces measures to delink electricity prices from global gas amid Mideast war
headlines.summary: Britain unveiled reforms aimed at weakening the connection between global gas prices and electricity costs, as Middle East conflict drives energy market volatility.

headlines.headline: EU Seeks US Jet Fuel and SAF to Counter Iran War Strain
headlines.summary: The European Commission announced guidance on airport slots, fuel supplies, and passenger rights as jet fuel shortages loom due to the Iran war.

headlines.headline: Vance to Depart for Iran Peace Talks in Pakistan
headlines.summary: Vice President JD Vance and top US officials are expected to travel to Islamabad for potential Iran peace negotiations as the ceasefire deadline approaches.

headlines.headline: US Navy Minesweepers Head Toward Middle East as Hormuz Clearance Operation Shapes Up
headlines.summary: Avenger-class mine hunters USS Chief and USS Pioneer have been tracked sailing westward from Japan as Trump announces an operation to clear Iranian mines from the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: Belarus Building Roads and Artillery Positions Near Ukraine Border, Zelensky Warns
headlines.summary: President Zelensky says Russia may try to draw Belarus deeper into the war as new military infrastructure appears along Ukraine's northern frontier.

headlines.headline: US Army Deploys Low-Cost Ukraine-Tested Drones to Counter Iranian Shahed Threat
headlines.summary: Merops interceptor drones, priced at ,000 per unit, offer a cost-effective defense against Iranian Shahed drones costing ,000-,000. The Army has already purchased 13,000 units.

headlines.headline: Pentagon Reaches Out to US Automakers for Weapons Production as Wars Drain Stocks
headlines.summary: Defense officials have contacted GM, Ford, GE Aerospace, and Oshkosh about using factory capacity for military gear as Iran and Ukraine conflicts deplete US munitions.

headlines.headline: Major Natural Gas Discovery Off Indonesia Could Triple Eni Production by 2028
headlines.summary: Eni announced a 141 billion cubic meter gas find off East Kalimantan that could boost Indonesia's energy production and reduce oil imports amid global supply concerns.

headlines.headline: Nigeria Charges Six With Treason Over Alleged Coup Plot
headlines.summary: Nigerian authorities say they foiled a plot to overthrow President Bola Tinubu and charged six people including a retired major general and a serving police inspector with terrorism and treason.

headlines.headline: South Africa Police Chief Charged in $21 Million Corruption Scandal
headlines.summary: National police commissioner Fannie Masemola faces charges over an unlawful contract, marking a major blow to law enforcement leadership in one of Africa's largest economies.

headlines.headline: Trump and Top Republicans Join Marathon Bible Reading Event
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump and conservative Christian supporters are participating in a marathon Bible reading event, highlighting the administration's outreach to evangelical voters.

headlines.headline: EU Court Rules Hungary LGBTQ Law Violates EU Law
headlines.summary: The European Court of Justice found that Hungary's legislation banning LGBTQ content for minors violates EU law, in a landmark ruling with implications for rule-of-law disputes.

headlines.headline: Bloomberg: Can China Escape the Japanification Trap?
headlines.summary: As China's economy slows, analysts debate whether Beijing can avoid the decades-long stagnation that plagued Japan, with implications for global growth.

headlines.headline: Xi Jinping Calls for Strait of Hormuz to Reopen
headlines.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping told Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman that the strategic waterway should remain open, reflecting Beijing's difficult balancing act between Iran and Gulf states during the conflict.

headlines.headline: Japan Issues Megaquake Advisory After 7.7 Magnitude Earthquake
headlines.summary: A powerful earthquake off northern Japan triggered tsunami alerts and a rare advisory warning of slightly elevated megaquake risk. Officials urged preparedness in 182 coastal towns.

headlines.headline: Chad Deploys 1,500 Troops to Haiti for UN Gang Suppression Force
headlines.summary: Chad will send two battalions to join the Kenya-led multinational force combating gang violence in Haiti, where armed groups control most of Port-au-Prince.

headlines.headline: Tim Cook Steps Down as Apple CEO, Hardware Chief John Ternus to Succeed Him
headlines.summary: After 15 years leading Apple, Tim Cook will become executive chairman on Sept. 1. Hardware engineering head John Ternus takes over as CEO as the company navigates the AI transition.

headlines.headline: Chinese Carrier Liaoning Transits Taiwan Strait for First Time Since December
headlines.summary: The Chinese aircraft carrier Liaoning sailed through the Taiwan Strait in a move that comes days after a Japanese destroyer made a similar passage on a historically sensitive date for China.

headlines.headline: IEA Chief: Iran War Creating Worst Energy Crisis in History
headlines.summary: The head of the International Energy Agency says the US-Iran conflict has created the biggest energy crisis ever, surpassing the combined effects of the 1973, 1979, and 2022 crises when combined with Russian gas supply disruptions.

headlines.headline: Russia Sees Growing Interest in Orlan Drones Amid Middle East Conflict
headlines.summary: Russian drone manufacturer STC reports increased foreign interest in Orlan-series UAVs at the DSA exhibition in Malaysia, citing the Middle East conflict and demand for cost-effective surveillance systems.

headlines.headline: Palantir Faces Backlash Over AI-Driven Military Doctrine
headlines.summary: The defense tech company sparked debate with a social media thread promoting AI-driven military deterrence, arguing Silicon Valley has an obligation to support national defense and that AI will define the next era of warfare.

headlines.headline: Oil Markets on Edge as Trump Signals No Ceasefire Extension Without Deal
headlines.summary: President Trump told Bloomberg the US-Iran ceasefire is highly unlikely to be extended without an agreement, adding the naval blockade outside the Strait of Hormuz will remain until a deal is signed.

headlines.headline: Myanmar Junta Leader Offers Peace Talks, Rebel Groups Reject
headlines.summary: Myanmar's new military-backed president Min Aung Hlaing proposed peace talks with opposition groups within 100 days, but the Karen National Union and Chin National Front rejected the offer, saying they have no plans to negotiate with an illegitimate administration.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo Visits Cameroon to Address Separatist Conflict
headlines.summary: Pope Leo XIV arrived in Cameroon for a four-nation Africa tour, with separatists declaring a three-day ceasefire to allow safe passage. The visit highlights one of the world's most neglected conflicts in the country's Anglophone regions, where thousands have died since 2017.

headlines.headline: Venezuela Opposition Leader Machado Plans Return, Urges Elections
headlines.summary: Nobel Peace Prize winner Maria Corina Machado said she expects to return to Venezuela by end of 2026 and is pressing for swift elections. She warned that delays risk civil unrest as Venezuelans expect major changes after Maduro's capture.

headlines.headline: UN Secretary-General Candidates Face Live Public Grillings in New York
headlines.summary: Four candidates vying to become the next UN secretary-general face televised hearings Tuesday and Wednesday, marking a rare public scrutiny process for the leadership of the crisis-wracked global organization.

headlines.headline: US Pushes for Iran Peace Talks as Ceasefire Deadline Looms
headlines.summary: Security remains tight in Pakistan ahead of hoped-for talks between US and Iranian delegations as a two-week ceasefire set to expire Wednesday, with Vance and top officials expected to arrive in Islamabad.

headlines.headline: Japan Overhauls Pacifist Defense Policy to Enter Global Arms Market
headlines.summary: The Japanese government has scrapped restrictions on overseas arms sales in a major shift from post-WWII pacifist policies, seeking to counter regional security threats from China and North Korea.

headlines.headline: Iran grants field commanders greater autonomy over Iraq militias amid war pressures
headlines.summary: Iran has given its commanders more power to direct militia operations in Iraq without central approval, a decentralization driven by the ongoing conflict with the US. This shift could lead to more unpredictable attacks and complicates diplomatic efforts.

headlines.headline: Seized Iranian ship Touska carried dual-use equipment, US sources say
headlines.summary: The Iranian-flagged container ship seized by US forces on Sunday was likely carrying equipment that could have both civilian and military applications. The seizure highlights continued enforcement of the US naval blockade despite ceasefire negotiations.

headlines.headline: Trump Board of Peace in talks with DP World on Gaza reconstruction
headlines.summary: Representatives of Trump's "Board of Peace" have held discussions with Dubai-owned DP World about rebuilding Gaza infrastructure. The talks signal early planning for post-war reconstruction even as ceasefire negotiations continue.

headlines.headline: Weak Indian monsoon threatens volatile crop prices amid Iran crisis
headlines.summary: A forecasted weak monsoon in India could drive agricultural price volatility at a time when global food markets are already stressed by the Iran conflict and Hormuz disruptions. The combination could hit Asian consumers particularly hard.

headlines.headline: US military veterans arrested on Capitol Hill protesting Iran war
headlines.summary: Dozens of US military veterans were arrested while protesting the Iran war at the Capitol. Anti-war groups say the conflict is deeply unpopular and becoming a political crisis for the Trump administration as ceasefire deadline approaches.

headlines.headline: Pakistan confident Iran will attend US peace talks
headlines.summary: A senior Pakistani official told Reuters that Pakistan is confident it can get Iran to attend talks with the United States, as diplomatic efforts intensify before the ceasefire deadline.

headlines.headline: Vice President Vance has not yet departed for Iran talks in Pakistan
headlines.summary: JD Vance remains in the US as prospects for a second round of negotiations with Iran remain unclear. Iran has condemned recent US naval actions as armed piracy.

headlines.headline: Board of Peace envoy says Gaza disarmament plan needs quick progress
headlines.summary: The lead envoy for Trumps Board of Peace expressed cautious optimism about a Gaza disarmament plan, but cautioned Hamas talks will not be easy and time is critical.

headlines.headline: Oil prices fall as markets bet on US-Iran peace talks proceeding
headlines.summary: Crude oil fell over $1 per barrel on expectations that US-Iran peace talks will take place this week, potentially reopening critical Middle East supply routes.

headlines.headline: China's Xi calls for Strait of Hormuz to remain open in talks with Saudi crown prince
headlines.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping held a phone call with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, calling for normal passage of ships through the Strait of Hormuz to be maintained as Beijing steps up diplomatic efforts to help end the Iran war.

headlines.headline: Trump denies Israel influenced his decision to attack Iran
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump stated that Israel did not persuade him to attack Iran, pushing back against reports that Benjamin Netanyahu influenced his decision and responding to criticism from right-wing commentators.

headlines.headline: UN secretary-general race heats up as candidates emerge to replace Guterres
headlines.summary: The 10th United Nations secretary-general will be elected this year for a term starting January 1, 2027. Multiple candidates are positioning themselves to succeed Antonio Guterres as the race gains momentum.

headlines.headline: Japan lifts decades-old ban on lethal arms exports in major policy shift
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi approved new rules expanding Japan's ability to export advanced defense equipment, marking a significant shift as Tokyo boosts defense spending and its arms industry.

headlines.headline: China's youth unemployment rises to 16.9% ahead of record graduation season
headlines.summary: China's official youth jobless rate climbed to 16.9% in March, with another 12.7 million graduates set to enter the workforce, deepening the employment crunch.

headlines.headline: China bets on coal conversion to secure energy amid Iran war disruption
headlines.summary: Far from its eastern metropolises, China is doubling down on coal as a core element of its energy and industrial security strategy, seeking to reduce vulnerability to supply disruptions from the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: Three ships including Iranian vessel attempt Hormuz transits
headlines.summary: Three vessels, including one Iranian-flagged ship and two others with unclear ownership, attempted to transit the Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing tensions and blockade concerns.

headlines.headline: US delays weapons deliveries to European allies as Iran war strains stockpiles
headlines.summary: The Pentagon has informed European countries including Baltic and Scandinavian states that previously contracted weapons deliveries will be delayed as the Iran conflict draws on US munitions stocks. The delays highlight how the war that began in February is stretching American military supplies.

headlines.headline: Rubio defends Maduro capture to wary Caribbean leaders
headlines.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio told Caribbean leaders that Venezuela is better off today after the Trump administration military operation to capture Nicolás Maduro. Caribbean Community bloc members raised concerns about the legality of the raid while Rubio promoted the Donroe Doctrine for US dominance in the Western Hemisphere.

headlines.headline: Oil prices jump 4% as Trump announces seizure of Iranian cargo ship
headlines.summary: Brent crude rose above $94 per barrel after President Trump said the US Navy intercepted an Iran-flagged vessel. Iran has closed the Strait of Hormuz again after a temporary reopening, threatening to target any ship that approaches the waterway critical to global oil supplies.

headlines.headline: British universities paid firm to spy on pro-Palestine students
headlines.summary: An Al Jazeera investigation reveals 12 elite UK universities paid Horus Security Consultancy, run by former military intelligence officials, to monitor student protesters including pro-Palestinian activists. The firm harvested social media data and conducted counter-terror assessments on academics.

headlines.headline: Kuwait declares force majeure on oil shipments as Hormuz blockade blocks exports
headlines.summary: Kuwait declared force majeure on crude and refined product shipments after the Strait of Hormuz blockade prevented vessels from entering the Gulf, hampering its ability to meet customer commitments.

headlines.headline: South Korea president rejects claim minister leaked US intel on North Korea
headlines.summary: President Lee Jae Myung dismissed as "absurd" allegations that his minister overseeing relations with Pyongyang revealed classified US intelligence on North Korean nuclear facilities.

headlines.headline: Russia values seized gold producer stake at $1.9 billion ahead of auction
headlines.summary: The Russian property agency valued a 67.2% stake in gold producer UGC at 140.4 billion roubles ahead of a May auction to sell the seized assets.

headlines.headline: UK stocks fall as Iran ceasefire uncertainty lifts oil prices
headlines.summary: Britain's main stock indexes dropped in broad-based losses as fears the US-Iran ceasefire could collapse weighed on investor sentiment.

headlines.headline: Erdogan vows tighter gun laws after deadly Turkish school shooting
headlines.summary: Turkey will tighten gun-ownership rules and increase penalties after rare deadly school shootings sparked national outrage.

headlines.headline: Israel Establishes "Yellow Line" in South Lebanon Despite Ceasefire
headlines.summary: Israel has created a "Yellow Line" buffer zone in southern Lebanon near the border, where its forces continue to operate despite a 10-day ceasefire with Hezbollah, raising concerns about long-term territorial control.

headlines.headline: Philippines-US Launch Largest Balikatan Exercises Near Taiwan
headlines.summary: The largest-ever Balikatan military exercises began between Philippine and US forces, including maritime strike drills on an island near Taiwan, as Washington seeks to reassure Asian allies amid the Iran crisis.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Launches 5% Leasing Program for Defense Industry
headlines.summary: Ukraine named three state banks to provide preferential equipment leasing to defense manufacturers at a 5% annual rate, aiming to boost domestic arms production capacity.

headlines.headline: UAE Thwarts Iran-Linked Terror Plot, Arrests Network Members
headlines.summary: UAE authorities dismantled an Iran-linked terror network that planned sabotage operations, recruited Emirati youth, and channeled funds to suspicious foreign entities.

headlines.headline: Turkey Defense R&D to Boost Other Sectors, Official Says
headlines.summary: Turkish defense industry R&D achievements could serve as a catalyst for other sectors, according to officials highlighting the spillover benefits of military technology investments.

headlines.headline: Gulf states fear US-Iran talks may cement Tehran's control over Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: Gulf Arab states worry that ongoing US-Iran negotiations may only achieve reopening the strategic Strait of Hormuz, falling short of broader de-escalation they deem essential for regional stability.

headlines.headline: Israel tightens grip on south Lebanon despite ceasefire
headlines.summary: Israel warned residents to stay out of a belt of territory at the Lebanese border, entrenching its control over southern Lebanon despite the ceasefire in the war with Hezbollah.

headlines.headline: Russia detains German woman in alleged Ukrainian-directed terror plot
headlines.summary: Russia's FSB security service detained a 57-year-old German woman with explosives, claiming it was a false-flag operation orchestrated by Ukrainian intelligence.

headlines.headline: Russia-friendly former president Rumen Radev sweeps Bulgaria election
headlines.summary: Rumen Radev, a pro-Russia former fighter pilot, won a landslide victory in Bulgaria's snap parliamentary election, potentially shifting the EU and NATO member closer to Moscow.

headlines.headline: China warns US-Philippines-Japan drills risk regional trust
headlines.summary: China criticized expanded military cooperation as US and Philippines began annual exercises with Japan participation. The drills include maritime strike operations near Taiwan.

headlines.headline: US-Philippines military exercises test real-world readiness
headlines.summary: Philippine and US forces launched annual exercises with maritime strike drills near Taiwan. Manila military chief said operations would test readiness under real-world conditions.

headlines.headline: Gulf stocks fall on renewed US-Iran tensions
headlines.summary: Gulf equities declined on fears the US-Iran ceasefire could unravel after the US seized an Iranian cargo vessel. Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remained largely suspended.

headlines.headline: Gold eases as investors await clarity on US-Iran talks
headlines.summary: Gold prices fell as the dollar strengthened, with markets watching whether US-Iran peace talks will proceed this week after renewed tensions over the weekend.

headlines.headline: Britain Sidelined in Carney-Backed Defense Bank Talks
headlines.summary: The UK Treasury has expressed little enthusiasm for a Mark Carney-backed initiative to provide lower-cost loans to finance armed forces, leaving Britain on the margins as allies push for defense infrastructure investment.

headlines.headline: EU Commission Jobs Reshuffle on Hold as Chiefs Stay Put
headlines.summary: The first serious round of movement in the upper ranks since Ursula von der Leyen took office has stalled, with key commissioners remaining in their posts longer than anticipated.

headlines.headline: Canadian Tourist Killed in Shooting at Mexican Pyramid Site
headlines.summary: A Canadian woman was killed and several others injured in a shooting at the Teotihuacan pyramids, a major tourist destination near Mexico City. The attacker later died by suicide.

headlines.headline: Turkey Jails Eight Over Deadly 2024 Cable Car Accident
headlines.summary: A Turkish court sentenced eight people to prison terms for a 2024 cable car accident that killed one person and injured multiple others at a popular tourist destination in southern Turkey.

headlines.headline: Israels Independence Day Marked Amid War Clouds
headlines.summary: Cities across Israel moved ahead with Independence Day celebrations despite the fragile ceasefires with Lebanon and Iran, though some municipalities opted for scaled-down community-based events.

headlines.headline: Japan Scraps Ban on Lethal Weapons Exports in Historic Policy Shift
headlines.summary: Japan has approved ending its ban on lethal weapons exports, marking the biggest change to its postwar pacifist policy as the country seeks to build up its arms industry and deepen defense cooperation with partners.
