headlines.headline: Cuba Confirms Talks With US, Seeks End to Energy Blockade
headlines.summary: A Cuban Foreign Ministry official confirmed a 'respectful and professional' exchange with US officials, as Cuba seeks an end to Trump's energy blockade amid ongoing tensions.

headlines.headline: UN Says US Iran War Spending Could Have Saved 87 Million Lives
headlines.summary: The head of the UN humanitarian agency expressed frustration that the $2 billion weekly cost of the Iran conflict comes amid major cuts to aid budgets, noting the funds could have saved millions globally.

headlines.headline: Gaza Needs $71 Billion to Rebuild After Devastating War, Study Shows
headlines.summary: A new study by the EU, UN and World Bank estimates Gaza needs $71.4 billion over the next decade to recover from two years of war that devastated most of the Palestinian territory.

headlines.headline: Russia Launches Deadliest Aerial Assault of 2026 on Ukraine
headlines.summary: Russia attacked Ukraine with nearly 700 drones and 19 ballistic missiles, killing 16 people including a 12-year-old child. The assault struck Kyiv, Odesa, Dnipro, and energy infrastructure in the southeast.

headlines.headline: US Seizes Iranian Cargo Ship in Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: US Marines seized an Iranian-flagged vessel in the Strait of Hormuz after the destroyer USS Spruance disabled its propulsion. Iran warned of retaliation, saying it will not participate in peace talks under threats.

headlines.headline: Largest US-Philippines Military Exercise Begins as China Warns Against Playing With Fire
headlines.summary: Over 17,000 troops from US, Australia, Philippines, Japan and other nations began the Balikatan exercises. Japan sent 1,400 troops for the first time, while China warned participating countries they are escalating tensions.

headlines.headline: Pro-Russian ex-president Radev wins Bulgaria snap elections
headlines.summary: Rumen Radevs Progressive Bulgaria won 44.6% of the vote, positioning the Kremlin-friendly party to govern alone and marking a victory for pro-Russian representation in the EU.

headlines.headline: Zelensky criticizes US envoys for visiting Moscow but not Kyiv
headlines.summary: President Zelensky called it disrespectful that US envoys Witkoff and Kushner have flown to Moscow multiple times but have not yet visited Kyiv, as peace talks with Russia remain stalled.

headlines.headline: Ukraine aims to develop domestic ballistic defense system within a year
headlines.summary: President Zelensky announced an ambitious timeline to develop a European anti-ballistic missile defense system with European partners, seeking to reduce reliance on US Patriot systems.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo XIV joins tradition of pontiffs sparring with world leaders
headlines.summary: The popes veiled rebukes of Trump over the Iran war place him in a millennia-spanning tradition of pontiffs clashing with political figures from Attila the Hun to Napoleon.

headlines.headline: New Zealand PM Luxon Survives Leadership Confidence Vote
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Christopher Luxon retained the backing of his National Party lawmakers after calling a confidence vote amid sliding poll numbers months before the general election. He left without taking questions from reporters.

headlines.headline: British Royals Mark Queen Elizabeth's 100th Birthday
headlines.summary: King Charles visited an exhibition dedicated to his late mother's fashion on what would have been Queen Elizabeth II's 100th birthday. The monarch would have turned 100 on Tuesday, with several royal events planned to mark the occasion.

headlines.headline: Developing Nations Seek Path Out of Permacrisis at IMF Meetings
headlines.summary: Policymakers from developing countries left IMF-World Bank meetings frustrated that successive external shocks are derailing efforts to tackle debt and deliver better lives for citizens struggling with food and fuel costs.

headlines.headline: European Stocks Fall on US-Iran Ceasefire Concerns
headlines.summary: European shares declined Monday as investors awaited progress on possible US-Iran talks ahead of the ceasefire expiry. Markets reassessed the durability of the two-week truce.

headlines.headline: China deploys warships for Pacific combat drills amid Japan tensions
headlines.summary: A Chinese naval task group led by a warship will sail through the Bashi Channel into the Western Pacific for combat drills, as the US, Philippines and Japan conduct major joint exercises nearby in a show of allied military cooperation.

headlines.headline: NATO fuel network strains European commercial aviation
headlines.summary: Airlines warn flights could be disrupted within weeks as Europe fuel system strains under competing military and commercial demand, with NATO priority fuel movements squeezing civilian jet fuel supplies.

headlines.headline: Europe convenes parallel Gaza summits as Board of Peace stalls
headlines.summary: European nations hosted two parallel meetings on Gaza reconstruction, seeking new avenues to work with the US as disarmament talks with Hamas make glacial progress and the strip faces an estimated $71 billion in reconstruction needs.

headlines.headline: Trump labor secretary resigns amid misconduct investigation
headlines.summary: Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer resigned after an internal watchdog probe into misconduct allegations, including an affair with a subordinate and drinking on the job. She becomes the third cabinet official to leave the Trump administration in recent months.

headlines.headline: Israeli Strikes Kill Five in Gaza as Hamas Clashes with Militia
headlines.summary: Israeli strikes killed at least five Palestinians in Gaza as Hamas fighters clashed with an Israeli-backed militia. The violence highlights the complex multi-front tensions in the enclave.

headlines.headline: European Defence Stocks Cool as Investors Reassess War Winners
headlines.summary: Investors have cooled on European defence stocks as profit-taking and stretched valuations collide with uncertainty over the future of warfare, with the Iran conflict highlighting the effectiveness of low-cost drones.

headlines.headline: Putins High-Tech Submarines Challenge NATO in Atlantic
headlines.summary: Russias advanced submarines are increasingly challenging NATO in the North Atlantic, with RAF Lossiemouth serving as the front line of defense against Russian incursions from the Arctic.

headlines.headline: Israeli Soldier Seen Striking Jesus Statue in Lebanon
headlines.summary: An Israeli soldier was photographed striking a statue of Jesus in Lebanon, sparking condemnation. Prime Minister Netanyahu said he was stunned and saddened by the incident.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Business Group Urges Politics-Free Trade with China
headlines.summary: The head of a top Taiwan business group called for keeping politics out of trade and tourism exchanges with Beijing, after China unveiled new incentives for the island.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian drone strike kills one at Russian Black Sea port
headlines.summary: A Ukrainian drone attack on Russia's Tuapse port sparked a fire and killed at least one person, marking the second attack on the facility in less than a week as Ukraine continues targeting Russian energy infrastructure.

headlines.headline: Australian bank flags $503 million impairment hit from Middle East volatility
headlines.summary: National Australia Bank expects credit impairment charges to double as the Iran war roils global financial markets, highlighting the economic fallout spreading beyond the Middle East.

headlines.headline: Six women win 2026 Goldman Environmental Prize
headlines.summary: The first all-women cohort of Goldman Prize winners hails from Colombia, Nigeria, Papua New Guinea, South Korea, the UK and the US, recognizing grassroots environmental activism.

headlines.headline: Trump says he's not under pressure over Iran war deal as peace talks remain uncertain
headlines.summary: Iran has balked at President Trump's demands for a peace deal amid the ongoing Strait of Hormuz standoff, with the ceasefire deadline approaching and uncertainty about whether Tehran will join talks in Pakistan.

headlines.headline: China recalculates Taiwan strategy as US-Iran crisis dominates global attention
headlines.summary: Beijing is intensifying diplomatic pressure on Taiwan ahead of 2028 elections while simultaneously positioning itself as a global peacemaker amid the Gulf crisis. China has escalated tensions with Japan over Taiwan comments and is pushing the narrative that a Kuomintang victory would avert conflict.

headlines.headline: Veterans arrested at US Capitol during anti-war protest against Iran conflict
headlines.summary: Dozens of veterans and military family members protesting the Iran war were arrested by US Capitol Police after occupying the Capitol building. The demonstration reflects growing domestic opposition to US military involvement in the Gulf.

headlines.headline: Hamas disarmament talks stalled as peace envoy admits negotiations difficult
headlines.summary: Board of Peace envoy Nickolay Mladenov acknowledged that Hamas disarmament negotiations have stalled after the group ignored his ultimatum. He warned an agreement must be reached within two weeks at most.

headlines.headline: US and Iran signal readiness for war as Pakistan ceasefire talks remain in limbo
headlines.summary: The White House confirmed JD Vance is prepared to fly back to Islamabad for another round of talks, but Tehran has not confirmed participation. Both sides are warning they are ready for continued conflict if the ceasefire extension fails.

headlines.headline: Iran Considers Peace Talks as Ceasefire Deadline Nears
headlines.summary: Iran is evaluating participation in US peace talks in Pakistan following moves to end a US blockade of Iranian ports. The two-week ceasefire is set to expire, with uncertainty growing over the fate of negotiations.

headlines.headline: Fitch Cuts Philippines Outlook to Negative on Energy Shock
headlines.summary: Credit ratings agency Fitch revised the Philippines outlook to negative from stable, citing medium-term growth risks from disrupted public investment and high exposure to the global energy crisis.

headlines.headline: EU Has Five Months of Jet Fuel Reserves, Dutch Government Says
headlines.summary: The Dutch government estimated the European Union has enough kerosene supply to last about five months, drawing on domestic production and strategic reserves amid global energy disruptions.

headlines.headline: Indian Rupee Falls as Markets Brace for Ceasefire Expiry
headlines.summary: The Indian rupee logged its steepest one-day fall in a week as wary investors braced for the end of the US-Iran ceasefire amid rising Middle East tensions.

headlines.headline: Iran signals no plans to join US peace talks as ceasefire deadline looms
headlines.summary: Iran says it has no plans to send negotiators to Islamabad for a new round of talks with the United States, citing US violations of the ceasefire including the naval blockade and seizure of an Iranian cargo ship. Less than 48 hours remain before the fragile ceasefire expires.

headlines.headline: EU hosts Gaza peace conference as Orbans defeat opens path for action on Israel
headlines.summary: More than 60 nations sent representatives to Brussels for talks on stability and peace in Gaza and the West Bank. Hungarys election defeat of Israel ally Viktor Orban removes a key veto that had blocked EU sanctions on Israeli settlers and other actions.

headlines.headline: Pro-Russian ex-president Radev wins Bulgaria election in landslide
headlines.summary: Rumen Radevs Kremlin-friendly party won 44.6% of the vote in Bulgarias snap parliamentary elections, potentially pushing the EU and NATO member closer to Moscows orbit. The victory represents a major shift in Bulgarian politics.

headlines.headline: Israel and Lebanon engage in direct negotiations amid Iran ceasefire
headlines.summary: A separate but intertwined conflict shows signs of progress as Israel and Lebanon engage in direct negotiations with a temporary ceasefire in place. The talks involve the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah.

headlines.headline: Bulgaria Enters Uncharted Territory as Pro-Russian Radev Wins Landslide Election
headlines.summary: Former President Rumen Radev's Progressive Bulgaria won 44.7% in Bulgaria's eighth parliamentary election in five years, securing the first absolute majority since 1997. The result raises questions about Bulgaria's EU and NATO alignment as Radev pursues dialogue with Russia.

headlines.headline: Turkish FM Fidan Slams Greek Cypriots and Israel for Alliance Against Muslim Countries
headlines.summary: At the Antalya Diplomacy Forum, Hakan Fidan criticized the alliance between Greek Cypriots and Israel, saying it undermines regional stability. He said Turkey seeks to "extinguish conflicts" while Israel forms partnerships against Muslim nations.

headlines.headline: French Conservatives Select Bruno Retailleau as 2027 Presidential Candidate
headlines.summary: Les Républicains party members selected party president Bruno Retailleau as their candidate for the 2027 French presidential election with 73.8% support. The 65-year-old described his candidacy as born from "duty" rather than ambition.

headlines.headline: Turkey to Train 200 Nigerian Special Forces Under New Defense Agreement
headlines.summary: Nigeria and Turkey signed a defense pact to help fight Nigeria's 17-year jihadist insurgency. Turkey will train 200 Nigerian special forces soldiers as part of the agreement.

headlines.headline: Iran Rejects Any Transfer of Enriched Uranium Stockpiles in Nuclear Talks
headlines.summary: Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson stated Monday that at no stage of current or previous negotiations has Iran agreed to transfer or dilute its enriched uranium stockpiles, signaling continued tension ahead of ceasefire deadline.

headlines.headline: Pro-Russian Former President Radev Wins Bulgarian Election in Landslide
headlines.summary: Rumen Radev, Bulgaria's Kremlin-friendly former president, has won Sunday's parliamentary election by a landslide, potentially pushing the EU and NATO member state closer to Moscow's orbit.

headlines.headline: China Deploys Warships for Pacific Drills as Regional Tensions Rise
headlines.summary: A Chinese naval task group will conduct combat drills in the Western Pacific, coinciding with joint US-Philippines-Japan military exercises that Beijing says risk eroding regional trust.

headlines.headline: Zelenskiy Says Druzhba Oil Pipeline to Be Restored by End of April
headlines.summary: Ukraine president said the damaged Druzhba pipeline carrying Russian oil to Eastern Europe will be operational again by the end of April.

headlines.headline: Kremlin Says Russian Oil Exports Cannot Be Ignored After US Sanctions Waiver
headlines.summary: The Kremlin commented on a US sanctions waiver for some Russian oil exports, saying Russia is a responsible global energy player whose volumes cannot be dismissed.

headlines.headline: Russia evacuates over 600 employees from Irans Bushehr nuclear plant
headlines.summary: Rosatom chief Likhachev confirms only 20 volunteers remain at Bushehr with 4 in Tehran as Russia completes evacuation of nuclear personnel from Iran amid ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: Trump says Iran deal to be signed tonight in Islamabad
headlines.summary: President Trump announced an Iran deal would be signed tonight in Islamabad. No Iranian response has been given to Trumps claim yet.

headlines.headline: EU lawmakers urge Norway to share windfall energy profits amid Mideast tensions
headlines.summary: Calls grow for Norway to share increased energy profits as the Iran conflict and fragile ceasefire push prices higher, boosting Norways export earnings.

headlines.headline: US to delay weapons deliveries to European countries due to Iran war
headlines.summary: Some European nations have been informed that select US weapons deliveries may be delayed as the Iran war continues to draw on American weapons stocks.

headlines.headline: Starlink outage hit Navy drone tests exposing Pentagon reliance on SpaceX
headlines.summary: During April 2025 Navy tests involving unmanned boats and drones, Starlink struggled to provide solid network connections, highlighting the Pentagons growing dependence on SpaceX.

headlines.headline: US-Iran Peace Talks Set to Resume as Ceasefire Deadline Looms
headlines.summary: Vice President JD Vance is expected to travel to Pakistan for the second round of peace negotiations, though Iran has not confirmed its participation as the two-week ceasefire nears its end.

headlines.headline: Trump Invokes Defense Production Act to Expand US Petroleum Output
headlines.summary: President Trump signed a memorandum calling for expanded domestic petroleum production, refining and logistics capacity, citing national defense needs amid the ongoing Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Apple Names John Ternus as CEO, Tim Cook Becomes Executive Chairman
headlines.summary: Apple appointed insider John Ternus as its next CEO, with Tim Cook transitioning to executive chairman in a major leadership shakeup at the worlds most valuable company.

headlines.headline: Trump Sends Mixed Signals on Iran War Path as Ceasefire Nears End
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump gave mixed messages on the path ahead for the U.S. war with Iran, with the ceasefire set to expire Wednesday and prospects for extension described as "highly unlikely."

headlines.headline: BOJ Likely to Hold Rates Steady Amid Middle East Uncertainty
headlines.summary: The Bank of Japan is likely to hold off raising interest rates next week as fading prospects of a near-term end to the Middle East war keep the country's economic and price outlook highly uncertain.

headlines.headline: Hungary Election Winner Peter Magyar Names Finance and Foreign Ministers
headlines.summary: Hungarian election winner Peter Magyar nominated key ministers including Andras Karman as finance minister and Anita Orban as foreign minister for his incoming government.

headlines.headline: South Korea-US tensions flare over North Korea intelligence leak
headlines.summary: Seoul's Unification Minister Chung Dong-young rejects accusations he leaked sensitive information about North Korea's nuclear facilities, straining alliance coordination on Pyongyang policy.

headlines.headline: China threatens retaliation against EU firms over cybersecurity plans
headlines.summary: Beijing warns of broad retaliation if Huawei, ZTE and other Chinese companies are penalized under proposed EU cybersecurity regulations, escalating tech tensions.

headlines.headline: UN reports 350,000 displaced and 177 children killed in Lebanon since March
headlines.summary: UN peacekeepers continue observing Israeli artillery fire in southern Lebanon as humanitarian crisis deepens with over 1 million displaced facing worsening conditions.

headlines.headline: EU to host Taliban officials for talks on deporting Afghans
headlines.summary: Taliban officials expected in Brussels for negotiations on returning Afghan migrants to their home country, marking significant diplomatic engagement with the unrecognized government.

headlines.headline: Romania plunges into political turmoil as Social Democrats move to topple PM
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan faces ouster after coalition partners accuse him of destroying their social base with austerity measures, deepening Europe's governance instability.

headlines.headline: Canadian PM Carney says economic ties with US are weakness that must be corrected
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney announced Canada must diversify trade relationships and reduce dependence on the US economy, signaling a major shift in Canadian economic policy amid tensions.

headlines.headline: New Zealand declares state of emergency as severe floods hit Wellington region
headlines.summary: Widespread flooding in Wellington has submerged vehicles, uprooted trees and triggered landslides. State of emergency declared as cleanup begins.

headlines.headline: Around 250 Rohingya refugees missing after boat capsizes in Andaman Sea
headlines.summary: A vessel carrying Rohingya refugees capsized in the Andaman Sea, with approximately 250 people reported missing in the latest tragedy involving the persecuted minority.

headlines.headline: At least 21 killed as bus crashes into Kashmir gorge
headlines.summary: An overcrowded bus skidded off a mountain road and fell into a gorge in Indian-administered Kashmir, killing at least 21 passengers and injuring dozens more.

headlines.headline: Oil Prices Surge 6% as US-Iran Ceasefire Falters, Stocks Retreat from Records
headlines.summary: Global oil prices jumped 6% on fears the US-Iran ceasefire may collapse after the US Navy seized an Iranian cargo ship. Wall Street stocks pulled back from record highs as investors assessed the geopolitical risk.

headlines.headline: Iran Vows Retaliation After US Seizes Cargo Ship, Peace Talks in Doubt
headlines.summary: Iran condemned the US Navy seizure of an Iranian-flagged tanker as armed piracy and vowed retaliation. The incident has cast doubt on whether the second round of peace talks in Islamabad will proceed.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Officer Deploys 343 Days on Front Line, Highlighting Manpower Crisis
headlines.summary: A Ukrainian infantry officer spent 343 consecutive days on the front line, believed to be one of the longest continuous combat deployments. The case underscores Kyivs growing manpower challenges after years of war.

headlines.headline: Poland and France Discuss Joint Nuclear Exercises as US Commitment Wavers
headlines.summary: Polish PM Donald Tusk and French President Macron discussed joint nuclear deterrence exercises. Poland is exploring deeper defense ties with France amid concerns about US commitment to European security.

headlines.headline: Iranian president voices deep mistrust of US ahead of Islamabad peace talks
headlines.summary: President Pezeshkian says historical mistrust of the US government remains as second round of Iran-US talks prepare to kick off in Pakistan. The comments come as the ceasefire deadline approaches.

headlines.headline: Saudi crown prince hosts Sudan army chief as .5B arms deal stalls
headlines.summary: MBS met with General Burhan in Jeddah amid reports a major Saudi-backed weapons deal faces delays. The visit highlights Saudi efforts to maintain influence in Sudans ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: US Navy warns sailors to secure social media from Iranian online threats
headlines.summary: The Navy issued security guidance to service members to protect electronic devices and accounts amid increased online threats linked to the Iran-Israel war.

headlines.headline: Pentagon deploys AI to vet China ties of university professors
headlines.summary: After watchdog criticism of understaffing, the Defense Department is turning to artificial intelligence to screen Pentagon-funded researchers for potential espionage ties to Beijing.

headlines.headline: African economies strain as Iran shuts Hormuz route again
headlines.summary: African nations face renewed cost pressures after Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz again, disrupting vital shipping routes and threatening fuel imports across the continent.

headlines.headline: Germany Summons Russian Ambassador Over Direct Threats
headlines.summary: Berlin summoned the Russian ambassador to condemn what it called "direct threats" against targets in Germany, escalating diplomatic tensions between the two countries. The move reflects growing friction between Russia and European nations.

headlines.headline: Israel Condemns Soldier's Desecration of Crucifix in Southern Lebanon
headlines.summary: A photo showing the desecration of a crucifix smashed by an Israeli soldier in a southern Lebanese Christian village drew widespread condemnation from Israeli leaders, the United States and church leaders. The incident has damaged Israel's support among evangelical Christians.

headlines.headline: US Air Force Seeks Better Infrared Jammers for Combat Rescue Helicopters After Iran Mission
headlines.summary: The US Air Force is seeking better infrared countermeasures for its HH-60W combat rescue helicopters after one took multiple hits from Iranian small-arms fire during a high-profile pilot rescue mission. The aircraft currently lacks a capable advanced infrared countermeasures system, significantly increasing the risk of infrared-guided missile engagement.

headlines.headline: New Zealand Declares State of Emergency as Record Rainfall Triggers Wellington Flash Floods
headlines.summary: New Zealand declared a state of emergency for Wellington after the city received 77mm of rain in less than an hour—its heaviest rainfall on record—triggering flash floods, landslides and evacuations. A search is underway for a man at a flooded property, with the Fire Service responding to 150 calls for help overnight.

headlines.headline: Europol Hackathon Identifies 45 More Ukrainian Children Forcibly Deported to Russia
headlines.summary: An international open-source investigation coordinated by Europol uncovered information on 45 children forcibly transferred or deported by Russia from occupied areas of Ukraine. The two-day operation produced leads on transportation routes, individuals involved, and facilities where children were taken.

headlines.headline: Norwegian F-35s Intercept Russian Il-38 Surveillance Aircraft Over Arctic
headlines.summary: Norway confirmed two F-35 fighters were scrambled from Evenes Air Station to intercept a Russian Il-38 maritime patrol aircraft approaching its airspace on April 13. The incident highlights continued Russian military activity near NATO's northern flank amid heightened tensions with the alliance.

headlines.headline: Iraq's Ruling Shia Bloc Races to Choose Prime Minister as US and Iran Watch Closely
headlines.summary: Iraq's Coordination Framework must nominate a prime minister by April 26 as the country balances ties between the US and Iran amid the ongoing conflict. IRGC Quds Force chief Ismail Qaani visited Baghdad to influence the selection, with incumbent PM al-Sudani facing a challenge from pro-Iran former PM Nouri al-Maliki.

headlines.headline: China Expresses Concern Over US Seizure of Iranian Tanker Touska
headlines.summary: China said it was concerned about the seizure of the Iranian-flagged tanker Touska by US forces, noting the vessel has frequently traveled between China and Iran. The incident adds another layer of complexity to the US-Iran standoff as Beijing navigates its energy and diplomatic interests in the region.

headlines.headline: Ugandan and Congolese Troops Rescue 200 Captives From IS-Affiliated Militants
headlines.summary: Ugandan and Congolese soldiers rescued at least 200 civilians in a raid on an Islamist militant camp in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo. The operation targeted IS-affiliated militants who have been conducting attacks in the volatile region bordering Uganda.

headlines.headline: India and South Korea Aim to Double Trade to $50 Billion by 2030 Amid Global Uncertainty
headlines.summary: India and South Korea agreed to nearly double bilateral trade to $50 billion by 2030 during South Korean President Lee Jae Myung's state visit to New Delhi. Both nations seek closer ties amid unpredictable relations with the US under President Trump, signing MoUs on shipbuilding, chips, and technology cooperation.

headlines.headline: Slovenia's Prime Minister Golob Fails to Form Government, Opening Door to Right-Wing Coalition
headlines.summary: Slovenia's caretaker Prime Minister Robert Golob admitted his party cannot form a coalition government after razor-thin election results. The failure opens the door for former three-time Premier Janez Janša and his right-wing SDS party to attempt forming a government.

headlines.headline: French Prosecutors Summon Elon Musk in Criminal Probe of X Platform Over Abuse Images and Deepfakes
headlines.summary: Elon Musk was summoned by French prosecutors for questioning as part of a widening criminal investigation into X and its AI chatbot Grok. The probe focuses on allegations of child sexual abuse material, deepfakes, and data extraction on the platform, further straining US-EU relations over Big Tech regulation.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu Condemns IDF Soldier Who Smashed Jesus Statue in Lebanon as Backlash Grows
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu condemned 'in the strongest terms' an IDF reservist who smashed a statue of Jesus in a southern Lebanese village, saying he was 'stunned and saddened.' The IDF confirmed the viral photo's authenticity and launched an investigation amid backlash from US conservatives and Christian communities.

headlines.headline: Israel's Mossad and Security Agencies Dismantle Iranian Terror Network in Azerbaijan
headlines.summary: Mossad, Shin Bet, and the IDF announced they dismantled a global Iranian terror network operating in Azerbaijan that was targeting Western sites. Top Iranian leaders of the network from Unit 4000 of the IRGC Intelligence were killed, dealing a blow to Tehran's overseas operations.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo Condemns 'Tyrants' Spending Billions on Wars During Africa Visit
headlines.summary: Pope Leo XIV criticized 'tyrants' who spend billions on wars during his visit to conflict-hit Cameroon, saying 'the world is being ravaged by a handful of tyrants.' The pontiff also criticized foreigners who exploit Africa's wealth for profit, days after a public spat with President Trump over the Iran war.

headlines.headline: Iran Executes Two Men Convicted of Links to Israel's Mossad Spy Agency
headlines.summary: Iran executed two men convicted of cooperating with Israel's Mossad intelligence service and planning attacks inside the country. The two, identified as Mohammad Masoum Shahi and Hamed Validi, were accused of belonging to a spy network and had received training abroad including in Iraq's Kurdistan region.

headlines.headline: Four Candidates Vie to Lead Embattled UN Amid Global Crises and Budget Woes
headlines.summary: Four candidates are competing to succeed Antonio Guterres as UN Secretary-General, facing unprecedented global instability, wars and a crushing budget crisis. Chile's Michelle Bachelet, Argentina's Rafael Grossi, Costa Rica's Rebeca Grynspan, and Senegal's Macky Sall will face grillings by 193 member states.

headlines.headline: US and Philippines Kick Off Major Combat Drills Despite Iran War Focus
headlines.summary: The United States and the Philippines kicked off one of their largest combat exercises Monday, demonstrating continued US military commitment to the Indo-Pacific region despite Washington's focus on the Iran war. The annual Balikatan exercises come amid rising tensions with China in the South China Sea.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo Criticizes Foreigners Exploiting Africa's Wealth During Cameroon Visit
headlines.summary: Pope Leo XIV criticized foreigners who 'in the name of profit, continue to lay their hands on the African continent to exploit and plunder it' during his visit to Bamenda, Cameroon. The pontiff addressed 20,000 worshippers in a city at the center of a brutal separatist rebellion that has killed at least 6,000 people.

headlines.headline: North Korea Tests Ballistic Missiles With Cluster Munition Warheads
headlines.summary: North Korea test-launched five Hwasong-11 Ra ballistic missiles armed with cluster bomb and fragmentation mine warheads, with Kim Jong Un and his daughter observing. The tests highlight Pyongyang's push to develop weapons capable of penetrating US and South Korean defenses, potentially inspired by cluster munitions use in the Iran war.

headlines.headline: Iran Rejects Second Round of US Peace Talks Citing 'Excessive Demands'
headlines.summary: Iran's state news agency IRNA denied participation in new peace talks with the US, citing Washington's 'excessive demands, unrealistic expectations' and the continued naval blockade. The diplomatic setback came as JD Vance and Jared Kushner were reportedly preparing to travel to Islamabad for negotiations.

headlines.headline: Oil Prices Surge 6% as US-Iran Standoff Blocks Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: Oil prices jumped more than 6% in early trading after Iran reversed its decision to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. US crude rose to $87.90 per barrel and Brent climbed to $95.64, wiping out much of Friday's 9% decline as renewed doubts emerged about when the vital waterway will fully reopen.

headlines.headline: Trump Says US Seized Iranian Cargo Ship After Blockade Breach Attempt
headlines.summary: President Trump said US forces seized the Iranian-flagged cargo vessel Touska after it ignored warnings and attempted to breach a US naval blockade. Iran's military called the seizure 'armed piracy' and vowed retaliation, as the fragile ceasefire set to expire Wednesday faces growing strain from mutual accusations of violations.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Deploys Robots to Replace Human Soldiers in Combat Assault Missions
headlines.summary: Ukraine's military is increasingly using robots to replace human soldiers in combat, including assault missions. The unmanned ground vehicles and drones allow Kyiv to preserve manpower while maintaining pressure on Russian forces, marking a significant evolution in modern warfare tactics.

headlines.headline: Ukraine PM Says IMF Agreed Raising Tax on Self-Employed Is 'Not Constructive'
headlines.summary: Ukrainian Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko said the IMF agreed during Washington talks that introducing VAT tax on self-employed individuals was 'not constructive' given Ukraine's wartime economic pressures. The issue has been contentious as Kyiv seeks to balance IMF reform demands with domestic political sensitivity.

headlines.headline: Gold Falls as Dollar Strengthens on Renewed US-Iran Tensions
headlines.summary: Gold prices fell as the dollar firmed amid renewed US-Iran tensions, with the Strait of Hormuz closure pushing oil prices higher and reviving inflation fears. The precious metal's decline reflects investor flight to the dollar as Middle East uncertainty persists.

headlines.headline: Global Leftists Rally in Barcelona to Counter Far-Right Resurgence
headlines.summary: Thousands of leftists gathered in Barcelona over the weekend to brainstorm ways to halt the rise of authoritarianism, discussing reforms from UN Security Council restructuring to billionaire taxation. The summit aims to build momentum against the far-right's electoral gains across Europe and the Americas.

headlines.headline: Analysis Questions Whether Strait of Hormuz Will Open Anytime Soon
headlines.summary: A consistent theme in global oil markets since the US-Israel attack on Iran has been that the Hormuz closure will be short-lived. However, with Iran reversing course on reopening and hardliners gaining influence in Tehran, analysts are questioning whether optimism about a rapid resolution is warranted.

headlines.headline: Pro-Russian Former President Radev Claims Victory in Bulgaria's Parliamentary Elections
headlines.summary: Russia-friendly former president Rumen Radev is on course to win Bulgaria's parliamentary elections with a potential outright majority, a result that could stabilize the country but shift the EU and NATO member's foreign policy orientation toward Moscow amid the ongoing geopolitical realignment.

headlines.headline: Bulgaria's Pro-Russian Radev Set for Landslide Win in Eighth Election in Five Years
headlines.summary: Exit polls put Rumen Radev's Progressive Bulgaria coalition at 37%, more than double the score of the closest runner-up. The former president, who has opposed EU sanctions on Russia, may need to form a coalition but could fundamentally alter Bulgaria's stance within NATO and the European Union.

headlines.headline: New York Times Reports Bulgarian Voters Back Radev's Pro-Russian Coalition
headlines.summary: With citizens yearning for prosperity enjoyed by other Europeans, Bulgaria held its eighth election in five years, with pro-Russian former President Rumen Radev's coalition poised to win. The outcome could end political deadlock but raise concerns about Bulgaria's commitment to Western alliances.

headlines.headline: Zelensky Condemns US Sanctions Waiver Giving Russia Billions for War Effort
headlines.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky condemned the easing of sanctions on Russian oil after the United States extended a waiver allowing Moscow to sell crude already loaded on vessels. Zelensky warned that Russia's shadow fleet of 110 tankers would bring billions to fund the war against Ukraine.

headlines.headline: The Guardian Live Coverage: US Officials Head to Pakistan as Hormuz Crisis Deepens
headlines.summary: The Guardian's live coverage tracks the deepening Middle East crisis as US officials prepare to travel to Pakistan for negotiations amid the Iran-US standoff. The Strait of Hormuz remains a critical flashpoint with global energy supplies at stake.

headlines.headline: Oil Price Plunge on US-Iran Peace Hopes Gives Indian Markets Some Breather
headlines.summary: The Indian rupee and government bonds will be comforted by a drop in oil prices even as shipping through the Strait of Hormuz remains at a standstill, days before a ceasefire between the United States and Iran is set to expire.

headlines.headline: Wall Street Set to Wade Deeper Into Earnings Season as Stocks Hit Record Highs
headlines.summary: Investors will look to a heavy week of US corporate results to further fuel a stunning rebound in the stock market, which has shaken off war-related concerns to reach record peaks despite ongoing Middle East tensions.

headlines.headline: US Officials Attend IMF Meetings in 'Parallel Reality' as World Grapples With Oil Crisis
headlines.summary: The IMF's spring meetings buzzed with talk of a 13 million barrel per day oil shortfall reshaping economies, rates and defense alliances, yet US officials treated it as a sideshow. The disconnect highlights how counting on US leadership to resolve crises is no longer the guarantee it had long been.

headlines.headline: Trump Issues Iran Ultimatum as Bulgaria Votes and Robots Race Ahead
headlines.summary: US President Donald Trump accused Iran of a 'total violation' of the ceasefire, threatened to wipe out Iran's bridges and power plants, and said his envoys will arrive in Pakistan on Monday for more talks. Meanwhile, Bulgaria's pro-Russian former president appears set for election victory.

headlines.headline: Man Kills 8 Children in Louisiana Mass Shooting, Deadliest US Attack in Two Years
headlines.summary: A gunman killed eight children aged 1 to 14 in domestic-related shootings at two homes in Louisiana before being shot dead by police. It was the deadliest mass shooting in the United States in more than two years, igniting debates over gun violence.

headlines.headline: Bulgaria's Pro-Russia Former President Radev Wins Landslide Election Victory
headlines.summary: Progress Bulgaria, the Kremlin-friendly coalition led by former President Rumen Radev, is on track to win 37-38% of the vote according to exit polls. The result could end years of weak coalition governments and shift the EU member's foreign policy orientation toward Moscow.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Carries Out First-Ever Shahed Interception Using Naval Drone Platform
headlines.summary: Ukraine's military carried out the first-ever interception of a Russian Shahed drone using a naval drone platform operated by the 412th Nemesis Brigade. The breakthrough demonstrates Kyiv's growing innovation in maritime defense capabilities against Russian aerial threats.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Strikes Russian Oil Refinery in Krasnodar Krai, Major Fire Reported
headlines.summary: Ukrainian drones struck the Tuapse Oil Refinery in southern Russia's Krasnodar Krai during an overnight attack, causing a major fire. Kyiv continues its campaign to disrupt Moscow's war funding by targeting Russian energy infrastructure.

headlines.headline: Memorial Day 2026: 174 IDF Soldiers and 79 Israeli Civilians Killed in Past Year
headlines.summary: Israel's Memorial Day 2026 commemorates 174 IDF soldiers and 79 Israeli civilians killed over the past year, a significant increase reflecting the ongoing multi-front war. The toll underscores the human cost of the Iran war and Gaza conflict.

headlines.headline: UK Police Investigate Iran Links to Arson Attacks on Jewish Sites in London
headlines.summary: British police are investigating possible Iranian links to a series of arson attacks on Jewish targets in London, with the UK chief rabbi warning of a 'sustained campaign of violence' against the Jewish community gathering momentum.

headlines.headline: US Military Strike Kills Three on Alleged Drug Boat in Caribbean
headlines.summary: The US military killed three people in a strike on a boat accused of ferrying drugs in the Caribbean Sea, continuing the Trump administration's campaign against 'narcoterrorism' that has now killed at least 181 people. Critics have questioned the legality of the boat strikes amid continued operations despite the Iran war.

headlines.headline: Restoring Oil Flows Through Hormuz Will Take Months or Years Even After War Ends
headlines.summary: Even if the guns fall silent, oil and gas flows through the Strait of Hormuz will take months—possibly years—to recover to pre-war levels. The stop-start shipping traffic underscores profound uncertainty hanging over the world's most critical energy chokepoint.

headlines.headline: US Navy Destroyer Disables Iranian Cargo Ship Breaking Blockade
headlines.summary: The USS Spruance fired on the Iranian-flagged M/V Touska after a six-hour standoff when the vessel attempted to reach Bandar Abbas in violation of the US naval blockade. Marines boarded the ship, which remains in US custody, while Iran vowed retaliation calling it 'armed piracy.'

headlines.headline: Ukraine Unveils New Koral Surface-to-Air Missile for Domestic Air Defense
headlines.summary: Ukraine displayed what appears to be the Koral medium-range surface-to-air missile during a defense industry exhibition, with an estimated range of 100km and anti-ballistic missile capability. The domestically developed SAM could help offset Ukraine's shortage of Western air defense systems.

headlines.headline: Pakistani Journalist Says Iran's Military Strength Delivered Strategic Results
headlines.summary: A senior Pakistani journalist argues Iran achieved strategic gains in its war with the US and Israel, including establishing control over the Strait of Hormuz and potentially accelerating the reduction of the American military footprint in the Middle East. Pakistan mediated the ceasefire and continues hosting negotiations.

headlines.headline: Iran Will Never Cede Control of Strait of Hormuz, Senior Politician Tells BBC
headlines.summary: Iranian lawmaker Ebrahim Azizi told the BBC that Tehran will 'never' give up control of the Strait of Hormuz, calling it an 'inalienable right.' He said Iran is introducing a bill to enshrine its control over the strategic waterway in law, viewing it as a permanent asset rather than just a bargaining chip in negotiations.

headlines.headline: Zelensky Condemns US Extension of Russian Sanctions Waiver Amid Energy Crisis
headlines.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky condemned the US decision to extend a waiver allowing Russia to sell oil already loaded on vessels until May 16. Zelensky said 'every dollar paid for Russian oil is money for the war,' warning that Russia's shadow fleet of 110 tankers would bring $10 billion to Moscow's coffers.

headlines.headline: US Businesses Can Begin Claiming Tariff Refunds Monday After Supreme Court Ruling
headlines.summary: A refund system for businesses that paid tariffs the Supreme Court ruled unconstitutional is set to launch Monday. Over 330,000 importers paid about $166 billion on 53 million shipments, with 56,497 importers eligible for $127 billion in refunds including interest. The process is expected to take 60-90 days per approved claim.

headlines.headline: Asia's Energy Pain Far From Over as LNG Shock Hits World's Factory
headlines.summary: Asian economies are grappling with tightened LNG supply, mounting foreign exchange pressure and damage already locked into Q2 performance as the Iran war disrupts 20% of global oil and LNG flows. The Philippines' GDP growth was downgraded to 3.7% from projected 5-6%, with the IMF warning of 'permanent scarring' across the region.

headlines.headline: India Faces Fertiliser Crisis as Iran War Disrupts Gulf Supplies
headlines.summary: India's urea imports have surged to $935 per tonne from $508 pre-war, with ammonia facilities in Qatar and Saudi Arabia shut after Iranian strikes. Domestic urea production has fallen from 2.5 million tonnes monthly to just 1.5 million tonnes due to LNG supply disruptions, threatening the kharif crop season.

headlines.headline: Australia's Big Banks Brace for Oil Shock Fallout as Hormuz Remains Blocked
headlines.summary: Australia's major lenders Westpac and National Australia Bank are increasing bad loan provisions as the prolonged Hormuz closure begins impacting the economy. The government has announced a $1 billion emergency loan program administered through the big four banks to help businesses weather the fuel crisis.

headlines.headline: North Korea Tests Cluster Warhead Missiles With Kim Jong Un Observing
headlines.summary: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un supervised test-launches of the upgraded Hwasongpho-11 Ra tactical ballistic missile with cluster bomb and fragmentation mine warheads. Five missiles struck a 12.5-13 hectare target area with 'very high density,' demonstrating the regime's continued weapons development amid global distractions.

headlines.headline: Spain Urges EU to End Association Agreement With Israel Over International Law Violations
headlines.summary: Spain announced it will ask the European Union to end its association agreement with Israel over alleged violations of international law. The move marks a significant escalation in European diplomatic pressure on Israel amid the broader Middle East conflict.

headlines.headline: Japan's Taiwan Strait Warship Transit on Treaty Anniversary Raises New Flashpoint With China
headlines.summary: A Japanese warship's transit of the Taiwan Strait on the anniversary of a key security treaty drew sharp criticism from Beijing, which said the passage 'harmed the feelings of the Chinese people.' Tensions in East Asia reached a fresh peak amid competing maritime claims.

headlines.headline: Japan Self-Defense Forces to Join Philippine-US Military Drills in South China Sea
headlines.summary: Japan's Self-Defense Forces will take part in annual military drills led by the Philippines and the US, testing Tokyo's commitment to security in the South China Sea. The exercises come as Japan expands its regional defense partnerships amid growing tensions with China.

headlines.headline: Bahrain Reviews Citizenship Laws After Foiling Iran-Linked Cells
headlines.summary: Bahrain's King Hamad announced measures against those who 'betrayed' the country after authorities foiled Iran-linked cells. The Gulf state is reviewing citizenship laws, potentially stripping nationality from dissidents, amid heightened regional tensions with Iran.

headlines.headline: Two US Embassy Officials Die in Car Crash in Mexico After Drug Operation
headlines.summary: Two US embassy officials died in a car crash in Chihuahua alongside two Mexican agents following a drug operation. The officials were trainers working with Mexican counterparts as part of ongoing bilateral security cooperation efforts.

headlines.headline: US Navy Seizes Iranian Cargo Ship Near Strait of Hormuz, Tehran Vows Response
headlines.summary: The US Navy fired on and seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship near the Strait of Hormuz after a six-hour standoff, with Iran's military calling it 'piracy' and vowing a swift response. The incident throws the fragile ceasefire into question as US negotiators head to Pakistan for talks.

headlines.headline: CBS Reports on Operation to Remove Iran's Highly Enriched Uranium Stockpile
headlines.summary: A 60 Minutes report examines the challenges of securing Iran's estimated 1,000 pounds of 60%-enriched uranium stored deep underground at Isfahan. Experts say the operation would require thousands of US troops and weeks on the ground, with any deal needing Iran's cooperation.

headlines.headline: Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz Again, Says Traffic Control Until War Ends
headlines.summary: Iran has again closed the Strait of Hormuz, reversing a brief reopening and saying it will maintain 'supervision and control' over traffic until the war definitively ends. Iran's chief negotiator stated: 'It is impossible for others to pass through the Strait of Hormuz while we cannot.'

headlines.headline: Belarus Leader Says Meeting With Trump Possible Once 'Big Deal' Is Ready
headlines.summary: Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko said he would be ready to meet US President Donald Trump once a 'big deal' between the two countries has been prepared. The statement signals potential shifts in Belarus-West relations amid the ongoing geopolitical realignment.

headlines.headline: IMF and World Bank Meetings Show Limits in Mitigating Shocks, Reliance on US
headlines.summary: The IMF spring meetings revealed growing global anxiety about a 13 million barrel per day oil shortfall, but US officials treated the energy crisis as a sideshow. The disconnect highlights how counting on US leadership to resolve crises is 'no longer the guarantee it had long been.'

headlines.headline: Iran Replenishing Missile Launchers Faster Than Before War, Guards Commander Says
headlines.summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guards Aerospace Force commander said the country now updates and replenishes its missile and drone launchers at a higher speed than before the war with the US and Israel. The statement signals Iran's continued military capacity despite weeks of strikes.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo Decries Escalation in Ukraine War, Calls for Weapons to Fall Silent
headlines.summary: Pope Leo on Sunday decried the intensification of the war in Ukraine, calling 'for the weapons to fall silent and for the path of dialogue to be followed.' The appeal comes amid Russia's recent mass missile attacks that killed at least 17 civilians.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Pushes Europe to Build Ballistic Missile Defense System
headlines.summary: President Zelenskiy said Europe must have its own defense system against ballistic weapons, with Ukraine already holding talks with several countries on its creation. The push comes as ballistic missile defense remains one of Ukraine's biggest challenges in the war with Russia.

headlines.headline: Pro-Russian Former President Set for Landslide Win in Bulgaria Election
headlines.summary: Pro-Russian former President Rumen Radev is set for a runaway victory in Bulgaria's election and may even secure a parliamentary majority, exit polls showed. The result could end years of weak coalition governments and alter the EU member's foreign policy orientation.

headlines.headline: France and Greece to Renew Defense Pact for Five Years Amid Iran War
headlines.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron will travel to Greece next week to renew a security pact between the two countries for another five years, a Greek government official said. The renewal comes against the backdrop of the war in Iran and shifting European security dynamics.

headlines.headline: Israeli Soldier Filmed Smashing Jesus Statue in Lebanon Sparks Outrage
headlines.summary: An image showing an Israeli soldier using a jackhammer to smash a statue of Jesus in southern Lebanon went viral, sparking outrage including among US conservatives. The incident in the Maronite Christian village of Debel drew condemnation from figures like Marjorie Taylor Greene and Matt Gaetz, potentially damaging Israel's support among evangelical Christians.

headlines.headline: Iran Vows 'Swift Response' After US Seizes Iranian Cargo Ship Near Hormuz
headlines.summary: Iran's military vowed retaliation after US Marines seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship near the Strait of Hormuz. Trump said the Navy 'blew a hole in the engineroom' of the vessel Touska. Meanwhile, Brent crude surged 7% to $96.85 as markets reacted to renewed Hormuz uncertainty.

headlines.headline: Chinese Humanoid Robot Beats Human Record in Half-Marathon by 6 Minutes
headlines.summary: A humanoid robot named Lightning, developed by Chinese company Honor, completed a half-marathon in Beijing in 50 minutes 26 seconds—beating the human world record by over 6 minutes. The race attracted 105 robot competitors, up from 21 last year, showcasing China's rapid advances in humanoid robotics and autonomous navigation.

headlines.headline: Iranian National Charged in US With Trafficking Arms to Sudan for Tehran
headlines.summary: US federal prosecutors charged Iranian national Shamim Mafi with brokering the sale of drones, bombs, and millions of rounds of ammunition manufactured by Iran and sold to Sudan. Mafi, a lawful US resident since 2016, was arrested at Los Angeles International Airport and faces up to 20 years in prison if convicted.

headlines.headline: Ukraine's Long-Range Strikes Inflicted $2.3 Billion in Russian Oil Losses in March
headlines.summary: Ukrainian estimates show long-range strikes on Russian oil infrastructure caused at least $2.3 billion in lost oil revenue for Russia in March alone, Zelensky said. The attacks disabled about 40% of Russia's oil export capacity as Ukraine targets the Kremlin's key revenue source.

headlines.headline: Gulf Equities Mixed as Hormuz Uncertainty Caps Ceasefire Optimism
headlines.summary: Gulf equities ended mixed on Sunday after President Trump said Iran had committed a serious violation of the ceasefire, renewing uncertainty over the Strait of Hormuz and the state of US-Iran talks despite his insistence that a peace deal would happen. Markets remain cautious amid conflicting signals.

headlines.headline: Dollar Rebounds as Middle East Tensions Reignite, Hormuz Closed
headlines.summary: The dollar headed for its highest level in a week at the start of Asian trading as renewed tensions in the Middle East sent investors scurrying for safe havens. Iran's reversal on reopening the Strait of Hormuz sparked fresh risk aversion.

headlines.headline: Oil Jumps, Stock Futures Slip as Iran Tensions Unsettle Markets
headlines.summary: Oil prices jumped, the US dollar rose and stock futures fell as investors dealt with conflicting messages about the Iran war and news that the Strait of Hormuz was closed again. The renewed uncertainty overshadowed ceasefire hopes.

headlines.headline: Kim Jong Un Oversees Test of New Cluster Warhead Missile as North Korea Accelerates Weapons Program
headlines.summary: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un oversaw test launches of upgraded short-range ballistic missiles including the Hwasong-11 Ra equipped with a cluster warhead, state media KCNA reported. The test demonstrates Pyongyang's continued weapons development despite international pressure.

headlines.headline: IMF Cuts 2026 Global Growth Outlook, Warns of Recession Risk From Iran War
headlines.summary: The International Monetary Fund has trimmed its global growth forecast for 2026, warning that a prolonged Iran war could significantly worsen the outlook if energy prices surge further. The revision reflects growing economic uncertainty from the Middle East conflict.

headlines.headline: Michigan Attorney General Rejects Trump Administration Ballot Request in Election Push
headlines.summary: Michigan's attorney general is rejecting an effort by the US Justice Department to obtain ballots and other voting materials, pushing back against what critics call a broader administration effort to challenge election administration.

headlines.headline: Trump Approval Rating Hits Record Low in NBC News Poll as Iran War Drags On
headlines.summary: A new NBC News poll shows the war in Iran is dragging down President Trump's approval rating, with just 37% of American adults approving of his performance. The poll reflects growing public concern about the prolonged conflict.

headlines.headline: Most Voters Blame Trump for Rising Gas Prices, Quinnipiac Poll Shows
headlines.summary: Most registered voters blame President Trump for rising gas prices, according to a new Quinnipiac University national poll. The survey highlights the political cost of the Iran war's impact on energy prices ahead of the midterm elections.

headlines.headline: Oil Prices Rebound 7% as Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz Again
headlines.summary: Oil prices rebounded more than 7% on Monday after tumbling more than 9% on Friday as Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz again, reversing a brief reopening. Brent crude futures jumped $6.56 to $96.94 a barrel as the vital waterway remains blocked.

headlines.headline: EV Sales Soar in Europe as Iran War Drives Petrol Prices to Record Highs
headlines.summary: Sales of fully electric cars in Europe's main auto markets jumped by almost a third in the first quarter of 2026, as drivers sought alternatives to combustion engines after the Iran war caused the highest spike in petrol prices in years.

headlines.headline: Bangladesh Raises Fuel Prices 10-15% as Iran War Drives Up Global Costs
headlines.summary: Bangladesh raised retail fuel prices by 10% to 15%, citing a sharp surge in global crude oil prices and tightening supplies caused by the ongoing Middle East conflict. The energy ministry announced the increases late Saturday as the Iran war continues to ripple through global energy markets.

headlines.headline: Two Foreigners Arrested in Iran for Importing Starlink Technology
headlines.summary: Iranian authorities arrested four individuals, including two foreign nationals, in the country's northwest on charges of being part of a 'U.S.-Israel-linked espionage network' for importing Starlink satellite internet technology. The arrests highlight Iran's continued crackdown on unauthorized communications technology.

headlines.headline: US Energy Chief Warns Gas Prices Could Stay Above $3 Until Next Year
headlines.summary: The US Energy Secretary warned that gasoline prices could remain above $3 per gallon through next year, with the average price at $4.05 per gallon compared to $3.16 a year ago. The Iran war's disruption of global oil supplies continues to pressure American consumers.

headlines.headline: US Officials Attend IMF Meetings in 'Parallel Reality' as World Grapples With Oil Crisis
headlines.summary: IMF spring meetings buzzed with talk of a 13 million barrel per day oil shortfall reshaping economies and defense alliances, but US officials treated the energy crisis as a sideshow. The disconnect highlights growing tensions between American policy and global economic concerns.

headlines.headline: Investors Return to 'TINA' Trades as US-Iran Ceasefire Revives Stock Market Hopes
headlines.summary: The US-Iran ceasefire has revived 'TINA' (There Is No Alternative) stock market trades, driven by peace hopes, soaring US earnings growth, and the relative insulation of the American economy from energy shocks. Investors are piling into US stocks as recession fears ease.

headlines.headline: DHS Shutdown Raises Security Concerns for FIFA World Cup 50 Days Away
headlines.summary: With the FIFA World Cup just over 50 days away, questions are mounting about whether the Department of Homeland Security shutdown will affect security preparations for the tournament. Former DHS officials express concerns about readiness amid political uncertainty.

headlines.headline: India Brings 500 MW Sodium-Cooled Nuclear Reactor Online Using Thorium
headlines.summary: India has brought a 500 MW sodium-cooled reactor online at Kalpakkam, leveraging its abundant domestic thorium resources for energy security. The country plans to build 18 standard design reactors as part of a state-led nuclear program with full control over the value chain.

headlines.headline: Japan's Energy Diplomacy Runs Into China's Head Start in Central Asia, Latin America
headlines.summary: As Japan steps up energy diplomacy to secure alternatives to Middle Eastern oil and gas, Tokyo is finding that China has already established a significant lead in Central Asia and Latin America. The competition highlights shifting energy geopolitics as nations seek to diversify away from the volatile Middle East.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo Voices Solidarity With Ukraine as Civilian Attacks Escalate
headlines.summary: Pope Leo XIV expressed solidarity with Ukraine amid escalating attacks against civilians, saying he was 'deeply saddened' by the recent escalation. The Pope renewed his appeal for 'weapons to fall silent' after Russia launched a mass missile attack that killed at least 17 civilians.

headlines.headline: Iranian Official Says Over 20 Passenger Planes Damaged in US-Israeli Attacks
headlines.summary: An Iranian aviation official said more than 20 passenger planes were damaged in US-Israeli attacks, though reports of wider damage were inaccurate as most airport capacity remains intact. The statement comes amid ongoing conflict that has disrupted regional aviation.
